En: I dream about you all the time.
Zh: “我总是会梦见你。”

En: Measurehead's amazing!
Zh: “测颅先生太棒了！”

En: Just look at him, the wonderful hunk.
Zh: “看看他，多么的强壮。”

En: No one will get past you!
Zh: “没人过得了你这关。”

En: Oh, Measurehead!
Zh: “哦，测颅先生！”

En: Not soft and weak like other men.
Zh: “跟其他柔弱的男人都不一样。”

En: Measurehead is *law*.
Zh: “测颅先生就是*法律*。”

En: Everyone should try to be more like Measurehead.
Zh: “每个人都该向测颅先生学习。”

En: Looking monolithic.
Zh: “看起来很庞大。”

En: Measurehead crushes all.
Zh: “测颅先生能碾压一切。”

En: You're the right stuff, Measurehead.
Zh: “你就是那个对的人，测颅先生。”

En: No man matches Measurehead.
Zh: “没有人能跟测颅先生相匹敌。”

En: Nobody comes close to Measurehead.
Zh: “没人能近得了测颅先生的身。”

En: The future belongs to Measurehead.
Zh: “未来属于测颅先生。”

En: Measurehead makes all men quiver.
Zh: “测颅先生能让所有男人瑟瑟发抖。”

En: More than enough to please a woman.
Zh: “取悦女人绰绰有余。”

En: Measurehead is a powerful man.
Zh: “测颅先生是一个强大的男人。”

En: I've come to Martinaise investigating a murder.
Zh: “我来马丁内斯是为了调查一起凶杀案。”

En: Murder, huh? That sounds like a lot of hard work. You'd never see me investigating a murder.
Zh: “凶杀案？听起来挺麻烦的。我就永远都不会去调查凶杀案。”

En: It's actually very fun and easy.
Zh: “其实很好玩，也很简单。”

En: You're right. It's almost impossible. I'm 91% sure I'll never get it solved.
Zh: “你说得不错，破案几乎是不可能的。我91%能确信这个案子永远也破不了。”

En: (Whisper) "I have no idea what I'm *doing*."
Zh: （低语）“我也不知道自己到底在*干嘛*。”

En: Maybe you want to help me solve it by telling me who did it?
Zh: “没准你能帮得上忙？告诉我凶手是谁。”

En: Of course, *policía*. He gives you a sly smile. "It wasn't me. You can rule me out. Easier that way."
Zh: “没问题，*警官*。”他会心地一笑，“不是我。你可以把我排除掉，这样更简单一些。”

En: He didn't do it. It's the truth.
Zh: 不是他干的。这是事实。

En: Should we? He's *nice*. I don't like *nice*.
Zh: 我们应该这样吗？他人很*好*。我不喜欢*好人*。

En: I don't believe you. Poste L'Aventurier said the same thing. They tried to get me to be their postman. So fun, so easy, they said. It's just walking. He shakes his head at the memory.
Zh: “我不信。冒险家邮局说过一样的话，他们只想骗我做他们的邮递员。他们说，很好玩，很简单的，只是走走路而已。”他一边回想一边摇了摇头。

En: It wasn't. I'm doing much better here at the harbour. Being an honest Union man with *a lot* of free time on his hands.
Zh: “然而事实并非如此。我在港口这儿要比当邮差强太多了，作为一名诚实的工会成员，我手上有*大把*的自由时间。”

En: Probably not. He nods. "I wouldn't even know where to start with that. Much better here at the harbour -- for an honest Union man like me."
Zh: “很有可能会没结果。”他点点头。“要我的话甚至都不知道该从哪里入手。还是在港口工作好——对于我这么个诚实的工会成员来说。”

En: He whispers back: "That's okay. I have no idea what I'm doing either. I don't even know what day it is..." He thinks and then decides: "Don't tell me. It's a *better* day that way."
Zh: 他低声回复道：“没关系的，我也不知道自己成天在干什么。我甚至都不知道今天星期几……”他想了一下，然后开口道：“别真的告诉我，这样的话日子会变得*更美好*一些。”

En: Measurehead? I think I met him.
Zh: “测颅先生？我好像已经见过他了。”

En: Yeah, the two-and-a-half-metre tall Semenese supremacist up there. He points to the bridge above the gates. "Walk right past him..."
Zh: “就是那边那个身高两米半的萨马拉至上主义者。”他指着大门顶上的那座桥。“从他身边直接走过去……”

En: *Anything* else I should know about this task? This weasel person? When he'll be home?
Zh: “关于这个任务，还有*什么*的我需要知道的吗？这个黄鼠狼是什么人？他什么时候回家？”

En: All right, I may have *some* advice for you on how to deal with Jean-Luc there -- out of solidarity to the RCM. It's low on technology, however.
Zh: “好吧，要对付那个让·卢克的话，我确实可以给你点*建议*——这是为了向RCM表达诚意。不过技术含量挺低就是了。”

En: Hmm... maybe that *would* work? We'll have to see.
Zh: 嗯……也许*能管用*？我们拭目以待吧。

En: Yes?
Zh: “你说？”

En: First -- don't fight him. Obviously. Second, get him to share his theory by being *subordinate*. Admit your lack of expertise. Basically grovel. That's how I'd do it, he tips his beret and concludes: "You're welcome."
Zh: “首先——别跟他打，没什么好解释的。第二，展现出*顺从*的姿态让他分享自己的理论，承认自己没有发言权。基本上就是要保持卑躬屈膝，要是我就会这么做，”他整了整贝雷帽，然后总结道，“不用谢。”

En: You've been in there, he means -- talked to the boss man too probably...
Zh: 他的意思是，你去过那里——还很可能跟老大聊过……

En: Labour utopia. (Nod approvingly.)
Zh: “劳工的乌托邦。”（赞许地点点头。）

En: Understood. The lieutenant takes a note. "This has been of limited use -- still, thank you."
Zh: “明白。”警督记下笔记。“虽然用处有限——不过还是谢谢你。”

En: He means -- a more violent faction could easily take care of such a thing.
Zh: 他的意思是——换作是一个更暴力的帮派的话，三两下就能摆平这种事情。

En: No problem. I wish the best to you in your search. Sure am glad it's not *my* search... He takes a sip from his flask.
Zh: “没问题。祝你们搜寻工作一切顺利。当然了，很高兴用不着*我*去搜查……”他从酒瓶里喝了一口。

En: Thank you -- this has been of *limited* use.
Zh: “谢谢——这个*没啥用*。”

En: Of course. I got you -- you don't know anything. He taps the side of his nose with a little wink.
Zh: “当然。我明白的——你什么也不知道。”他拿手指轻轻点了下鼻侧，对你挤了个眼。

En: Got it.
Zh: “了解。”

En: I'm more of a philosophical dockworker, see -- I like to talk about the big picture stuff. Who I am. Who you are. What we are fighting for... The man takes a big sip from his flask.
Zh: “我更接近于一个哲学派的码头工人。我喜欢讨论宏大的命题，我是谁，你是谁，我们为何而战……”男人从酒瓶里喝了一大口。

En: However much you feed the wolf...
Zh: “不管给狼喂多少肉……”

En: ... the wolf always wants more. I like wolves.
Zh: “……狼总是还想要更多。我喜欢狼。”

En: Cool. I don't have anything to add to that.
Zh: “酷。我没什么要补充的。”

En: A *none-of-the-above* kind of man? No harm in being that.
Zh: “你是*以上都不是*那一卦的吗？无伤大雅。”

En: You mean corruption?
Zh: “你是说腐败？”

En: By that you mean corruption?
Zh: “那你的意思是说腐败？”

En: ... the old man is corrupt for our *benefit* and we know it. Appreciate it, even. He is, personally, not too lavish.
Zh: “……老头子之所以腐败是为了我们的*利益*，大家都知道这一点，甚至还挺感激的。就他个人来说，他并不个很铺张的人。”

En: That desk seemed lavish to me.
Zh: “那张桌子在我看来就挺铺张的。”

En: You're right. He's very ascetic.
Zh: “你说得对，他是个清心寡欲的人。”

En: Okay, he's *reasonably* lavish, sure. That's his prerogative. It's not like you want a saintly demeanour on a corrupt motherfucker. That would be a manipulative illusion.
Zh: “好吧，他的确有*那么点*铺张，没错，但这是他的特权。这不像寄希望于一个腐败的猪头会做出圣人之举，后者纯粹是一种精心炮制出来的假象。”

En: Besides, there are no non-corrupt systems in the world anyway. Moralism least of all.
Zh: “再说了，这个世界上根本不存在没有腐败的系统，而*道德主义*离这个标准最为遥远。”

En: Besides, there are no non-corrupt systems in the world anyway. And *moralism* is the most corrupt of them all.
Zh: “再说了，这个世界上根本不存在没有腐败的系统，而*道德主义*恰恰是其中最腐败的一种。”

En: This man has political theory -- and it has not failed him today.
Zh: 这个男人有一套自己的政治理论——而且今天并没有让他失望。

En: Not you, you would like to say... But then there's that weasel door.
Zh: 你会想说，你没有……不过又想了想，还有黄鼠狼家那扇门。

En: This was great. You feel mentally reinvigorated.
Zh: 太棒了，你感觉自己精神焕发。

En: Be honest with him.
Zh: 对他坦诚相告。

En: I've tried that. They don't like what I came up with.
Zh: “我已经试过了，没人喜欢我想到的名字。”

En: Too bad. Can't live in this world without a good name, friend. Important things, they are. Vessels of the soul.
Zh: “可惜啊。人生在世怎么能够没个好名字呢，我的朋友。名字很重要，是灵魂的容器。”

En: See how we're all busy concentrating on the racist mugs? That's what the ruling class wants.
Zh: “看见我们是怎么把注意力浪费在这些种族主义马克杯上的吗？这就是统治阶级想要的。”

En: Yeah, it would be really bad if we got into an argument about it. Waste of a good noon. Or afternoon? Or night? He looks around, oblivious suddenly. "Anyway, thanks for helping out, friend."
Zh: “是啊，要是我们为此争吵起来的话可不好，那样就白白浪费了如此美好的一个中午，还是午后来着？还是晚上？”他四下看了看，“不管怎么说，感谢你的帮助，朋友。”

En: I don't know about that. Anyway, like I said before, bossman said that weasel's not a friendly *boia*. He touches his beret. "So thanks for helping out."
Zh: “那我就不知道了，反正老大说那个黄鼠狼不是个好说话的*仔*。”他摸了摸自己的贝雷帽。“所以，谢谢你的帮忙。”

En: You're *Tequila Sunset*. You have always been Tequila Sunset. It has always been too late.
Zh: 你是*龙舌兰日落*。你一直都是龙舌兰日落。日头总是太迟。

En: A really cool guy.
Zh: “所谓牛仔就是酷炫的仔。”

En: He says it matter-of-factly. Like it's no big deal.
Zh: 他就像是在陈述事实，特别轻描淡写。

En: No. It will prove useful still, believe your thieving fingers.
Zh: 不。这应该还是有用的，相信你天生做贼的手指吧。

En: He doesn't have the stamina to deliver mail on these tough streets. But *you* do. Maybe if this cop thing doesn't work out...
Zh: 这块街区太过于凶险，他没有足够的体力在这里送信。但是*你*有。如果你做不来警察这份工作，也许……

En: Feels like he's hinting at something, but you're too hungover (or dumb) to understand right now.
Zh: 感觉他在暗示什么，不过你的宿醉（或者是愚蠢）太过严重，现在还领会不到。

En: None of this mess we're in. This jivin' and jugglin'... What's it for? He doesn't wait for your answer. "To feed our children I guess."
Zh: “不是眼下的这些麻烦事。这些误导啊，小花招啊……这有什么用呢？”他没有等待你的回应。“我猜，是为了养育我们的孩子吧。”

En: He means he's not gonna tell you, 'cause he doesn't know. But he will shoot his mouth off with you now that you're working for Evrart.
Zh: 他的意思是他不会告诉你，因为他不知道。不过既然你现在为艾弗拉特做事了，他会对你知无不言的。

En: Who *he* is and what they're fighting for? This is interesting.
Zh: *他*是谁，他们在为什么而抗争？这很有意思。

En: Ask him about the Hardie boys.
Zh: 问问他哈迪兄弟的事。

En: Yes, I'm already on it.
Zh: “是的，我已经在办了。”

En: Yes. He nods. "You said as much."
Zh: “是的。”他点点头。“你已经说过了。”

En: He can't know that. Maybe you're just younger on the photo.
Zh: 他不可能知道的。也许您只是在相片上显得比较年轻。

En: That's a good one. But I know you're not a dockworker. I even gave you that key to the weasel den, remember? He raises his flask. "Now how can I help you?"
Zh: “不错不错，不过我知道你不是码头工人，我之前还把黄鼠狼窝的钥匙给你了，记得吗？”他举起酒瓶。“现在，我有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Something changes in his disposition. A merciful smile in the corner of his eye.
Zh: 他的性情有些变化。他的眼角露出了一丝怜悯的微笑。

En: He's not lying -- about not doing it himself.
Zh: 他没有撒谎——在没有亲自去做这件事上。

En: Unless...
Zh: 除非……

En: Evrart says Titus Hardie and his boys killed him.
Zh: “艾弗拉特说凶手是提图斯·哈迪和他的兄弟们。”

En: That so? The man twirls his moustache around his finger. "I haven't heard anything about that. But if that's true... well, he was an enemy combatant."
Zh: “这样的吗？”男人把小胡子盘在自己的手指上。“这我倒是没听说过，不过如果是如此……反正对面是敌方的雇佣兵。”

En: He believes the Hardie boys could've done it.
Zh: 他相信凶手很有可能是哈迪兄弟。

En: He's a chatty guy. *Wants* to talk about the strike too. Just can't break the command structure and tell you now.
Zh: 他是个健谈的家伙，也*愿意*谈罢工的事，只是碍于权力架构不能现在就告诉你。

En: (Shake your head.) "It's better than begging, c'mon."
Zh: （摇摇头。）“拜托，这还是比乞讨要好些吧。”

En: Sure. The man whistles a jaunty tune, the wind rustling his whiskers.
Zh: “好说。”男人吹起一段欢快的口哨，风把他的胡须吹得沙沙作响。

En: I almost had a heart attack...
Zh: “我差点就心脏病发作了……”

En: I'm really sorry, friend. Frittte's nearby, get some *nosaphed*. He points to the boutique just right of him. "Good stuff. Helps against heart attacks too."
Zh: “我真的很抱歉，朋友。弗利多就在附近，去弄点*鼻通灵*吧。”他指向右边的精品店。“好东西，对心脏病也有效。”

En: (Attempt to establish contact.) "Listen, Mañana, there's something *important* I need to talk to you about..."\n
Zh: （尝试进行交流。）“对了，马列拉，我有件*重要的*事情要和你说……”

En: (Attempt to establish contact.) "Listen, brother, there's something *important* I need to talk to you about..."
Zh: （尝试进行交流。）“对了，兄弟，我有件*重要的*事情要和你说……”

En: What's that, boia? He tilts his head slightly.
Zh: “怎么了，牛仔？”他微微歪着脑袋问道。

En: Now's your chance! Remember, communists are notoriously skittish, so it's best to *insinuate* your way in...
Zh: 你的机会来了！记住了，康米主义者是众所周知地难搞，所以你最好用*暗示*的方法切入话题……

En: Hold on: This isn't a man who goes in for subterfuge. He prefers someone who'll look him in the eye and say exactly what's on his mind.
Zh: 等一下，这个人不是那种喜欢耍花样的家伙。他更喜欢那种能直视他的双眼、说出自己心中所想的人。

En: Okay, maybe it's time to abandon the subtle approach.
Zh: 好吧，或许应该放弃这种投机取巧的方式了。

En: It was a foolish approach to begin with.
Zh: 用这种方式打开话题很不明智。

En: I'm looking for some people who know how to really grind a sausage, if you know what I mean.
Zh: “我在找一个摆弄香肠的高手，你懂我的意思吧？”

En: It involves our mutual friend, Kras. (Put your hands next to your head and curl your fingers like deer antlers.)
Zh: “这和我们共同的好友卡拉斯有关。”（把你的双手放在头的两侧，弯曲手指模拟鹿角的样子。）

En: I'm investigating a peculiar smell, one with revolutionary implications.
Zh: “我在调查一种独特的气味，一种蕴含革命意义的气味。”

En: I'm looking for a secret cabal of *mole people*.
Zh: “我在寻找一个*鼹鼠人*秘密阴谋集团。”

En: I believe there's a cell of radical communists operating in Martinaise. Can you help me find them?
Zh: “有一帮激进的康米主义者在马丁内斯活动。你能帮我找到他们吗？”

En: What I'm trying to say is: I'm looking for communists. You know any?
Zh: “我的意思是，我正在寻找康米主义者。你认识他们吗？”

En: Hrmmm. Have you tried a butcher shop? He looks at you with perfect equanimity.
Zh: “唔，你去肉铺找过了没？”他非常镇定地看着你。

En: Don't think I've ever met a Kras, personally. Then again, I've never been to Graad. He narrows one eye at you.
Zh: “你别自以为是地觉得我认识什么卡拉斯。再说了，我连格拉德都没去过。”他眯起眼睛看着你。

En: Could be coming from one of the jammed lorries? Cargo's been sitting out for a while. He sniffs the air and looks toward the roundabout.
Zh: “可能是从那些困住了的货車上传来的？那些货物放那儿很久了。”他嗅了嗅空气中的气味，然后看向环岛。

En: Hrmm... you might have to look elsewhere for help with that. He scratches his chin.
Zh: “唔……你可能得去别的地方问问了。”他挠挠下巴。

En: Now that *is* an important subject, but also a sensitive one. Still, good of you to just come straight out with it. No beating about the bush... He smooths the twin bars of his moustache.
Zh: “这*确实*是件挺重要的事，但也挺敏感的。不过你能直截了当、开门见山地说出来还挺不错……”他捋了捋自己的两撮小胡子。

En: Ah, now I see. Can't say I'm surprised. You seem to take your communism seriously. Not a bad thing, mind you. It's good for a man to take time to think about the whole socio-political world picture.
Zh: “啊，我现在算是明白了。也没什么奇怪的。你好像把康米主义看得很重要。其实也不是坏事。一个人如果能花时间去思考整个社会政治界的事情也挺不错……”

En: Communistas, huh? Can't say that's where I thought you were heading with this. Not that I'm criticizing. It's good for a man to take his time and think about the whole socio-political world picture.
Zh: “康米主义者？我一开始就觉得你会说这个事情。我可不是在指责你啊。一个人如果能花时间去思考整个社会政治界的事情也挺不错……”

En: It's certainly been an interesting development to witness first-hand.
Zh: “能见证第一手情报的确挺有趣的。”

En: Yes, he certainly has a way of just coming out and saying things that normal people wouldn't.
Zh: “是的，他确实总能站出来说一些平常人不会说的话。”

En: Oh, he certainly likes to take his time with things, especially when politics are at issue.
Zh: “哦，他确实喜欢以自己的步调处理事务，特别是和政治相关的。”

En: So you've given up copping and now you're hunting communistas. Care to say why?
Zh: “那你是放弃了当警察，追捕起康米主义者了啊。能说说为什么吗？”

En: I'm not 'hunting' communists, I'm trying to get organised by them.
Zh: “我不是要‘追捕’他们，而是需要他们帮我找到组织。”

En: I'm on a sort of quest to develop my political potential and I need their help.
Zh: “我正准备培养自己的政治潜力，所以需要他们的帮助。”

En: Honestly, I had a weird dream and now I need to get to the bottom of it.
Zh: “说实话，我是因为做了一个奇怪的梦，想要查个究竟。”

En: Oh, I see... He gives you an exaggerated wink.
Zh: “哦，我明白了……”他朝你夸张地眨了眨眼。

En: A fellow plains-roamer, in search of greater understanding. A classic story... He nods approvingly.
Zh: “一位平原漫游者同胞，正在寻求更深层次的理解。多么经典的故事啊……”他赞同地点点头。

En: Chasing a dream, huh? I have to respect that, boia... He gives you a small salute.
Zh: “追逐梦想是吧？那我得尊重一下你，牛仔……”他向你敬了个礼。

En: I wish I could help. Unfortunately, I don't know many communistas.
Zh: “我也希望能帮上忙，不过很不巧，我认识的康米主义者不多。”

En: Maybe, maybe not. He shrugs. "Either way, I'm not institutional. A man's got to blaze his own trail, in my book..."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“可能是，可能不是。总之呢，我也不是制度主义的人。我觉得人应该走自己要走的路……”

En: Some are, some aren't. He shrugs. "It's a big institution, room for all kinds: communistas, Semenese supremacists, even an anti-institutional boiadeiro..."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“有些是，有些不是。在这个庞大的制度里有各种各样的人：康米主义者、塞美尼至上主义者，甚至是反抗制度的牛仔……”

En: Didn't realize you could smell communism. He frowns. "But then, I've never studied the subject..."
Zh: 他皱着眉。“没想到你还能闻到康米主义的味道。不过我从来没了解过……”

En: I know you said you're not really a communist, but aren't you actually a communist?
Zh: “我知道你说过自己不算康米主义者，但事实上呢？”

En: I thought you Union guys were all communists?
Zh: “我还以为你们工会的人都是康米主义者呢？”

En: Are you sure? You smell just like a communist.
Zh: “你确定吗？你身上有股康米主义者的气息。”

En: Blazing his own trail, being his own man... not exactly your typical communist spiel.
Zh: 走出自己的道路，活出自己的人生……这并不是康米主义者的典型言论。

En: There is warmth in his voice when he talks about the Union. Whatever his personal politics, this is his home.
Zh: 他说到工会的时候语气里充满热情。不管他的政见如何，这里就是他的家。

En: You can smell all sorts of things... fear, disgust, desire. Not communism, though.
Zh: 你可以闻到各种各样的味道……比如恐惧、嫌恶和欲望。但闻不到康米主义。

En: Okay, so maybe he's not exactly a communist, but there's definitely some communistic residue about him.
Zh: 好吧，也许他并不算是康米主义者，但肯定受到过康米主义的影响。

En: Alright, so if he isn't personally a communist, he's definitely hanging out with them.
Zh: 好吧，就算他不是康米主义者，也肯定和他们厮混过。

En: Maybe it's not taught in any of your so-called universities, but there's definitely something going on here...
Zh: 或许你们那所谓的大学里没有教过这个，但这里肯定有猫腻……

En: He snaps his fingers. "Ah, but you know, I did meet a genuine *ideólogo* a few months ago. Perhaps he is your guy?"
Zh: 他打了个响指。“啊，不过是这样，几个月前我遇到过一个真正的*思想家*。没准他就是你要找的人？”

En: What's an *ideólogo*?
Zh: “什么是*思想家*？”

En: Did he say his name was 'Steban,' by chance?
Zh: “他是不是碰巧名叫‘史蒂文’？”

En: How did you meet him?
Zh: “你是怎么遇见他的？”

En: Okay, how do I find this guy? (Proceed.)
Zh: “好吧，我要怎么找到这家伙？”（继续。）

En: I couldn't tell you. Once I declined his offer, we finished our cigarettes and he disappeared back into the night. Just before he melted into the shadows he turned to me and said...
Zh: “我也没法说。我拒绝了他的提议，等我们抽完烟之后，他就消失在夜色里了。在离开之前，他转身对我说……”

En: *Remember Dobreva and Abadanaiz*...
Zh: “*记住多布列娃和阿巴达纳伊兹……*”

En: Wow, this one's a deep cut. Julia Dobreva and Jean Abadanaiz, popularly known as the 'Revolutionary Lovers', served as Dual Commissars of Revolution on the Insulindian front during the Antecentennial Revolution. Their forbidden love, as well as their disappearance during the final collapse of the Revacholian commune only contributed to their mystique...
Zh: 哇，这就值得深剖一下了。茱莉娅·多布列娃和让·阿巴达纳伊兹是一对有名的“革命伴侣”，他们是世纪前大革命时期伊苏林迪前线的革命双政委。他们之间的禁忌之爱，以及在瑞瓦肖公社摧毁前便消失无踪，使得他们的故事更加具有神秘色彩……

En: Their remains were discovered on a beach in Ozonne in '35. Subsequent forensic analysis suggested suicide by poison.
Zh: 在35年的时候，欧佐纳尔的海滩发现了他们的遗体。后经法医检验表明，他们是服毒自杀的。

En: ... and then he was gone.
Zh: “……然后他就走了。”

En: Who are Dobreva and Abadanaiz?
Zh: “谁是多布列娃和阿巴达纳伊兹？”

En: 'The Revolutionary Lovers.'
Zh: “‘革命伴侣。’”

En: What do you think it means? (Conclude.)
Zh: “你觉得这是什么意思？”（总结。）

En: I don't know. Guess not everyone remembers. A tight, half-smile crosses his face.
Zh: 他的脸上浮现出生硬而勉强的微笑。“不知道。大概不是所有人都记得吧。”

En: So they were lovers, eh? Makes sense, he seemed like a romantic, in his way.
Zh: “所以他们是伴侣吧？那就说得通了，他看起来有种自己的浪漫。”

En: He's not the only one, it seems. The lieutenant doesn't even look up from his notebook.
Zh: “而且好像也不止他一个。”警督埋着头继续看着他的笔记本。

En: *Ahem*... The lieutenant clears his throat.
Zh: “*嗯哼*……”警督大声清了清喉咙。

En: Been wondering about that myself. He scratches his chin. "Some communista inside-talk, could be. Not meant for the wider public. They love that kind of thing. You'd have to ask someone who knows this ideólogo personally..."
Zh: “我也在思考这个问题。”他挠挠下巴。“可能是康米派的内部谈话吧，不是针对公众的。他们就喜欢这么做。你得去问问那些了解这种思想的人……”

En: Not to interfere in your personal errand, but it might have something to do with that *passphrase* Cindy mentioned earlier.
Zh: “我不想干涉你的个人事务，但这可能和辛迪之前提到过的*口令*有关。”

En: Of course, such "inside-talk" might also serve as a handy passphrase...
Zh: 当然了，这种“内部谈话”也挺适合当作口令……

En: I have to say, though: It sounds like you've found yourselves a proper hunt.\n
Zh: “不过我还是想说，你们好像找到了一个适合的猎物。”

En: I have to say, though: It sounds like you've found yourself a proper hunt.
Zh: “不过我还是想说，你好像找到了一个适合的猎物。”

En: That's really dumb. You should feel embarrassed even suggesting that.
Zh: “那可真是够蠢的。说出这种建议你都不觉得尴尬吗？”

En: Wait, what was that about again?
Zh: “等等，你刚刚说什么来着？”

En: Oh, right. Good thinking, Kim.
Zh: “哦，好的，你真的太有见地了，金。”

En: I was totally about to say that.
Zh: “我也正想这么说呢。”

En: She said that you would need some kind of *passphrase* to gain access to this so-called *mega secret* meeting. He snaps the elastic band around his notebook.
Zh: “她说你需要某条*口令*才能进入所谓的*高度机密*会议。”他把笔记本上的皮筋扣了起来。

En: The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点了点头。

En: Of course you were, detective. Apologies for interrupting. The lieutenant smiles to himself.
Zh: “当然了，警探。抱歉打扰了。”警督暗自笑道。

En: You're right. How foolish of me. Won't happen again, detective.
Zh: “你说得对，我怎么蠢成这样。以后不会了，警探。”

En: Just in time, the faithful partner arrives with the key that turns the lock. You two make a good pair...
Zh: “可靠的伙伴正好带着能解锁的钥匙来了。你俩可真是天生一对……”

En: No, you weren't. Otherwise you *would have* said it. In truth, it was quite beyond your associative powers.
Zh: 不，你没有。否则你*早就*说过了。其实这已经超出了你的联想能力。

En: Sound like you two may have been riding together too long. The strain of the open range, and so on...
Zh: “感觉你俩好像一直不太对付啊。在公共场合都这么剑拔弩张的……”

En: I salute you, boias, and wish you good fortune on this grand ideological quest. He tips his hat ever so slightly.
Zh: “向你致敬，牛仔，希望你在探寻伟大思想的道路上顺顺利利。”他轻轻抬了一下帽子。

En: I have to say, though: It sounds like you've found yourselves a proper hunt.\n
Zh: “不过我还是想说，你们好像找到了一个适合的猎物。”

En: I have to say, though: It sounds like you've found yourself a proper hunt.
Zh: “不过我还是想说，你好像找到了一个适合的猎物。”

En: Is that so? He raises an eyebrow. "Well, in that case, I salute you, boia, and wish you good fortune on this grand ideological quest."
Zh: “是吗？”他扬起眉毛。“好吧，既然这样的话，我向你致敬，牛仔，希望你在探寻伟大思想的道路上顺顺利利。”

En: That's alright. I think you may have just given me the last piece of the puzzle.
Zh: “没关系。你刚才可能已经把最后一块拼图给了我。”

En: You know, a guy with a theory. Someone who likes to pit his theory against other theories in deadly theory-combat.
Zh: “就是一个满肚子理论的人。而且他还喜欢用自己的理论去和别的理论殊死搏斗。”

En: Didn't say anything about his name. Wasn't the sort of encounter where names matter.
Zh: “完全没提起过他的名字。那种场合下名字也不怎么重要。”

En: It was late one night as I was leaving the harbour. He was waiting on the corner in a bright white jacket, classic Saramirizian style...
Zh: “有一天深夜，我正准备离开港口。他穿着一件经典的萨拉米尔泽风格白色夹克在街角等待着。”

En: He asked me for a light. We shared cigarettes. Then he asked if I ever thought about 'getting into some of the extra-physical branches of communism'.
Zh: “他找我借火，我们一起抽烟。然后他就问我想不想‘加入康米主义的分支机构’……”

En: See? All this political chit-chat is just an excuse to rip tar with strangers. Let's say we just skip right to the good part, then?
Zh: 瞧见没？这些政治方面的闲聊就是为了和陌生人聊天找个借口。我们不如直接跳到正题吧？

En: Sounds like he was trying to recruit you to the homo-sexual underground.
Zh: “感觉他像是准备招你加入地下同性恋组织。”

En: Hang on. What does that mean, *the extra-physical branches of communism*?
Zh: “继续。*康米主义的分支机构*是什么意思？”

En: What did you tell him?
Zh: “你都跟他说了什么？”

En: (Nod.) "An extremely cool way to establish contact. Wish I'd thought of that."
Zh: （点头。）“这种建立交流的方式真是太厉害了。我要是能想到就好了。”

En: The same thing I always tell people who try to press some claim on me: I said, 'Every boiadeiro rides alone'.
Zh: “对那些想跟我提要求的人，我都会这么说：‘牛仔都是独行侠。’”

En: I don't think so, boia. I took it to mean he was asking me to join some sort of underground cell...\n
Zh: “我觉得不是，牛仔。我觉得他是想让我加入某种地下组织……”

En: No idea. I took it to mean he was asking me to join some sort of underground cell...
Zh: “不清楚。我觉得他是想让我加入某种地下组织……”

En: A very old-school organising technique, the sort of thing communistas used to do before the Revolution.
Zh: “这是一种极其老派的组织方式。在大革命以前，康米主义者通常都是这么做的。”

En: He shrugs. "It was alright. A little like something out of a Vespertine spy novel, but I must respect the effort."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“其实还好。这有点像维斯珀的侦探小说，不过我尊重这份努力。”

En: Scab? asks a man with jolly eyes, tilting his head.
Zh: “工贼？”一个男人歪着头，快活地问道。

En: What exactly *is* a scab?
Zh: “工贼究竟*是*什么？”

En: A kind of a worm, content with mere survival. They come, they want to do *our job* for shittier pay, screwing over both themselves and us. Everybody loses.
Zh: “一种蛆，只要有基本的温饱就会满足。他们想要以更低的报酬来干*我们的活*，害人又害己。这样下去所有人都是输家。”

En: Personally, I'd rather beg than scab. If the gentlemen shouting on the street came *begging*, maybe they'd have gotten something.
Zh: “就我个人而言，我宁愿讨饭也不做工贼。如果现在正在底下大喊大叫的先生们真的转行去*讨饭*的话，他们的收入说不定还能有所改善。”

En: (Nod.) "Have you tried talking to them?"
Zh: （点头。）“你有尝试跟他们交流吗？”

En: We've explained the matters, but they don't listen. He shakes his head. "This lot would be reasonable and go home if the big guy wasn't riling them up all the time."
Zh: “我们已经解释过利弊了，但他们不听。”他摇摇头。“如果不是那个大块头一直煽动他们的话，他们早就回家去了，不会一直这么不讲道理。”

En: I'm not a scab. I'm a cop. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我不是工贼。我是警察。”（继续。）

En: Ah, I was just messing with you. His smile deepens his wrinkles even more. "No one's ever seen a cop scab."
Zh: “啊，我刚才是跟你开玩笑呢。”他的笑容越发灿烂，皱纹变得更深了。“从来就没有工贼警察这个工种。”

En: Oh. Good, good! What matters do you want to discuss with Evrart?
Zh: “哦，那太好啦！你想跟艾弗拉特讨论什么事情呢？”

En: I need to get past the gates and speak with your boss.
Zh: “我要进这扇门，跟你的老板聊聊。”

En: Sure, I've had the necessary free time. He spreads his arms wide, using the reach to show how much time he has. "Fortunately, there's always time."
Zh: “当然，我的空闲多得是。”他张开手臂，比划着自己到底有多少时间。“幸运的是，时间总是有的。”

En: The look in his brown eyes conjures up an understanding: for him, having command of his time is the most important thing.
Zh: 他棕色眼睛里的神情让你明白了一件事情：对于他来说，保持对自己时间的支配权才是最重要的事情。

En: It all comes together now, the way he speaks about scabs, his general attitude. He's a follower of a 500-year-old Franconigerian boiadeiro code -- itself an appropriation of Vespertine cool...
Zh: 他谈论起工贼的方式，他整个的生活态度，现在所有这些线索全都串在一起了。他遵循着拥有500年历史的弗兰考格斯牛仔之道——而所谓的牛仔之道本身又是对维斯珀酷炫风的一种文化挪用……

En: He might have information about a killing that took place behind the hostel.
Zh: “关于旅馆后院发生的那起凶杀案，他可能掌握了一些信息。”

En: Body still hangin' in the tree? He rubs his chin as if pondering his core beliefs. "Aye, that's a rough pickle... can't help you with that, sorry."
Zh: “尸体还挂在树上呢？”他摩挲着下巴，好像在苦思自己的核心信念。“那确实是个很棘手的问题……这件事我帮不不上忙，抱歉。”

En: There's been talk of suspicious activity at the harbour.
Zh: “有传言说港口有一些可疑的活动。”

En: Why are you striking?
Zh: “你们为什么要罢工？”

En: We're negotiating our share.
Zh: “我们要争取属于自己的股份。”

En: Your... share?
Zh: “你们的……股份？”

En: Wait, so not wages or pensions or...
Zh: “等等，所以你们为的不是工资，或者养老金，或者……”

En: How large a *share* would you like?
Zh: “你想要多少*股份*？”

En: This stuff -- they already covered.
Zh: “你说的这些——他们已经提供了。”

En: Aye. He seems pleased with himself.
Zh: “是的。”他好像对自己感到很满意。

En: All of it. However, right now we want all the harbour workers to be on the company's board, so they could take part in *the decision-making process*.
Zh: “全部。不过，目前我们想让所有码头工人都能加入公司董事会，这样就能参与*公司决策*。”

En: Shouldn't you be grateful then? It's a lot more than most people here have.
Zh: “既然如此难道你们不应该心存感激吗？你们拥有的待遇已经比这里大多数人都要好得多了。”

En: Never grateful. Were we ever grateful, we'd have nothing. You fight for every piece.
Zh: “永远不要心存感激。如果感激的话，我们就一无所有了。每一份利益都是通过斗争得来的。”

En: Are you a... communist?
Zh: “你是……康米主义者吗？”

En: No, he pauses to think for a moment. "I don't think I'm a communist. Seeing something of value and saying *I want it all to myself* is a much older and simpler notion. No science to it at all..."
Zh: “不，”他停下来想了一会儿。“我不自认为是康米主义者。相比之下，看到了什么值钱的东西然后说一句*我想要，没别人的份儿*更古老、更单纯，跟科学没有半点关系。”

En: Even a weak child can think it. The only things holding someone back are *I can't* and *I shouldn't*.
Zh: “这种反应连一个小毛孩都会有，唯一阻拦人付诸实践的只有心中的*我不能*和*我不应该*。”

En: But don't you think the owners have a right to the harbour? Didn't they organize it into existence?
Zh: “但是你不觉得企业主对港口拥有某种权利吗？港口不是因为他们的规划才得以出现的吗？”

En: You see, the owners already benefit greatly from the harbour. No one can doubt that. They get so much.
Zh: “你想，企业主们已经通过港口获得了巨大的利益，这一点毋庸置疑，他们已经得到太多了。”

En: *The board* is where the stockholders send their representatives. Often those representatives don't even represent the stockholders' interests. It's not actually like some council-based democracy.
Zh: *董事会*是股东委派代表的地方，那些代表一般来说根本无法代表股东的利益，因此董事会和委员会民主制并不是一回事。

En: You seem to have the wrong idea here. The board of a major company is not *council democracy*.
Zh: “你的想法好像错了。大公司的董事会并不是*委员会民主制*的。”

En: Quite a shitty board, I must say. Means someone's gotta reform it. I, for one, want to face the *fat ones'*, when decisions are made.
Zh: “说实话，这届董事会也忒差劲了。这就意味着必须有人改革它。当董事会作出决定的时候，我希望能作为其中的一员直面那些*肉食者*。”

En: Got the picture. Let me ask you something else.
Zh: “明白了。我再问你点其他问题。”

En: But the stuff you do aligns admirably well with the World Republic, why not call yourself a communist?
Zh: “但是你们在做的事情完全符合国际主义的理念啊，你为什么不认为自己是康米主义者呢？”

En: I have nothing against communists, they are honourable boiadeiros. He takes a swig from his flask. "And they have good analysis."
Zh: “我对康米主义者没有任何意见，他们是值得尊敬的牛仔。”他从酒瓶里喝了一大口。“而且他们很会分析事情。”

En: But my own code serves me well. If my code starts failing -- a code can fail a man as well as a man can fail a code -- then I will have to submit to a new one. Which may well be communism.
Zh: “但我按着目前的人生信条过得还挺好的。如果有一天我的人生信条出了问题——就像人有时会背叛自己人生信条，人生信条也会反过来背叛一个人——到时候我就只能换一个新的了，而我下一个人生信条很有可能就是康米主义。”

En: I guess I understand...
Zh: “我大概懂了……”

En: I am not sure I understand.
Zh: “我好像没太听懂。”

En: It sounds like you're a communist who thinks he isn't doing enough to call himself one.
Zh: “听起来你就是一个康米主义者，只不过觉得自己的成就还够不上这个称号。”

En: See, I am primarily a lazy person. He looks very amused, as if thinking about some private joke or mystery.
Zh: “你看，我本质上就是个懒人。”他乐了，就好像是想到了一个很私人的笑话或者是秘密。

En: The bossman, Evrart, what can you tell me about him?
Zh: “能跟我说说你们的老大，艾弗拉特吗？”

En: Hey, if the rich folk screw over poor folk, with little concern for their merits or futures, why shouldn't poor folk screw over rich folk from time to time?
Zh: “喂，如果有钱人会欺负穷人，而且一点都不关心他们的利益或未来，那为什么穷人不能偶尔也欺负一下富人呢？”

En: I think it's best you make up your own mind, now that you've met him. He shrugs. "In my eyes, he is a capable organizer and a decent businessman."
Zh: “既然你已经见过他了，我觉得你最好自己判断。”他耸耸肩。“在我看来，他是个有才干的组织者，也是个很正派的商人。”

En: By Heavens, why would he not be corrupt? We live in a harsh and disordered world, see. And in this world...
Zh: “老天爷啊，他怎么可能会真的两袖清风呢？瞧，我们生活在一个残酷又混乱的世界，而在这个世界里……”

En: He is *reasonably* lavish, sure. That's his prerogative. It's not like you want a saintly demeanour on a corrupt motherfucker. That would be a manipulative illusion.
Zh: “他是有点*那么点*铺张，确实，但他有那样的资格。并不是说你希望一个腐败的杂种会做出什么崇高的行为。那就是一种被人精心操纵的错觉。”

En: What does bossing the Union entail anyway?
Zh: “话说，统领工会需要什么？”

En: I guess you kinda get to be the village chief. He oversees the harbour, makes deals with the owners or other relevant parties. Watches out for his own.
Zh: “我猜有点像当村长之类的吧。他会监督这个港口的作业，跟企业主或其他相关方达成协议，照顾自己的手下。”

En: Wait... I don't even know your name. Who are you?
Zh: “等等……我好像还没问你的名字呢。怎么称呼？”

En: Call me Mañana. I'm just a humble harbour-worker... for the past six, seven years.
Zh: “叫我马列拉吧。我只是个卑微的港口工人……干了六七年了。”

En: Call Me Mañana.. What kind of a name is that?
Zh: “叫·我·马列拉……这算哪门子的名字啊？”

En: It means *call me tomorrow*.
Zh: “意思是*明天打给我*。”

En: So, not a given name, I take it?
Zh: “所以，我猜，这不是你的本名了？”

En: I'd say a real name is what most people call you. He realizes something.
Zh: “依我看，大多数人叫你什么，什么就是你的本名。”他悟到了什么。

En: So it's a... nom de guerre?
Zh: “所以这是个……假名？”

En: A nom de guerre would be more like *Guerra Mañana*. He chuckles. Then realizes something.
Zh: “如果是假名的话，就应该是*假·马列拉*。”他咯咯地笑了起来，然后又悟到了什么。

En: Hold on, have you had any other names?
Zh: “等等，那你还有其他名字吗？”

En: Now that we're getting so well acquainted -- what's your name?
Zh: “既然现在咱俩都已经这么熟了——你叫什么名字？”

En: It's... umm... Harry.
Zh: “我的名字是……呃……哈里。”

En: I hear you and it's... uh, pleasure meeting you, *Harry*. He nods. "But do keep an eye out for an alternative, alright?"
Zh: “收到，那么……呃，很高兴认识你，*哈里*。”他点点头。“不过记得留心一下有没有其他可替代的新名字，好吗？”

En: Honesty. I *lost* it while drinking.
Zh: “老实说，我的名字在我之前喝完酒之后就*找不到*了。”

En: Wow! This is major! You're like... a man born anew! His eyes light up. "Okay, so if you can't find it, maybe make up a new one?"
Zh: “哇哦！这下厉害了！你基本上就是……一个重获新生的男人！”他的眼睛亮了起来。“好吧，既然找不回来的话，也许可以再想个新的？”

En: I may take that into consideration.
Zh: “我会考虑一下的。”

En: I prefer to be called Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “我更喜欢别人叫我龙舌兰日落。”

En: I just found my name some time ago, on the coast. Finally! The name's Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “我不久前刚找回我的名字，就在海滩上，谢天谢地！我的名字叫龙舌兰日落。”

En: It's been said I call myself *Tequila Sunset*.
Zh: “据说我自称*龙舌兰日落*。”

En: The man is deeply moved. You see his eyes turn teary with appreciation. He is extremely happy for you.
Zh: 那个人被深深地触动了。你看见他的眼睛因为欣赏之情而饱含泪水。他正由衷地为你感到高兴。

En: He reaches his hand out to you and shouts: "Boiadeiro!"
Zh: 他朝你伸出手，然后大喊道：“牛仔！”

En: Shake his hand.
Zh: 跟他握手。

En: Leave him hanging.
Zh: 不理他。

En: Well, boiadeiro -- He pauses to think a moment on the situation. "I guess I don't really have a claim to your hand or anything."
Zh: “呃，牛仔——”他停下来想了一下眼下的状况，“我想我无权干涉你的手或别的任何东西的自由。”

En: You receive a good, strong handshake in return.
Zh: 你得到一个强而有力握手作为回应。

En: You said boiadeiro? Tell me about them.
Zh: “你说牛仔？跟我科普一下吧。”

En: What's a... *boiadeiro*?
Zh: “*牛仔*……是什么？”

En: Mañana sounds like a mesque word, but he doesn't actually look much like a mesque.
Zh: 马列拉听起来像是梅斯克的人名，但他实际上看起来并不像梅斯克人。

En: Mañana? You don't look like a Mesque...
Zh: “马列拉？你看起来不像梅斯克人啊……”

En: I'm not. I'm *Vacholiero*. Not all Mesques have the Mesque look, either, you know. Things in the real world are a bit more... fluid. Stuff ain't always what it appears to be.
Zh: “我确实不是梅斯克人。我是*瓦科莱*。不过要知道，不是所有梅斯克人都长着典型的梅斯克脸。现实世界要更……微妙一些，事情并不总是符合人们想当然的期望。”

En: It feels like there's an entire history behind the word... but it doesn't really matter. You got it. You know what it *means*.
Zh: 这句话背后感觉隐藏着他全部的往事……但这并不重要，你明白的。你知道这*意味着*什么。

En: He takes a swig from his flask, then offers you some.
Zh: 他从酒瓶里喝了一大口，然后递给你。

En: Oh yeah! (Take a swig.)
Zh: “来吧！”（喝上一口。）

En: Thanks, I'll pass. I'd just go wild.
Zh: “谢谢，我还是算了，会发酒疯的。”

En: And then we might go wild together, which sounds fun. But I guess I have a strike to watch.
Zh: “那我们可以一起疯，听起来怪有意思的，但是我还要盯着游行呢。”

En: Keeps you warm on a day like this. Or any day, for that matter.
Zh: “像今天这种气温，喝酒能暖身子。其实不管什么天气喝酒都能暖身子。”

En: What kind of a cool guy exactly? It's not *just* a cool guy, is it?
Zh: “你说的酷男到底是哪种？而且应该*不只*一个，对吧？”

En: Like me and you. Exactly so. He takes a swig from his flask, then offers you some.
Zh: “就像咱俩这样的，没错。”他从酒瓶里喝了一大口，然后也分给了你一点。

En: Have a moment of silence.
Zh: 享受片刻的沉默。

En: Does this mean you can let me through the gate?
Zh: “这是否意味着你能放我进去呢？”

En: The Measurehead thing didn't go well for me. Could you help me with that somehow?
Zh: “我在测颅先生那边进展得并不顺利，你能帮个忙吗？”

En: Sorry. Busy surveying the situation. He takes a swig from his flask. "I'm sure you'll figure something out. You guys have all sorts of gadgets these days."
Zh: “抱歉，我很忙，还得盯着这里的状况呢。”他从酒瓶里喝了一大口。“我敢肯定你一定会想到办法的，你们警察的门道可多着呢。”

En: Ola, wandering man. Mañana looks at you with soft and hazy eyes. "How can I help you now?"
Zh: “你好啊，闲人。”马列拉用柔和又朦胧的眼睛看着你。“有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Olá, wandering man. He looks at you with soft and hazy eyes. "How can I help you?"
Zh: “你好啊，闲人。”他用柔和又朦胧的眼睛看着你。“有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: It sounds scary, I don't want to.
Zh: “听起来怪吓人的，我还是算了吧。”

En: No doubt he's communist enough to call himself such. But he won't.
Zh: 毫无疑问，他完全有资格自称康米主义者，但他并没有。

En: He knows who he is. Firmly grounded -- has no need to reinforce or elaborate his political identity to himself or others.
Zh: 他知道自己是谁，没有一丝怀疑和动摇——无需再向自己或其他人强调或解释自己的政治认同。

En: Hold on, where did they all come from?
Zh: “等等，他们都是从哪里来的？”

En: Beats me. Somewhere in the ground, I think.
Zh: “难到我了。我想，大概是从地底的什么地方钻出来的吧。”

En: You don't seem to like them much.
Zh: “你看起来不是很喜欢他们。”

En: Gotta be bloody stupid or freakin' evil to scab. Or, I guess... scared, maybe. But scared of what, of who? He looks at the mass, squinting his eyes as if trying to ascertain what they're scared of.
Zh: “当工贼的人不是蠢得要死，就是坏得要命，要不然，我猜……也许是太害怕了。他们怕的是什么东西或者什么人吗？”他看着人群，眯起眼睛，像是想搞清楚他们到底在害怕什么。

En: Not a given name, for certain. It's a taken name -- the kind for artists, or criminals, or revolutionaries and the sort. An alias. A *nom de guerre*.
Zh: 这个名字肯定不是他爸妈起的，而是他给自己起的——就是艺术家，或者罪犯，或者革命分子之流会给自己取的那种名字。这是一个代号，一个*假名*。

En: Suit yourself. Yet one can't live nameless; names are important things. Vessels of the soul.
Zh: “你随意，不过人生在世总不能没有名字，名字太重要了，那可是灵魂的容器。”

En: Okay, I have a name for myself, it's...
Zh: “好吧，我自己也有个名字，我叫……”

En: Can't escape Costeau
Zh: 还是逃不脱库斯托

En: Heard story from Idiot Doom Spiral
Zh: 白痴·厄运·漩涡那里听来的故事

En: You're Harry
Zh: 你叫哈里

En: Something golden, a wild fire.
Zh: 那是金色的，就像野火。

En: Your mind can only conjure up Hunk Handy, Fucke Waldéz, Bosco... you might want to decide to keep quiet about these.
Zh: 你脑海里只能想到汉克·汉迪，富克·沃德斯，博斯科……不过你最好还是别说出来。

En: Uhhhh.... I actually don't have a name.
Zh: “呃……其实我没有名字。”

En: It can take time to come up with a good one, for sure.
Zh: “当然，想出个好名字是要花时间的。”

En: The encroaching doom is golden red. It is inside you, but also outside, on the horizon, by the water's edge. Innermost but yet to happen -- things make sense from that angle...
Zh: 迫近的末日是金红色的。它在你的身体里，也在外部世界，在视线尽头，天海一线之处。它在心底最深处，但尚未到来——从那个角度看事情就说得通了……

En: I can't do it. I'm sorry.
Zh: “我做不到。抱歉。”

En: You're *Tequila Sunset*. You have been Tequila Sunset for a long time now.
Zh: 你是*龙舌兰日落*。迄今为止你已经当了很久的龙舌兰日落了。

En: The name's... Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “我的名字是……龙舌兰日落。”

En: No. I don't *want* to be Tequila Sunset.
Zh: 不。我*不想*当龙舌兰日落。

En: Name-invention feels too dangerous.
Zh: “编名字感觉是件很危险的事。”

En: Yeah, he nods. "A name's the kind of shit that can kill a man. Mañana's a good name in that regard."
Zh: “是啊，”他点点头。“名字这种鬼东西可是能杀人的。从这方面来说，马列拉可是个好名字。”

En: What's your deal with names, anyhow? Is it a spiritual thing or a philosophical one or...?
Zh: “那你怎么理解名字？精神层面，还是哲学意义上的，还是……？”

En: More a poetic thing. Any attempt to formulate a grand unified theory about it ends in the stupidest shit imaginable. Doesn't make it less true, though. He nods sagely.
Zh: “更像是诗。任何建立大一统理论去解释名字的尝试到头来都沦为了你能想象到的最蠢的狗屎，但理论的不适用丝毫没有减少它的真实。”他点点头，就像一个贤者。

En: That means I can't *tell* you about it. I can only *show* you. You may understand in time... or not. But it might be easy enough.
Zh: “意思就是我没法*告诉*你，只能*展示*给你看。你也许迟早有一天会明白……也许永远都不会明白。不过事情其实挺简单的。”

En: Sounds stupid, is what I think.
Zh: “听起来很愚蠢，这就是我的想法。”

En: Yes, friend. The world is certainly stupid enough to have stupid features. He grins.
Zh: “是的，朋友。这个世界本身就很愚蠢，所以到处都能发现愚蠢。”他咧开嘴笑了。

En: The man sits on the railing, his hands reaching far and wide, yet it feels as if he could effortlessly go even wider, if need be. An endless torrent of time.
Zh: 男人坐在栏杆上，双臂张得特别开，不过感觉他的双臂还能毫不费力地继续这么伸展下去，之间象征着无尽的时光洪流。

En: Why not? I need to get through the gate to the harbour. You need to let me in!
Zh: “为什么不呢？我需要穿过大门进入港口。你必须放我进去！”

En: Good talking to you. Gotta run. [Leave.]
Zh: “跟你聊得很开心。我该走了。”[离开。]

En: Nice talk. Gotta get moving. [Leave.]
Zh: “跟你聊得很开心。我该走了。”[离开。]

En: Can't live in this world without names, see. Important things, they are. Vessels of the soul.
Zh: “人生在世总不能没有名字，名字太重要了，那可是灵魂的容器。”

En: I can't disclose any details of an on-going case.
Zh: “我不能透露调查中案件的任何细节。”

En: The fat ones, always leeching on the honest man.
Zh: “那些肉食者，总是吸老实人的血。”

En: And you're the ones to reform, thus reaping your own benefits?
Zh: “所以你们是为了自己的利益才呼吁改革是吗？”

En: At least you got the benefits, that's something.
Zh: “至少你们还有所收获，不是吗？”

En: It's not enough. Not enough to get ahead. More about keepin' us in our place.
Zh: “那还不够，还不够让事业继续前行，顶多只能让我们保住现在的位置。”

En: So, anything else you wanted to discuss, boiadeiro?
Zh: “所以，你还有什么要讨论的吗，牛仔？”

En: Nod knowingly.
Zh: 用力点点头。

En: Yeah! Let's get this party started, it's your chance to shine, boy!
Zh: 耶！让我们开始派对吧，你大放异彩的时刻到了，小伙子！

En: What's there not to want? It's got range, meaning, layers, colours, associations, the whole works!
Zh: 为什么不想要？它有范围，意义，层次，色彩，联想，是一个完整的作品！

En: The name's done! It's Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau.
Zh: “名字想好了！就叫拉斐尔·安布罗修斯·库斯托。”

En: Are you *quite* sure?
Zh: “你*真的*确定吗？”

En: Of course! It's such a *cool* name. Solemn, classy, historical... almost regal.
Zh: “那当然！这个名字*特别酷*。庄严，有格调，富有历史感……基本上是王者的名字。”

En: Well, better than the other names I can come up with at least.
Zh: “至少比我想到的其他名字要好。”

En: Hold on, man. Keep it slow and steady, don't succumb to fate. It can take time to come up with a really good one...
Zh: “等一下，兄弟。这种事情稳妥些比较好，急不来的。想出个真正的好名字是需要花时间的……”

En: You can't tell a Raphaël what to do.
Zh: “一个名为拉斐尔的人还轮不到你来呼三喝四。”

En: Fine, perhaps I'll try again... later.
Zh: “好吧，也许我可以再想想……之后再说吧。”

En: There's no rush to find yourself.
Zh: “寻找自我的时候不用这么着急。”

En: If you say so. He shrugs and looks about at the surroundings.
Zh: “随你吧。”他耸耸肩，环顾了一下四周。

En: Yeah... okay, I'm totally Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “哦……好吧，我完全就是龙舌兰日落嘛。”

En: I don't operate in that capacity. I'm not a *granter of passage*. He takes a swig and points up the stairs with his flask. "The passage grants itself."
Zh: “我没有这样的能力，我无法*授予通行权*。”他喝了一口酒，然后用酒瓶指着上方楼梯的方向。“通行权是天赋人权。”

En: Aye -- walk right past Measurehead and go in.
Zh: “是啊——直接从测颅先生身边走过就行。”

En: Surely a step in the right direction -- feel that smooth texture, savour that flavour. How it flows down your gullet, like a warm caress. *That's* the sunset. By the time you arrived in this world it was already late.
Zh: 确实是朝正确的方向又前进了一步——感受那光滑的口感，品味那种味道。感觉它是如何沿着你的喉咙顺流而下，就像温暖的爱抚。*那*就是日落。你来到这个世界的时候天色已晚。

En: I have no interest in drinking while on duty.
Zh: “我出勤时不喝酒。”

En: A man walking on the edge of a razor, eh? Leaves more for me! He laughs and takes another sip from the flask.
Zh: “一个过着刀口舔血的日子的男人，是吧？给我多留一点！”他笑了笑，又从瓶子里喝了一口。

En: The idea of alcoholic swill sloshing around in your bloodstream feels fairly unappealing.
Zh: 想象酒精随着血液在你周身流淌的画面还挺诡异的。

En: Who do you think killed the hanged man?
Zh: “你觉得是谁杀了吊着的人？”

En: Any idea who killed the hanged man?
Zh: “知道是谁杀了吊着的人吗？”

En: The mercenary, aye? He shrugs. "Who could have killed him, that's indeed the question. Why even do such a thing?" He shakes his head solemnly.
Zh: “那个雇佣兵对吧？”他耸耸肩。“到底是谁杀了他，这确实是个问题。为什么要杀他呢？”他严肃地摇摇头。

En: What's the matter with this wimp? Is he obstructing *justice*?!
Zh: 这个孬种怎么搞的？难道他想妨碍*正义*吗？！

En: The merc was hanged with a very specific type of a cargo belt, one often used in harbours. It had the word *VERMILLION* written on it.
Zh: “那个雇佣兵是被一种特定类型的货缆吊起来的，这种缆绳在港口很常见，现场的那条上面写着*弗米利恩*的字样。”

En: The merc was hanged with a very specific type of a cargo belt. One often used in heavy transport areas, for example, harbours.
Zh: “那个雇佣兵是被一种特定类型的货缆吊起来的，这种缆绳在重型运输的区域经常会被用到，比如说，港口。”

En: The harbour is a prime area of suspicion. In your opinion -- are the dockworkers involved in the killing?
Zh: “港口是怀疑的重点区域。在你看来——会不会有码头工人参与了谋杀呢？”

En: Stop being a wimp, you need to help me out right *now*!
Zh: “别怂了，你*现在*必须帮我！”

En: Let's change the topic.
Zh: “我们换个话题吧。”

En: No need to get aggressive. He takes a swig from his flask. "I'm really not the one to ask."
Zh: “没必要这么咄咄逼人。”他从酒瓶里喝了一口。“你真的不该来问我。”

En: What a thought! He scoffs, looking shocked. "Why would noble workers resort to such a thing. Unless they were *pushed*, of course."
Zh: “真是个大胆的想法呢！”他嗤笑着，看起来有些震惊。“为什么高尚的工人会做出这种事情呢。当然了，除非他们是*被迫的*。”

En: Your dead guy -- was an enemy combatant.
Zh: “你的那个死人——是一名敌方的战斗人员。”

En: Despite of what I just said, I'm actually a worker like you guys! (Show him the stolen ID card.)
Zh: “不管我刚才说了什么，我其实跟你们一样是个工人！”（把偷来的身份证件拿给他看。）

En: I'm actually a worker, like you guys! (Show him the stolen card.)
Zh: “我其实也是个工人，跟你们一样！”（把偷来的卡片拿给他看。）

En: The man looks at the colourful piece of plastic, then at you, then back at the card, then at you again.
Zh: 男人看了一眼那张彩色塑料片，然后看了看你，接着又看了一眼卡片，最后又看了着你。

En: Umm... He looks at his flask for a moment. "But that's not you, officer?"
Zh: “嗯……”他盯着自己的酒瓶看了一会儿。“但这个不是你吧，警官？”

En: It's an old picture.
Zh: “这张照片是好早以前拍的了。”

En: (Take a close look at the card.) "Guess not."
Zh: （仔细看了一眼卡片。）“应该不是。”

En: From back when you were not a cop, but a heavy-set dark-skinned dockworker named *Santiago John*?
Zh: “所以拍这张照片的时候你还不是警察，而是一个体格魁梧、深色皮肤的码头工人，名叫*圣迭戈·约翰*？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Good thing too. I've known Johannes for a few years now, be *sinister* if he turned into a completely different person overnight.
Zh: “好消息是，我都认识约翰好几年了，如果他一夜之间变成了另一个完全不同的人，那才是真的*见鬼*了呢。”

En: He leans in to inspect the card. "How did you get his shift card anyway?"
Zh: 他凑近查看那张卡片。“话说你是怎么弄到他的工卡的？”

En: Does not seem plausible, now that I think of it.
Zh: “现在想一下，好像是不太可能。”

En: I found it on the ground. At the Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: “我在飞旋旅馆的地上找到的。”

En: Highly unlikely. I've known John for several years now. Also, you *told* me you're a cop, remember? How did you get his shift card?
Zh: “绝对不可能。我都认识约翰好几年了。而且是你自己*告诉过*我说你是警察的，还记得吗？你是怎么弄到他的工卡的？”

En: Sure, I have no real need for it.
Zh: “行吧，反正留着也没什么用。”

En: No, I'm afraid. The card's evidence in an on-going investigation.
Zh: “恐怕不行，这张卡片是一起正在调查的案件的证物。”

En: Thank you plenty. I'll make sure John gets his card back.
Zh: “谢谢你。我会保证把卡片还给约翰的。”

En: Oh, is my friend in some sort of trouble? Who knew...
Zh: “哦，我的朋友是惹上什么麻烦了吗？谁知道……”

En: Yes, and you also *told* me you're a cop. But now that this is settled... He leans in to inspect the card. "How did you get his shift card?"
Zh: “是的，是你自己*告诉*我说你是警察的。不过既然问题已经解决了……”他凑近查看着那张卡片。“你是怎么弄到他的工卡的？”

En: Suspicious activity? How very troublesome... yet, take a look around. Strikers, protests, scabs -- isn't it all so very suspicious? He smiles widely.
Zh: “你说可疑活动？这下可真的麻烦了……不过看看你周围吧。罢工的，抗议的，反罢工的——这些活动哪个不可疑？”他笑的很灿烂。

En: So mysterious. So intriguing. The man gives out a jolly chortle. "Either way, the road in front of you is wide and open."
Zh: “这么神秘，如此诱人。”男人发出愉快的笑声。“不管怎么说，前方的道路已经向你敞开了。”

En: Plenty to go around. Don't get greedy and try to swipe mine, though. He smiles merrily. "You've got your own."
Zh: “绰绰有余，不过不要太贪，别把我的那份也独吞了，”他开心地笑了。“你有你自己的那份。”

En: All right then. (Conclude.)
Zh: “那好吧。”（总结。）

En: Well met, Mañana. That's a sweet-ass name.
Zh: “很高兴遇到你，马列拉。这是个好名字。”

En: A name worthy of the man who holds it, friend. Names are important.
Zh: “一个配得上它所有者的名字，我的朋友。名字是很重要的。”

En: Oh, okay. Economy stuff. I get it. I want to talk about something else.
Zh: “哦，好吧。经济学的话题。我明白了。我想聊点别的。”

En: The man whispers a jaunty tune. A coastal breeze ruffles his hair.
Zh: 男人低声哼唱着愉快的曲调。海岸的微风吹乱了他的头发。

En: Why is that?
Zh: 为什么会这样？

En: He likely believes that when someone has radical ideas, he should still rather present himself as a self-interested moderate, not as a missionary of a minority ideology.
Zh: 他可能是相信：当一个人表达激进想法的时候，他仍该继续表现得像个利己主义的温和派，而非小众意识形态的布道者。

En: It might come in handy to present ideas as a part of reality -- the only reality that exists -- instead of some abstract *true form*. On the other hand, he's not lying when performing this sleight of hand. He's a self-interested moderate.
Zh: 将观念表述为现实——唯一真实存在的现实——的一部分，而非什么抽象的*真实形态*是很管用的。从另一方面讲，他玩的这一手话术并不算是在说谎。他是个利己主义的温和派。

En: I don't want to talk about these interesting things. It's not my business.
Zh: 我不想谈论这些有趣的话题，这都与我无关。

En: But certainly you have to form an opinion on everything and then tell it to everyone.
Zh: 不过你还是不得不对所有事都总结出自己的看法，然后把这些看法告诉所有人。

En: Because?
Zh: 因为？

En: What did he just call you?! A f*g? Oh no, not this again, you just got away from that fucking kid...
Zh: 他刚才叫你什么？！杂种？别了吧，怎么又来了，你才刚逃离那个该死的小屁孩的魔爪……

En: I'm a bit short on *money* right now. Can you give me some of it?
Zh: “我现在手头缺*钱*，你能给我点儿吗？”

En: I literally stole it off him while he slept.
Zh: “我是乘他睡着的时候偷的。”

En: It was like a sign, the wind deposited it before my feet. I feel a closeness to this person.
Zh: “像是个预兆，风把它吹到了我的脚下。对这个人的亲近感在我心底油然而生。”

En: No... I don't want to deal with this again. Did you just call me a f*g?
Zh: “不……怎么又来了。你刚刚是不是骂我杂种了？”

En: Sure thing, my friend, I can help you out. He flips a coin toward you.
Zh: “当然了，我的朋友，让我助你一臂之力。”他朝你扔了一个硬币。

En: Try to catch it.
Zh: 试着接住它。

En: Dresscode: Extreme performance.
Zh: 着装要求：极致表演。

En: The coin lands into your hand as if it has always belonged there. The swallow returns.
Zh: 硬币落在你的手心，仿佛它一直属于那里。燕儿归来了。

En: The coin narrowly slips by your out-stretched fingers and falls to the ground.
Zh: 硬币刚好从你伸出的手指的缝隙中滑落，掉到了地上。

En: Let it fall on the ground.
Zh: 什么都不做，让硬币落在地上。

En: Oh! I'm sorry... didn't mean to throw it like that. It's a thing we harbour folk do, passing around cargo and such.
Zh: “哦！抱歉……我不是故意要这么扔的。我们港口的工人日常就是如此，把东西传来传去什么的。”

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi picks up the coin and hands it to you.
Zh: 曷城警督捡起硬币，然后递给你。

En: Sometimes you gotta do what you gotta do. He nods knowingly. "But these scabs ain't begging -- they ain't holding on to their integrity."
Zh: “有些时候有些事情是不得不去做的。”他刻意地点了点头。“但是这些工贼不是在乞讨——他们没有坚守自己的尊严。”

En: Good one, officer. He grins. "Don't worry, we here have solidarity with the RCM."
Zh: “学得挺像的，警官。”他咧嘴笑了。“别担心，我们跟RCM是一条心。”

En: Psst. Show him your stolen card. Now hast arrived the perfect moment!
Zh: 把您偷来的卡片拿给他看，更待何时呢！

En: No, I need to continue my undercover infiltration of this compound. Otherwise it's as if I just stole the card for no reason.
Zh: “不，我还是要继续神鬼不觉地渗透进这个地方，否则我偷这张卡不就没有意义了吗。”

En: Oh, okay. Good luck with that. He tips his beret. "So, what do you need?"
Zh: “哦，好吧。那就祝你好运了。”他整理了一下贝雷帽。“所以，你有什么需要吗？”

En: I need to know what's behind these gates. I'm *exploring*.
Zh: “我需要知道这些门后面有什么。我正在*探险*。”

En: My friend, I respect the right to roam. The open range awaits... He gazes over the roundabout with a glint of longing.
Zh: “我的朋友，我尊重你四处闲逛的权利。广袤的大地在等待着你……”他带着一丝憧憬的目光凝视着环岛。

En: I'm looking for assistance with a *dead body* situation.
Zh: “我遇到了一具*尸体*的情况，正在寻求协助。”

En: That simple? I just walk in?
Zh: “就这么简单？我就直接这么走进去？”

En: Beneath the jolliness he suddenly seems doubtful.
Zh: 在那份愉悦之下，他突然间显得有些犹疑。

En: If it's all so simple -- why don't the strikebreakers just go up the stairs?
Zh: 如果真的这么简单——为什么那些工贼不直接上楼梯呢？

En: Past Measurehead?
Zh: “从测颅先生身边走过去？”

En: Yeah, the two-and-a-half-metre tall Semenese supremacist there. He points to the bridge above the gates. "Walk right past him..."
Zh: “是啊，就是那边那个身高两米半的萨马拉至上主义者。”他指着大门上方的那座桥。“从他身边走过去……”

En: Uh. Gotcha. I think I have some questions for you first.
Zh: “啊，明白了。我有些问题想先请教你一下。”

En: So you're saying it's actually *quite difficult*.
Zh: “所以你的意思是说其实*颇有难度*。”

En: Hmm, for some reason it doesn't seem like it's going to be that easy...
Zh: “嗯，出于某种原因，好像并没那么容易的样子……”

En: Don't worry, I'm sure it's not *completely* impossible. For example, you could best Measurehead in a physical confrontation.
Zh: “别担心，我敢肯定这也不是*完全*不可能的。比如说，你可以正面打赢测颅先生。”

En: Or you could convert to his Semenese supremacist world view. Or, hmm, he strokes his moustache, "maybe it actually *is* completely impossible."
Zh: “或者你可以皈依于他的萨马拉至上主义世界观。或者，嗯，”他摸了摸胡子，“没准这还*真是*一件完全不可能的任务。”

En: Has anyone here ever bested him in a physical confrontation?
Zh: “这儿真的有人打赢过他吗？”

En: Has any of the scabs tried converting to his world view?
Zh: “有工贼试过皈依于他的世界观吗？”

En: Got it. Another thing...
Zh: “明白了。还有一件事……”

En: Jean-Luc himself would say the philosophy has proven... *overly heroic* for the scabs to convert to. Not enough intuition.
Zh: “让·卢克会说哲学已经证明……这种世界观对于工贼来说*过度英雄主义*了。工贼缺乏那样的直觉。”

En: Not yet, no. He fixes his eyes on you, evaluating your prowess. "He's... incredibly strong."
Zh: “暂时还没有。”他的目光固定在你身上，评估着你的实力。“他强到了……不可思议的地步。”

En: Nothing a couple of solid hooks from Dexter and Sinister won't fix.
Zh: 没有什么是三两下结实的左勾拳右勾拳解决不了的。

En: No-no, I said *scab*.
Zh: “不不，我说的是*工贼*。”

En: Yeah, I'm a scab -- poor and downtrodden. (Wave your fist.) "Right to work!"
Zh: “是啊，我是工贼——贫穷而且饱受压迫。”（挥舞你的拳头。）“工作权！”

En: Oh, sorry, friend. Didn't mean to throw it like that... it's a thing we harbour folk do. Comes naturally.
Zh: “哦！抱歉……我不是故意要这么扔的。我们港口的工人日常就是如此，习惯成自然了。”

En: Appreciated.
Zh: “感激不尽。”

En: Say nothing, pocket the coin.
Zh: 什么也不说，把硬币放进口袋。

En: Appreciated.
Zh: “感激不尽。”

En: Say nothing, pocket the coin.
Zh: 什么也不说，把硬币放进口袋。

En: Don't worry. Much appreciated. (Pick up coin.)
Zh: “没事的。感激不尽。”（捡起硬币。）

En: Say nothing, pick up and pocket coin.
Zh: 什么也不说，把硬币捡起来放进口袋。

En: Leave the coin where it landed.
Zh: 让硬币留在原地。

En: Now you're in a mess. The time hath not arrived. Sorry.
Zh: 您现在真是大大的糟糕了。时机未至。抱歉。

En: Yes, he nods solemnly. "The city has a way of pairing souls. John's been out drinking himself blind..."
Zh: “是啊，”他郑重地点点头。“这座城市有自己的配对灵魂的方式，约翰也一直在外面喝得酩酊大醉……..”

En: He should be more careful, that Johannes. Can I have the card?
Zh: “那个约翰应该小心点的。工卡能给我了吗？”

En: Oh, John's been out drinking himself blind again? He should watch his step, that John. Can I have the card?
Zh: “哦，约翰又在外头喝高了？这家伙真应该多长点心。能把工卡交给我吗？”

En: You're quite the righteous man. He smiles appreciatively. "Honour allows us to keep a respectful distance from each other. So, what do you need?"
Zh: “你是个很正直的人。”他赞赏地笑了。“荣誉让我们彼此之间保持着互相尊重的距离。所以，你有什么需要的吗？”

En: You feel like you earned a significant amount of honour points today.
Zh: 你感觉自己今天积了不少荣誉点。

En: You seem to have spent a lot of time thinking about the political situation.
Zh: “你好像花了不少时间去思考政治形势。”

En: ... then press the button to unlock the door...
Zh: “……然后按下按钮，解锁大门……”

En: Aha.
Zh: “啊哈。”

En: ... then go past him again...
Zh: “……然后再从他身边经过……”

En: ... and you enter the harbour through the office. Esta!
Zh: “……然后通过办公室进入港口。大功告成！”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Right.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: He rubs the back of his head. "Was not meant as... provocation or ridicule."
Zh: 他摸了摸着后脑勺。“我倒没有要……挑衅或者讥讽的意思。”

En: Right, always glad to help out! We're on the same branch, you and I. Not like these slithering scabs.
Zh: “好吧，能帮上忙总是很高兴的！你和我是一类人，跟那些工贼不一样。”

En: Man of honour. He raises his eyebrow. "I respect that. We were talking about scabs, yeah?"
Zh: “一个正人君子。”他扬起眉头。“令人肃然起敬。我们刚才说的是工贼，对吧？”

En: Always glad to help out the RCM. Shame I can't do more -- things are meagre at the moment, due to... He nods toward the protesters.
Zh: “能帮到RCM总是很高兴的！可惜我能做的仅限于此——眼下情势不太方便，因为……”他朝着那帮抗议者点点头。

En: You know -- the winter's fat is slowly running out and all. Still better than scabbing, though.
Zh: “你知道的——冬天囤积的脂肪正在慢慢消退。不过还是比那帮工贼要好。”

En: Right, always glad to help out the RCM! We're on the same branch, you and I. Humans, I mean, not slithering scabs.
Zh: “好吧，能帮到RCM总是很高兴的！咱们都是同一个物种，人类，我的意思是，不像那些工贼。”

En: The Union has problems getting by?
Zh: “工会遇到问题了吗？”

En: I'm always glad to help out when possible -- not like these slithering scabs. He looks at them in disbelief.
Zh: “如果有可能的话，我总是乐意效劳的——不像那些工贼。”他满脸怀疑地看着他们。

En: Nothing to worry about, really. The jam's a bit of a mess, cargo can't get in or out. The rationing is a bit stricter, is all. Supplies should last the strike, though.
Zh: “其实没什么好担心的。封路确实有点麻烦，货物出不去也进不来，配给也苛刻了点，就这些了，不过物资应该足够撑到这次罢工结束。”

En: Every little bit helps, you know.
Zh: “滴水汇成河，你懂得。”

En: Gotta spend money to make money.
Zh: “钱要花得出去才能赚得回来嘛。”

En: Aye, you lost me there, honcho. But it's okay as long as you're not a sorry-ass scab. He looks at them in disbelief.
Zh: “是的，你把我弄糊涂了，老板，不过只要你不是那些可悲的工贼中的一员就行。”他用不可理喻的眼神看着他们。

En: Aye, now here's the thing... at any time, some other capitalist could take the place over with the blink of an eye, without even owning any of the buildings.
Zh: “情况是这样的……在任何时候，其他资本家都不用买下任何建筑，眨眼就能占据这个地方，甚至都不用真的拥有任何建筑。”

En: Right.
Zh: “没错。”

En: So you feel like you should also get something?
Zh: “所以你觉得自己也该得到些什么？”

En: I don't think I understand.
Zh: “我好像不太明白你的意思。”

En: He takes a swig from his flask. "So why should they be the *only* ones with the power to take stuff over?"
Zh: 他喝了一口酒。“所以凭什么*只有*他们有权接管这一切呢？”

En: I guess that makes sense.
Zh: “有道理。”

En: It's still kinda confusing to me, but whatever.
Zh: “我还是不太懂，不过无所谓了。”

En: I guess you're right, I should. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我想你是对的，我确实该这么做。”（总结。）

En: He's probably in his container at the harbour, so head right on in there. He re-adjusts the beret on his head.
Zh: “他应该在港口区自己的集装箱里，你直接去那儿就行。”他重新调整了一下头上的贝雷帽。

En: Right on, brother.
Zh: “说得对，兄弟。”

En: Just the way the world works, boiadeiro.
Zh: “世界就是这样运作的，牛仔。”

En: You shouldn't take what's not yours.
Zh: “你不该拿不属于你的东西。”

En: That's a strange way to go about it.
Zh: 这种做法挺奇怪的。

En: Basically, you're a self-interested moderate?
Zh: “所以你基本上就是个利己主义的温和派？”

En: That's one way to call it, friend. He chuckles. "An unnecessarily complicated way."
Zh: “你也可以这么说，朋友。”他轻笑到。“虽然有点过于复杂了。”

En: Then how can anything ever be 'mine'? But that's okay -- we don't have to agree. The prairie is wide enough for all of us.
Zh: “那到底怎样才会有东西真的属于‘我’呢？不过无所谓——我们没必要达成一致。这片牧场这么大，足够容得下我们所有人。”

En: What is this, news of the world? Talk about *personal stuff*. Get to know him.
Zh: 你们都在聊什么啊，世界局势？聊聊*个人问题*，了解一下他是怎样一个人。

En: Maybe I am a boiadeiro?
Zh: 也许我是个牛仔？

En: You already know the answer.
Zh: 你已经知道答案了。

En: No.
Zh: 你不是。

En: Pushed how?
Zh: “怎么个被迫法？”

En: Were you involved in his death?
Zh: “你跟他的死有关联吗？”

En: Did you kill him?
Zh: “是你杀了他吗？”

En: I ain't the murderin' type. But that's just me. Large organizations like our Union have all sorts of men -- with all sorts of skills.
Zh: “我不属于会杀人的那一类人，不过这也只是我的情况。像我们工会这么大的组织，里面的人肯定鱼龙混杂——也各有所长。”

En: This seditious talk sounds like communism. (Just so we're on the same page: Communism is basically wanting to kill the rich people or deporting them to a labour camp in southeast Graad. But don't say that out loud if you're a communist.)
Zh: 这种煽动性的言论听起来很像康米主义。（背景介绍：康米主义主要就是想杀掉所有富人，或者把他们流放到格拉德东南部的劳改营。但如果你是个康米主义者的话，这种大实话还是别公然说出来得好。）

En: Laziness, self-interest, and negligence certainly fit the doctrine espoused here.
Zh: “懒惰，利己主义还有玩忽职守，确实跟这里人拥护的信条很搭。”

En: At least some people are willing to make the necessary sacrifices in order to reach higher, and to better the world.
Zh: “至少有人愿意为了更高的地位以及一个更美好的世界而做出必要的牺牲。”

En: Is it the dulcet tone of ultraliberalism I hear?
Zh: “我刚才听到的是超自由主义那美妙的论调吗？”

En: Sure thing. A gust of wind blows through his hair.
Zh: “当然。”一阵风吹过他的头发。

En: Hold up, what does that mean?
Zh: “等等，那是什么意思？”

En: He was an agent of the opposition, attempting to undermine our honourable efforts.
Zh: “他听命于反动势力，试图破坏我们正义的斗争。”

En: Fair enough. Seems interesting.
Zh: “很好。好像挺有意思的。”

En: Words guide our life, we commune with each thing through its word. Every word is also a name, is it not? He smiles.
Zh: “字词引导着我们的生活，我们通过字词与万事万物建立起联系。每个字词都是一个名字，不是吗？”他笑了笑。

En: It doesn't sound that cool. Sounds antiquated, obsolete, *passé*. It's not *disco*.
Zh: 听起来不怎么酷，甚至很过时，落伍，*陈旧*，一点都不*迪斯科*。

En: That of a noble peasant or a travelling herdsman. True to yourself, independent in your actions, loyal to your friends.
Zh: 高尚的农民或是游荡的牧民。忠于自己，独立行动，忠于好友。

En: What if...
Zh: 假如……

En: No.
Zh: 不行。

En: But...
Zh: 但是……

En: Maybe...
Zh: 也许……

En: I could...
Zh: 我可以……

En: Actually do you know anything about the Hardie boys?
Zh: “说起来，你知道哈迪兄弟的事吗？”

En: Hold up. Four pieces? Helmet, cuirass, gauntlets, boots... what about the leggings?
Zh: “等等，你说四件？头盔，胸甲，手套，靴子……下身的护甲呢？”

En: Oh, they're just gone. They don't exist anymore, if they ever did at all. He gives you a jolly shrug. "Forget about them. I did."
Zh: “哦，就没了，不见了，如果真的有这么个部件的话。”他愉悦地耸了耸肩。“别追究了，我就是这么做的。”

En: I've already got some armour. I'm pretty happy without struggling to find more.
Zh: “我已经找到几件盔甲了，这样挺好，没必要再去苦苦寻找更多了。”

En: So you have. He smooths a stray moustache hair with his thumb and smiles. "No wonder you're a detective!"
Zh: “你还真找到了。”男人用拇指理了理杂乱的胡须，然后笑了。“难怪你能当警探！”

En: He can be very *enterprising*... The lieutenant looks at his feet, hiding his face.
Zh: “他有时候会尤其*进取*……”警督低头看着自己的脚。

En: You have no business with those boots. But here we are.
Zh: 你跟那双靴子没有任何关系。不过它们就在这里。

En: Four pieces of armour up for grabs, then. But I already sent the boots away...
Zh: “那就是一共有四件护甲，不过靴子已经被我送走了……”

En: The jolly man shrugs. "I guess you won't be collecting them all then. That's less work for you, at least!"
Zh: 快乐的男人耸耸肩。“那我猜你是集不齐了。至少这样你就可以少费点功夫啦！”

En: All of it? The lieutenant raises his brow. "There are junior officers out there, eager to prove themselves. I would leave *some* for them -- and I would leave the *boots* to Processing -- but okay. Let's find *all of it*."
Zh: “全部？”警督扬起眉头。“我们有不少急着想证明自己能力的初阶警员，换作是我的话我会把*几个部件*的调查工作交给他们——并且让*靴子*继续走局里的程序——不过好吧，我们把*全套*护甲都找出来吧。”

En: Especially getting those boots off him. You'd have an easier time wrestling the spurs off a boiadeiro than that.
Zh: “特别是你还得把靴子弄下来，有那个工夫这么为难自己的话那还不如去跟牛仔摔角去呢。”

En: Good luck if you go for those boots, though. You'd have an easier time wrestling the spurs off a boiadeiro than getting them off him.
Zh: “不过如果你决意要弄到那双靴子的话，那就祝你好运吧。跟牛仔摔角都要比从他身上脱下那双靴子要简单。”

En: It was late one night as I was leaving the harbour. He was waiting on the corner in a classic Saramirizian jacket, a lot like the one you've got there...
Zh: “有一天深夜，我正准备离开港口。他穿着一件老派的萨拉米尔泽夹克在街角等着，就像你身上这件……”

En: A key, huh? He runs his fingers through his moustache. "What door is this key supposed to open?"
Zh: “一把钥匙哈？”他用手指抚弄着嘴唇上的胡子。“那这把钥匙能打开的到底是怎样一扇门呢？”

En: He said it belonged to a weasel.
Zh: “他说是属于一只黄鼠狼的。”

En: I don't know, some whistleblower's I think?
Zh: “我不知道，我想大概是告密者的？”

En: Oh, say no more. I got you. He taps the side of his nose with a little wink.
Zh: “哦，无需多言，我明白了。”他的手指轻轻点了下鼻侧，朝你挤了个眼。

En: They're sort of like you. Preserve the rule of law and all that. Except it's Evrart's law. He takes a swig from his flask. "But, really, they're just like you."
Zh: “他们跟你们有点像，也会维持法纪之类的，只不过他们维持的是艾弗拉特的法律。”他从酒瓶里喝了一口。“但是，说真的，他们真的跟你们没太多区别。”

En: Los Ardies? He smiles. "They're an independent militant group. A bit too high-strung, but it comes with the responsibility."
Zh: “洛斯·阿迪斯？”他笑了。“他们是一个特立独行的激进团体。有点太绷着了，不过这也是他们肩负的责任的结果。”

En: Is he actually comparing you -- an officer of the law -- to some neighbourhood vigilantes?!
Zh: 他难道真的是在拿你——一名执法人员——跟某些街坊邻里的小喽啰比？！

En: Come on. He's just trying to keep the peace.
Zh: 行了，他只是想避免冲突。

En: There is only one law, friend -- and that's me!
Zh: “这世上只有一种法律，朋友——那就是我！”

En: Let it go.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: Why not? Must be nice being something else for a change. The rest of us are just folks.
Zh: “为什么不呢？如果换个人应该也挺新鲜的。其他人不过是普通人而已。”

En: Ola, Tequila Sunset, grand boiadeiro! Mañana looks at you with soft and hazy eyes.
Zh: “你好啊，龙舌兰日落，伟大的牛仔！”他用柔和又朦胧的眼光注视着你。

En: Olá, Tequila Sunset, boiadeiro grande! He looks at you with soft and hazy eyes.
Zh: “你好啊，龙舌兰日落，牛仔伟哉！”他用柔和又朦胧的眼光注视着你。

En: If it's so simple -- why did the scab leader warn you of the guards blocking the way?
Zh: 如果真这么简单的话——那工贼领袖为什么还要警告你会有守卫挡路？

En: Wire tapping. Telescopic batons. Futuristic circuit-bending to infiltrate harbour machinery. Maybe you could even knock that Kvalsund crane over using some remote controls...
Zh: “电话窃听。望远镜警棍。通过科幻的电路改装技术黑进港口区的机组。你说不定能远程将那台库瓦桑德吊车掀翻在地……”

En: He stops to think. "Or... calling in tactical air support! You guys have air support, right?"
Zh: 他停下来想了想。“或者……呼叫空中战术支援！你们有空中支援的，对吗？”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "Wait, we have *air support*?! Why didn't you tell me?"
Zh: （转向金。）“等等，咱们有*空中支援*？！你怎么不告诉我？”

En: We sure do, and don't you forget it.
Zh: “那当然，你最好给我记住这一点。”

En: Walk away. [Leave.]
Zh: 走开。[离开。]

En: Knocked him out good... he greets you. "Like the flying desperado!"
Zh: “直接撂倒……”他向你打招呼。“就像个亡命之徒！”

En: Imagine -- you cops going on a strike, but then another cop comes in and says: 'Let us cop! For less money.' He chuckles, then realizes:
Zh: “试想一下——你们警察在罢工，但是又来了一个警察，然后说：‘那就让我们替他们吧！钱少点也行。’”他轻笑起来，然后反应了过来：

En: You're hazy on the notion of a *scab*. Smells like politics, though. Maybe it's got something to do with the flask he reaches for, from time to time?
Zh: 你对*工贼*的理解比较微妙，不过这个词好像有政治的意味。也许跟他时不时就会举起的酒瓶有关？

En: You a scab *now*? The man with jolly eyes winks at you.
Zh: “*现在*你又成工贼了？”男人朝你眨了眨满是笑意的眼睛。

En: Evrart's got a lot of knowledge about a lot of things, aye. Doesn't often dole it out, though. But sure, why not...
Zh: “艾弗拉特的确懂的事情很多，只不过提得很少。不过当然了，为什么不……”

En: I'm not really an admirer of dead bodies. Might be someone else from the Union can render assistance... He shrugs.
Zh: “我对尸体没兴趣，也许工会里有其他人能帮到你……”他耸耸肩。

En: Because we don't. We don't have air support -- or any of those other things.
Zh: “因为我们并没有空中支援——或者其他类似的东西。”

En: I'm pretty sure we don't. At least not all of these things.
Zh: “我非常确定我们并没有那些技术，至少不是全部。”

En: Aye-aye, sir. The easy-going man winks at you.
Zh: “知道啦，先生。”这个随和的男人朝你眨眨眼。

En: I get it, hush-hush about the secret technology. He pats the side of his nose with his index finger.
Zh: “秘密技术要保密，我懂的。”他用食指按了按鼻翼。

En: It's that really cool flying desperado spin kick you did, boiadeiro. Often performed by the most hard-bodied boiadeiros on the steppes.
Zh: “你之前那下飞身回旋踢真是太酷了，牛仔，这招只有草原上最强的牛仔才使得出来。”

En: It was nothing.
Zh: “没什么。”

En: Yeah, I sure did that thing.
Zh: “是啊，我肯定做过那种事。”

En: He nods energetically. "So, what's on your mind this time?"
Zh: 他用力点点头。“所以，这次你又在想什么呢？”

En: So... He stares at you with his hazy eyes. "How'd you like our harbour?"
Zh: “所以……”他用朦胧的双眼盯着你。“你觉得我们的港口怎么样？”

En: Big guy -- he means the leader of the scabs.
Zh: 大块头——他的意思是工贼领袖。

En: Was nice enough.
Zh: “挺好的。”

En: It's but a rest area on the path leading across open plains, he notes solemnly, then turns to you, a wide smile adorning his face:
Zh: “这不过是个通往开阔平原的道路上供人休息的区域，”他严肃地评价道，然后面朝你，脸上挂着一个十分灿烂的笑容：

En: Complete shit.
Zh: “彻头彻尾的一坨屎。”

En: Speakin' of! What brings the RCM here? To the Wild North? Come to see the strife?
Zh: “话说回来，是什么风把RCM吹到到狂野的北部来的？你们是来看戏的吗？”

En: Evrart said you have a key to a door?
Zh: “艾弗拉特说你有一把门钥匙？”

En: I guess getting past the man ain't that simple after all… He pauses to think. "So, what now?"
Zh: “我猜过那个男人那一关确实没那么简单……”他停下来想了一会儿。“所以，现在该怎么办？”

En: Who or what is a flying desperado?
Zh: “亡命之徒？那是什么东西？或者是什么人？”

En: The boiadeiro stares at you with respect -- then gestures towards the trickles of blood adorning your clothes.
Zh: 牛仔充满敬意地盯着你——然后指了指你衣服上点缀的血迹。

En: Yeah, I got shot. No big deal.
Zh: “我中枪了，小事小事。”

En: It really hurts, Mañana. Pain -- that's what your strike brought upon us.
Zh: “当时可痛死我了，我跟你说。痛——这就是你们的罢工带给我们的。”

En:
Danger comes with the boiadeiro lifestyle, right?	“牛仔的生活方总是伴随着危险，不是吗？”
0x0100006B00003F02	Dialogue Text	GATES / MANANA	Call Me Mañana	He shrugs. There are types of danger. The one I'm usually concerned with is lung cancer, or getting mauled by wildlife. Not... bullets."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“危险分好多种，我经常担心的是染上肺癌，或者被野兽咬死，却从没担心过……子弹。”

En: Yet you live? He nods approvingly. "It calls back to an older era, where this was commonplace. You have a true boiadeiro heart."
Zh: “但你却活了下来？”他赞许地点点头。“这让我想起某个久远的年代，在那时挨枪子是司空见惯的一件事。你拥有一颗真正的牛仔之心。”

En: He shrugs. "Any political movement goes through a period of strife. Shame you got mixed up in it, though."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“所有政治运动都会经历一段时间的冲突。不过还是很遗憾把你卷进来了。”

En: What's going to happen next?
Zh: “接下来会发生什么？”

En: Time will tell... He shrugs. "I'll tell Evrart you dropped by. I'm sure he'll be glad." He gives you a small wink.
Zh: “时间会告诉我们一切……”他耸耸肩。“我会告诉艾弗拉特你来过。我敢肯定他一定会高兴的。”他朝你眨了眨眼。

En: Time will tell... He shrugs. "I'll tell Evrart you dropped by. I'm sure he'll be glad."
Zh: “时间会告诉我们一切……”他耸耸肩。“我会告诉艾弗拉特你来过。我敢肯定他一定会高兴的。”

En: Time will tell... He shrugs. "I'll tell the bossman you dropped by. I'm sure he'll be glad."
Zh: “时间会告诉我们一切……”他耸耸肩。“我会告诉老大你来过。我敢肯定他一定会高兴的。”

En: Okay, I think might be Harry. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，我想我的名字应该叫哈里。”（总结。）

En: My name is Harrier Du Bois. I think. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我的名字是哈里尔·杜博阿。应该没错。”（总结。）

En: Harry... he repeats. with a quizzical expression. "It's cool. But I'm sure you could come up with something even cooler."
Zh: “哈里……”他重复着，脸上带着一副嘲弄的神情。“挺酷的。不过我肯定你会想出个更酷的名字。”

En: A strong, solid name telling of dedication and honour. He gives a small salute. "Well met, Harrier."
Zh: “一个坚强可靠的名字，诉说着奉献和荣耀。”他向你敬了个礼。“很高兴认识你，哈里尔。”

En: Heh. He smiles. "The little *boia* did good on his promise."
Zh: “哈。”他笑了。“那个小*仔儿*还真兑现了自己的承诺。”

En: Hey, you're the man in boots at the gates -- Cuno said you know about the armour.
Zh: “喂，你就是那个传说中在大门附近的穿靴子男人——坤诺说你知道盔甲的事。”

En: Cuno told me you were supposed to know about the armour.
Zh: “坤诺告诉我，你应该知道盔甲的事。”

En: His promise?
Zh: “他的诺言？”

En: To get me into trouble. To *sic the pigs* on me -- pardon the choice of words. Not mine.
Zh: “把麻烦送我门口，*派猪头来日*我——请原谅这种措辞，不是我说的。”

En: What happened?
Zh: “出什么事了？”

En: What happened?
Zh: “出什么事了？”

En: I was asked to look into that armour situation. Official Union probe, you know -- track it down, see who took it.
Zh: “我奉命调查盔甲的事情。这次是工会官方的调查行动，你懂的——追查盔甲的下落，调查是谁拿走的。”

En: Serves me right for doing menial footwork. I dropped that probe right then and there -- and it *still* got me into trouble, he smiles. "One bad move is all it takes."
Zh: “我活该干这种卑微的跑腿活。我很快就放弃了任务——结果*还是*给自己惹上了麻烦，”他笑了笑。“一步错，步步错。”

En: So Cuno used us to, what? Scare you?
Zh: “所以坤诺想利用我们干什么？吓唬你？”

En: Did you?
Zh: “你查了吗？”

En: Did you?
Zh: “你查了吗？”

En: At first I thought -- why not, maybe the pieces can feed the strike? Buy us a few more days under the sun, you know.
Zh: “一开始我想——不挺好吗，也许这些东西对罢工会有所帮助？给我们多争取多几天公开活动的时间，你懂的。”

En: So I went to this boy. He said he'll make me his *prison bitch*. He's got *eyes everywhere*, the cops in his pocket and he's the king of Jamrock.
Zh: “所以我去找那个男孩。他说他要把我变成他的*性奴*，他*到处都有眼线*，警察都是他的囊中之物，他是加姆洛克之王。”

En: It's a minor nuisance. It's all good. He contemplates taking a swig from his flask.
Zh: “这只是个小麻烦，整体来看一切都还好。”他若有所思地从酒瓶里闷了一口。

En: He thinks -- not yet. Better to get this business out of the way. Sweeter then.
Zh: 他想——但是还没有。最好把这件事给解决了。之后能尝到甜头。

En: The probe into the armour -- what did you learn?
Zh: “追查盔甲的时候——你得到什么消息了？”

En: I learned that people don't want to talk to a drunk Union man about some armour.
Zh: “我得知了没人会愿意跟一个醉醺醺的工会成员谈论盔甲之类的事。”

En: Not much. Technical stuff mostly. That was the interesting part.
Zh: “不多，大部分都是技术上的事，这恰恰是有意思的地方。”

En: What else?
Zh: “还有呢？”

En: Okay. Anything else?
Zh: “好吧。还有别的吗？”

En: I did some research into this *armadura*. Let's say I have friends at the library, he explains with a wry smile. "I didn't get into the material science, just how it comes off."
Zh: “我研究了一下这件*护甲*。这么说吧，我在图书馆那边有几个朋友，”他苦笑着解释道。“我没有太深入材料科学，只是了解了一下这套护甲是如何被人从尸体上取下来的。”

En: What sort of technical stuff?
Zh: “哪种技术上的事？”

En: In parts. Four in total. The helmet was the first to go, the kid says he tore it off and kicked it into the sea. I believe him. The boots... He looks down at your feet. "Well, it looks like you're wearing them."
Zh: “护甲分为几个部位，一共四个部件。首先是头盔，小孩说被他扯下来然后踢海里去了，我相信他的话。靴子的话……“他低头看着你的脚，“好像正穿在你脚上呢。”

En: In parts. Four in total. The helmet was the first to go, the kid says he tore it off and kicked it into the sea. I believe him. The boots were still on the guy last I saw. Too hard to remove.
Zh: “护甲分为几个部位，一共四个部件。首先是头盔，小孩说被他扯下来然后踢海里去了，我相信他的话。靴子的话，我上次看见那个人的时候还在他脚上，很难弄下来。”

En: So -- as I count -- there are two parts missing: the gauntlets and the cuirass. This is where I left off. Too much hassle. More like a job for some *milicia*.
Zh: “所以——我一合计——还有两部分不见了：手套和胸甲。我在这里就停手了。实在是太困难。更像是留给*军人*的工作。”

En: Thank you for the cooperation, sir.
Zh: “感谢你的配合，先生。”

En: How then?
Zh: “怎么呢？”

En: How *does* it come off?
Zh: “那它是*怎么*被人取下来的？”

En: It's implied he finds it unlikely that you will succeed in this.
Zh: 这意味着他觉得你不太可能能做到。

En: No problem. He finally takes a swig from the flask. "If you see that kid, thank him from Call Me Mañana. Thank him for showing me the *way*."
Zh: “没问题，”他最后又从酒瓶里喝了一口。“如果你下次见到那个孩子，代我，叫·我·马列拉，谢谢他，感谢他为我指出一条*明路*。”

En: He is sincerely grateful he is not tracking down pieces of armour right now.
Zh: 他由衷地感激自己现在不用追踪那套盔甲部件了。

En: I'm ambitious. I'm going to find *all of it*. All the pieces.
Zh: “我有野心，我要把它们*全部*找出来，一件不落。”

En: I'm going to find *one* piece of it. One is enough.
Zh: “我打算找*一件*出来。一件就够了。”

En: Actually, I may have better things to do too.
Zh: “其实，我可能还还是想干点别的。”

En: Nice. The moustached man nods in approval. "From what I learned it was a very heavy, boring set of armour."
Zh: “很好，”小胡子男人赞许地点点头。“据我所知，那是一套非常笨重又无趣的盔甲。”

En: I agree, officer. Let's focus on the hanging and later some junior officers can take care of the rest.
Zh: “同意。我们还是先集中调查吊着的人，这件事可以之后交给几个低阶警员处理就行。”

En: Nice and balanced, the lieutenant nods. "Some junior officers can take care of the rest."
Zh: “合理的安排，”警督点点头。“剩下的几件交给低阶警员就好。”

En: All of it? The lieutenant raises his brow. "There are junior officers out there, eager to prove themselves. I would leave *some* for them, but okay. Let's find *all of it*."
Zh: “全部？”警督扬起眉头。“我们有不少急着想证明自己能力的初阶警员，换作是我的话我会把*几个部件*的调查工作交给他们，不过好吧，我们把*全套*护甲都找出来吧。”

En: Smart choice, the moustached man agrees. "It's only that *one* spot you need armoured too -- the one the bullet hits."
Zh: “明智的选择，”小胡子男人表示赞同。“到头来你只有一个部位需要防护——会被子弹击中的部位。”

En: A Mesquese epic then, all across Martinaise. He glances south, where the canal runs: "I hope it will be a real *bonanza* for you."
Zh: “整个马丁内斯境内，梅斯克出产的史诗级杰作，”他看向南边，运河流过的地方：“希望它会为你带来真正的*好运*。”

En: I was hoping you could tell me.
Zh: “我还指望你能告诉我呢。”

En: I got *that* key right here. And let me tell you, it's mighty good of you to help us out during the strike -- working class solidarity, as they say.
Zh: “*那把*钥匙就在我这里。我这么跟你说吧，你人真的很好，愿意在罢工期间帮我们——这就是所谓的工人阶级一条心。”

En: I heard something about a weasel and it didn't sound like a local *polar* weasel, if you know what I mean. (Wink.)
Zh: “我听说了一只黄鼠狼的事，听起来并不像是本地的*极地*黄鼠狼，你明白我的意思吧。”（眨眨眼。）

En: I saw an opportunity and I took it. I'm a hustlegrinder.
Zh: “我发现一个机会，然后就抓住了。我是个激进的实干派。”

En: I'm not really doing this for political reasons.
Zh: “我这么做并不是出于政治原因。”

En: I'm not opening this door for myself. I'm opening it for all working men.
Zh: “我想打开那扇门为的并不是我自己，而是所有的工人兄弟。”

En: Polar weasel? Oh... I know what you mean. I'm pretty sure he's actually Occidental though. So you're gonna be commitin' fratricide, my racist friend.
Zh: “极地黄鼠狼？哦……我懂你的意思了。不过我很肯定那个人是奥西登人。所以你打算要同室操戈吗，我的种族主义朋友。”

En: What you're looking for is a basement door behind the greenhouse -- that's behind the Whirling-in-Rags -- that's all I know. Our organisation is what you call *compartmentalized*. Means we keep out of each other's business.
Zh: “你要找的是温室后面的一扇地下室门——温室就在褴褛飞旋后方——我知道的就这么多。我们组织的架构是所谓的*分而治之*，也就是说我们互不干涉彼此的事务。”

En: Okay, but where did you get the key from?
Zh: “好吧，但钥匙你是从哪里弄到的？”

En: Where did you get the key from?
Zh: “钥匙你是从哪里弄到的？”

En: Hehe, I'm not so sure about the hustle-grind, but... He waves at you. "You know, it doesn't matter. It's a good thing you're doing. Thanks."
Zh: “呵呵，激进啊，实干啊，我是不那么肯定，不过……”他朝你挥挥手。“你知道吗，这不要紧。你做的是一件好事。谢谢。”

En: Oh so the none-of-the-above type, are you? I get it, I get it. I like to keep my distance too. He nods. "But it doesn't matter. It's a good thing you're doing. Thanks."
Zh: “哦，所以你是*以上都不是*那一卦的，是吗？我明白，我明白的，我也喜欢跟人保持距离。”他点点头。“不过不要紧，你做了件好事。谢谢。”

En: I knew this man was a commie. He smiles, tilting his head. "And it's a good thing you're doing too. Thanks."
Zh: “我知道这人是个康米分子。”他笑着歪起头。“你做的是一件好事。谢谢。”

En: The janitor gave it to me. Nice fella. We talked about life and things that really, *truly* matter. His gaze wanders off into the distance.
Zh: “保洁员给我的。他人很好，我跟他一起谈论生活以及一些*真真*要紧的事。”他凝视着远方。

En: He shakes his head. "I'm more of a philosophical dockworker. I like to talk about the big picture stuff. Who I am. Who you are. What we are fighting for..." The man takes a big sip from his flask.
Zh: 他摇摇头。“我更接近于一个哲学派的码头工人。我喜欢讨论宏大的命题，我是谁，你是谁，我们为何而战……”男人从酒瓶里喝了一大口。

En: He's a chatty guy. *Wants* to talk about the strike. Return once you've met the Union boss, and are on a better footing with the organization.
Zh: 他是个健谈的家伙，*愿意*讲罢工的事。等你见过工会老大、对工会组织更加了解了以后再来。

En: That weasel I visited. Turns out he has one hell of a colonial mug collection.
Zh: “那个黄鼠狼我已经去找过了，没想到他有一整套殖民主义马克杯的藏品。”

En: Yeah... He nods, rubbing his chin. "The janitor who gave me the key to his apartment said the guy's a bit of an asshole."
Zh: “是吧……”摸着下巴点了点头。“把他公寓的钥匙给我的保洁员说那家伙挺混蛋的。”

En: Yes, his mug-collection certainly represented... *antiquated* social values.
Zh: “是的，他的马克杯藏品确实代表了……*落后的*社会价值观。”

En: A man with such a funny mug collection can't be that bad.
Zh: “拥有那种搞笑马克杯藏品的人，肯定坏不到哪里去。”

En: All I'm saying is he had lots of mugs.
Zh: “我只是在说他有很多马克杯而已。”

En: Like I said before, I don't know much about this weasel, but the bossman said he's a real piece of work. He touches his beret. "Thanks for helping out, friend."
Zh: “就像我之前说的，我不太了解这个黄鼠狼，但是老大说他确实是朵奇葩。”他摸了摸自己的贝雷帽。“感谢你的帮助，朋友。”

En: Right. So where is everyone?
Zh: “好吧。其他人都去哪里了？”

En: It calls back to an older era, where this was commonplace, though. You have a true boiadeiro heart.
Zh: “这让我想起某个久远的年代，在那时这种事可谓是家常便饭。你拥有一颗真正的牛仔之心。”

En: Yet it calls back to an older era, where this was commonplace. You have a true boiadeiro heart.
Zh: “这让我想起某个久远的年代，在那时这种事可谓是家常便饭。你拥有一颗真正的牛仔之心。”

En: Hidin', gatherin' themselves. The harbour's in full lockdown, friend. No getting in or out... for the time being.
Zh: “他们正在暗处集结。港口已经完全封闭了，朋友。不能进出……目前。”

En: You can't help me get inside?
Zh: “你不能帮我进去吗？”

En: No, man. Not today. Today is war.
Zh: “做不到，伙计，至少今天不行。今天将会有一场战争。”

En: What's the strike about anyway?
Zh: “罢工到底是怎么一回事？”

En: You know... serious business. He smiles. "I'm sure the big boss would be glad to tell you. You'll have to ask him first."
Zh: “你懂的……罢工是件严肃的事。”他笑了。“我肯定大老板会很乐意告诉你的。你可以先去问他。”

En: Right! You talked to the boss, eye-to-eye -- like men of the plain. If you have any more questions, I'm set to talk.
Zh: “没错！你去跟老板聊聊，面对面眼对眼——像大平原上的男子一样。如果你还有其他问题，我随时都愿意奉陪。”

En: Serious business, as I'm sure you now realize. The boss would have been glad to tell you. Alas, you cannot get to him anymore.
Zh: “大事件，我肯定现在你已经意识到了。老板会很乐意跟你说说的。呜呼，可惜你现在见不着他了。”

En: What will *you* be doing now?
Zh: “那*你*现在打算做什么呢？”

En: I'll be okay here, doing lookout. He surveys the red flags, draped from the harbour gates. "Quite the sight, aren't they? Getting to like that red, I am..."
Zh: “我在这里挺好的，就望望风。”他看着港口大门悬挂下来的红色旗帜。“挺壮观的，不是吗？我都开始喜欢红色了……”

En: Don't worry about me. I live to alleviate the worries of our brothers. See if any other insane killers turn up. Then I'll run. And live.
Zh: “不用担心我，我活着就是为了帮兄弟们排忧解难。我会留心观察有没有别的杀人狂现身，有的话我就跑，然后活下来。”

En: I'm sure you'll do good.
Zh: “我确定你会没事的。”

En: Try not to get shot, eh?
Zh: “小心别挨枪子，好吗？”

En: You know it, friend. He smooths his moustache with his hand.
Zh: “你知道的，朋友。”他用手顺着自己的胡子。

En: I'm sure I'll be luckier than you, friend. His grin is as wide as a desert.
Zh: “我肯定自己能比你走运，朋友。”他笑的时候嘴咧得像沙漠一样宽阔。

En: The vile C-F race cauldron of pederasty, please.
Zh: “C-F类，鸡奸者的大染缸，多谢。”

En: My body is unimportant. I'm with the police and we need to get into the harbour.
Zh: “我的身体不重要。我是警察，我们需要进入港口。”

En: You could *internalize* Measurehead's race theory. It would *enrich* you rhetorically.
Zh: 你可以*内化*测颅先生的种族理论，这将*充实*你的修辞学。

En: SO IT WAS. MY UNPROMISING RACE PUPIL ENTERED THE HARBOUR AND USED MY SUPERIOR TO GIVE ME ORDERS. I SALUTE YOUR CUNNING AND WILL REMOVE THE BODY FROM THE TREE -- WITH MY BARE HANDS.
Zh: “原来如此。我这位没出息的种族学生进了港口，然后利用我的上级给我下了指令。我向你的狡猾致敬。我会去把树上那具尸体弄下来——徒手。”

En: Know anything about this mug? (Show him the mug.)
Zh: “这个马克杯你知道些什么吗？”（把马克杯拿给他看。）

En: He does not so much as glance at the object.
Zh: 他甚至看都没看那个东西一眼。

En: Know anything about it?
Zh: “知道些什么吗？”

En: This your kind of thing?
Zh: “这是你喜欢的东西吗？”

En: STOP SHOWING ME YOUR PATHETIC CUP. I HAVE NO INTEREST IN IT.
Zh: “别再把那个无聊的杯子拿给我看了。我对它一点兴趣都没有。”

En: Put this into the trash lately?
Zh: “这个杯子是你丢进垃圾堆的吗？”

En: He had nothing to do with it.
Zh: 这跟他无关。

En: Does he strike you as the kind of man who puts mugs into trash?
Zh: 你觉得他是那种会把马克杯扔进垃圾箱的人吗？

En: He's right there. It's almost impossible to *get it on* unless you're both drunk. It's too scary when people are sober.
Zh: 他说得对。要想*性奋*起来几乎是不可能的，除非你俩都喝醉了。清醒的时候还做这事就太可怕了。

En: This is your chance, he's talking... rip into him with a punch and catch him off guard.
Zh: 你的机会来了，他正在说话……冲上去狠狠给他一拳，打他个措手不及。

En: No... don't 'rip into' anyone. You're sensitive, remember? Communicate.
Zh: 不……不要‘冲向’任何人。你是个感性的人，还记得吗？要交流。

En: I need some of that race-stuff in my brain. Sock it to me.
Zh: “我需要往脑子里装点种族之类的知识，快来灌输给我吧。”

En: INANE POPULAR CULTURE LINGO. IT IS INTELLECTUALLY *UNINTERESTING* TO 'SOCK' ANYTHING TO AN INFANT SUCH AS YOU.
Zh: “都是些流行文化的空话。要给你这样的巨婴‘灌输’任何东西从理性上来说都*毫无乐趣可言*。”

En: Welcome to Revachol!!
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！！”

En:
Okay. I'll wait here, just take the body down. (Wait here while Measurehead goes.)	“好吧，我会在这里等着，把尸体放下来就好。（测颅先生离开的时候在原地等待。）
0x010000060001894D	Dialogue Text	GATES / MEASUREHEAD	Measurehead's Babe	Yeah, Jean-Luc must be really tearing it up over there. I wish I could see it..."
Zh: 让·卢克一定在那儿大肆破坏呢。真希望能亲眼目睹那场面……”

En: It's late outside, isn't it? You guys must really like doing what you do. To be out so late... She turns to behold the behemoth appearing around the corner, approaching the gates.
Zh: “天色已经很晚了，不是吗？你们这群人肯定真的很喜欢自己的工作吧，这么晚还可以在外面……”她转而注视着角落里出现的巨大身影，对方正朝着大门走过来。

En: Don't worry. Physical stuff like this is *really* easy for Measurehead. Mental stuff too. He's really spiritual, you know. She looks around.
Zh: “别担心。这种体力活对于测颅先生来说就是*小菜一碟*，脑力活也一样。他是个很有灵性的人，你知道的。”她看了看周围。

En: My jam is a mysterious *fourth* thing.
Zh: “我想了解*第四类*。”

En: True. That *is* a noble cranium -- but you got a hard noggin yourself!
Zh: 的确，那*是*一颗高贵的颅骨——但是你自己的脑袋也很硬！

En: Colourful tassels are, let's be honest, not a good sartorial choice for this century. You might want to avoid wooden clogs too.
Zh: 咱们还是说实话吧，彩色流苏绝不是本世纪最佳的着装选择，木屐你也还是别穿的好。

En: Yeah but you still *serve* him. How does that factor into your life?
Zh: “话是这么说，但你仍然是在*服侍*他。你是怎么消化这一点的？”

En: Yeah but you still *serve* them. How does that factor into your life?
Zh: “是啊，但你仍然是在*服侍*他。你是怎么消化这一点的？”

En: Am I mildly worried he might take it *seriously*, the lieutenant thinks. Yes. I am -- he concludes. I *am* worried.
Zh: 我莫非在担心他会把这件事*当真*？警督想道。是的——他总结道。我*确实*在担心。

En: YOU WILL NOT. YOU CANNOT WITHSTAND THE WASTE LAND OF REALITY.
Zh: “不可能。你根本无法与现实的荒原抗衡。”

En: YOU WILL NOT. YOU CANNOT WITHSTAND THE WASTE LAND OF REALITY.
Zh: “不可能。你根本无法与现实的荒原抗衡。”
