En: Wait, that means showing everyone that we're taking orders from the Union.
Zh: “等等，那样不是明摆着告诉所有人我们是听命于工会的吗。”

En: Wait, that showing everyone that I'm taking orders from the Union.
Zh: “等等，那样不是明摆着告诉所有人我是听命于工会的吗。”

En: YES. THAT IS PRECISELY WHAT IT MEANS, HOMONCULUS. THIS IS NOT GOING TO HAPPEN ANY OTHER WAY.
Zh: “是的。就是这个意思，小矮子。不然的话就别想了。”

En: Okay then. (Wait here while Measurehead goes.)
Zh: “那好吧。”（测颅先生离开的时候，在这里等着。）

En: No way, we're not going to do that. (Back off.)
Zh: “不，我们不会这么做的。”（后退。）

En: No, I'm not going to do that. (Back off.)
Zh: “不，我不会这么做的。”（后退。）

En: FINE. GOOD-BYE. RETURN TO YOUR DEGENERACY.
Zh: “好吧。再见。继续劣化下去吧。”

En: The woman looks toward the yard. "Jean-Luc must be really tearing it up over there. I wish I could see it..."
Zh: 女人看向院子的方向。“让·卢克一定在那儿大肆破坏呢。真希望能亲眼目睹那场面……”

En: I also wish I could see it.
Zh: “我也希望自己能看见。”

En: I don't. I've seen enough of that dead body already.
Zh: “我可不想看。那具尸体我已经看够了。”

En: Wait-wait, what should I do?
Zh: 等等，我应该怎么做？

En: Don't wait. You've only got a millisecond -- he's coming to, time is breaking loose from the stupor, his left hand curls up into a fist...
Zh: 别等了。你只有千分之一秒的时间——他要攻过来了，时间开始转动，他的左手已经握成拳头……

En: STOP IT. YOU ARE EMBARRASSING YOURSELF IN FRONT OF THIS WOMAN.
Zh: “住手。你这样只会让自己在这个女人面前蒙羞。”

En: We still need to get into the harbour. There is an interview to conduct. There must be another way...
Zh: “我们还是需要进入港口，还有证词需要收集呢。肯定还有其他方法……”

En: We still need to get into the harbour. We need help with the tree situation. There must be another way...
Zh: “我们还是需要进入港口，还需要有人帮忙解决树的问题。肯定还有其他办法……”

En: Try the jump? Up there, on the roof! It'll be worth it. Else there is little choice but to figure out a way to deal with the man...
Zh: 要不试试用跳的？就在那边，屋顶上！这绝对值得一试，否则除了直面那个男人之外就没别的选择了……

En: Try the jump? Up there, on the roof! It'll be worth it. Else there is little choice but to figure out a way to deal with the man...
Zh: 要不试试用跳的？就在那边，屋顶上！这绝对值得一试，否则除了直面那个男人之外就没别的选择了……

En: We still need to get into the harbour. There is an interview to conduct. There must be another way... His gaze wanders over the Whirling's roof to the yard.
Zh: “我们还是需要进入港口，还有证词需要收集呢。肯定还有其他方法……”他的目光开始在飞旋旅店院落四周的屋顶上游荡。

En: We still need to get into the harbour. We need help with the tree situation. There must be another way... His gaze wanders over the Whirling's roof to the yard.
Zh: “我们还是需要进入港口，还需要有人帮忙解决树的问题。肯定还有其他方法……”他的目光开始在飞旋旅店院落四周的屋顶上游荡。

En: Fight.
Zh: 战斗。

En: A shrill laughter interrupts you, echoing across Martinaise. It's Cuno! Then...
Zh: 一阵尖锐的笑声打断了你，在整个马丁内斯回荡。是坤诺！然后……

En: A shrill laughter interrupts you, echoing across Martinaise. Then...
Zh: 一阵尖锐的笑声打断了你，在整个马丁内斯回荡。然后……

En: Yes, yes, keep hiding your insecurities. Whatever, I'll find another way into the harbour. [Leave.]
Zh: “是的，没错，继续掩饰自己的不安全感吧。无论如何我都会找到其他办法进入港口。”[离开。]

En: That's all I wanted.
Zh: “我想要的就是这个。”

En: The spooky pair of blue eyes is peeking in from beyond the gates. The look is very intense, as if trying to burrow into your very soul.
Zh: 那双令人毛骨悚然的蓝眼睛正从大门内部盯着你。那双眼睛用力地瞪着你，仿佛要窥见到你的灵魂深处。

En: A faint, irregular breathing sound resonates through the gap. Almost inaudible, but still there.
Zh: 一阵不规则的微弱呼吸通过缺口发出共鸣，几乎完全听不见，不过的确有。

En: Goddamn! Who's there?
Zh: “该死！谁在那里？”

En: Hello?
Zh: “你好？”

En: The eyes blink rapidly. They seem to be focused on following your every move.
Zh: 眼睛快速地眨巴着，似乎在密切关注着你的一举一动。

En: Hey, I asked you a question.
Zh: “喂，我问你话呢。”

En: Huh, what a freak. [Leave.]
Zh: “哈，神经病。”[离开。]

En: Hello?
Zh: “你好？”

En: The eyes flicker in the darkness beyond the gates.
Zh: 那双眼睛在大门内部的黑暗中忽闪着。

En: I heard you. A muffled voice suddenly clambers through the thick, solid metal gates.
Zh: “我听见你的话了。”一个闷闷的声音蓦地从厚实又牢固的金属大门里传了出来。

En: Not through here, that's for sure.
Zh: “反正肯定不是从这儿过。”

En: And the other eye's on that hulking, blaring leader of the scabs. What a suspicious fucking guy.
Zh: “而另一只眼睛正盯着那个个头又大、声音又吵的工贼领袖呢。这家伙真太他妈可疑了。”

En: The eyes. The eyes! There are eyes everywhere, copper. Beware.
Zh: “我们是眼睛。我们是眼睛！眼睛无处不在，警察。当心。”

En: Welcome to fucking Martinaise, piggy! We've got our eye on you.
Zh: “欢迎来到操蛋的马丁内斯，猪猡！我们盯着你呢。”

En: There are *so many* eyes all over the place, you can't even fathom it. Every hole, every gap, every nook -- someone will be peeking. You can't escape.
Zh: “这个地方有*很多很多*的眼睛，你数都数不清。每一个孔洞、每一道缝隙、每一个角落——都有人在窥视着。你无处可逃。”

En: We saw you point a gun at the little foul-mouthed girl. What kind of an insane bastard move is that? Fuck, what's wrong with you?
Zh: “我们瞧见你用枪指着一个满嘴脏话的小女孩。他妈的这是怎样的丧心病狂啊？操，你有病啊？”

En: We saw you puking *a lot* in the yard. It was hilarious! You call yourself an officer of the law?
Zh: “我们看见你在后院吐了*一地*，笑死我了！你居然还好意思自称是执法人员？”

En: We saw you puke in the back yard, trying to get to the hanged man. Shows what a soft-bellied sissy you are.
Zh: “我们看见你在后院呕吐，还想够着那个吊着的男人。你还真是个软蛋。”

En: God fuck, don't do that, you lunatic.
Zh: “去你妈的，看什么看，你个神经病。”

En: What are you doing?
Zh: “你在做什么？”

En: Who are you?
Zh: “你是谁？”

En: Those are some creepy fucking eyes.
Zh: “那双眼睛可真够瘆人的。”

En: As if you've got better ones, Officer Bloodshot. A muffled voice suddenly clambers through the thick, solid metal gates.
Zh: “说的好像你的眼睛就好得到哪里去了，充血警官。”一个闷闷的声音蓦地从厚实又牢固的金属大门里传了出来。

En: Okay, I'm getting real tired of your shit. I'm out. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，我已经厌倦你这些屁话了。我走了。”[离开。]

En: I don't feel comfortable continuing this discussion. [Leave.]
Zh: “我不是很想继续讨论这个话题。”[离开。]

En: Suit yourself! The eyes gleam in the murky depths beyond the gates.
Zh: “随你便！”眼睛在大门内部的黑暗深处忽闪着。

En: Hang on, how *do* you know who I am and what I do?
Zh: “等一下，你是*怎么*知道我是谁，还有我是做什么的？”

En: How do I get to the other side of these gates?
Zh: “我要怎么才能进到大门的另一边？”

En: Are you some kind of a sex pervert?
Zh: “你是那种性变态吗？”

En: No. And even if I were, I'm not engaged in anything factually illegal. No crime here.
Zh: “并不是。而且就算我是，我也没做过任何事实上违法的事情。清清白白。”

En: Who do you think you are, being disdainful and judgemental about a person's private, idiosyncratic, voyeuristic-erotic preferences anyway? Fuckshit. Goddamn pigs.
Zh: “你以为自己是谁？就算，你有什么权力鄙视和评判别人私人、另类的偷窥癖？杂碎，死猪猡。”

En: Geez, sorry. I was just being curious?
Zh: “老天，抱歉。我这不是好奇嘛。”

En: I make it my responsibility to judge and police the intimate behaviours of complete strangers.
Zh: “评判和监督我完全不认识的人的私密行为是我的义不容辞的责任。”

En: Bi-curious or plain curious?
Zh: “是那种对双性恋的好奇，还是纯好奇？”

En: Believe me, we know. We've got our eyes on you. Can't keep it to yourself, can you? Always creepin' into the lives of others, trying to dominate their actions, even their conversations.
Zh: “相信我，我们早就知道了，我们已经盯上你了很久了。你总是管不住自己，对吧？总是喜欢溜进别人的生活，想要支配他们的行动甚至他们的谈话。”

En: What? I don't understand.
Zh: “什么？我听不懂。”

En: Umm... bi?
Zh: “呃……双性恋吧？”

En: Uhh... plain, maybe?
Zh: “呃……纯的，也许？”

En: Figures. What else is new! Certainly not a surprise when it comes to you nit-wit coppo-goons.
Zh: “我就知道。太阳底下没有新鲜事！你都奇葩成这样了，还有什么事情是你做不出来的吗。”

En: Yeah, I bet you are.
Zh: “我就知道。”

En: That's a bit boring, don't you think?
Zh: “你不觉得吗那样很无聊吗？”

En: Err... okay, I guess.
Zh: “呃……好吧，我猜。”

En: Can you stop being such a jerk?
Zh: “你能别这么混蛋吗？”

En: Fuck you, man, come say that to my face.
Zh: “去你妈的，臭小子，有本事出来当着我的面说啊。”

En: Hum. Well, this went... nowhere. Zero fun and excitement here. I'm getting real bored, how about we change the subject?
Zh: “唔，好吧，这样聊下去……也不会有什么结果。没劲，我真的烦了，不如我们换个话题吧？”

En: If you say so... I haven't really thought about it?
Zh: “如果你都这么说了……我好像一直都没想过这个问题？”

En: Ha ha ha ha! A loud, derisive laugh echoes wildly through the gates.
Zh: “哈哈哈哈！”一阵响亮又嘲弄的笑声穿过大门，疯狂地回响着。

En: Whatever. Jerk.
Zh: “随便你了。混球。”

En: I'm going to get over there and find you, mark my words!
Zh: “我迟早会进门把你揪出来的，说到做到！”

En: Ha ha. Okay, good luck! I'm getting bored now... change the subject, why don't we?
Zh: “哈哈。好吧，祝你好运！现在我觉得有点厌烦了……换个话题吧，不如？”

En: Fair point. Sorry, I haven't been... doing well these past days.
Zh: “说得好。抱歉。我这些天……过得不太好。”

En: You're apologizing to me after *I* called you a bunch of shitty things? Goddamn, now who's the weird one?
Zh: “*我*都用一堆脏话骂过你了，你居然还跟我道歉？老天啊，咱俩到底更奇怪一点？”

En: Maybe you're like a... masochist. Into humiliation and stuff.
Zh: “也许你就是那种……受虐狂吧，喜欢被人羞辱之类的。”

En: Nah... I'm just a sorry sack of a man, a positively shitty cop.
Zh: “不……我只是个可怜虫，一个心态积极的垃圾警察。”

En: Maybe I am? Never thought of that.
Zh: “没准我真是？从没想过这个问题。”

En: Ha... live and learn, lawman. Get out there and blossom into a flower! Go figure things out! Meanwhile, I'm getting bored... how about we change the subject?
Zh: “哈……活到老学到老吧，警官。走出去，变成一朵花然后绽放吧！去了解生活的真谛！话说回来，我有点厌烦了……不如我们换个话题吧？”

En: Do what, look?
Zh: “做什么，看吗？”

En: Yes!
Zh: “是的！”

En: From your perspective... I'm a Mysterious Pair of Eyes, boo!!
Zh: “以你的视角来说……我就是一双神秘的眼睛，轰！！”

En: Know what? Fine... no point in getting riled up by some clown trying to be abusive.
Zh: “你猜怎么着？算了……跟一个骂骂咧咧的小丑生气没任何意义。”

En: Ha ha. Okay, you've got me *cornered* there, good sir officer. Anyway, this is really boring... change the subject, why don't we?
Zh: “哈哈。好吧，好家伙，你把我怼得*无话可说*了。总之，这样聊下去真挺无聊的……换个话题吧，不然？”

En: I don't know if that's completely accurate...
Zh: “我不知道是不是真是这样……”

En: Exactly. That's what being a police officer is all about.
Zh: “没错。警察这个职业就是如此。”

En: *We* who?
Zh: “你说的*我们*又是谁？”

En: That's not funny.
Zh: “这一点都不好笑。”

En: Good joke, man. (Do a slow sarcastic clap.)
Zh: “哈哈哈很好笑。”（缓慢又讽刺地鼓掌。）

En: I meant, who are you *really*?
Zh: “我的意思是，你*到底*是谁？”

En: Ha ha. It was funny enough to me.
Zh: “哈哈，我觉得很好笑。”

En: Good use of sarcasm there, man. You hear a faint clapping sound, brimming with caricature.
Zh: “讽刺运用得很好嘛，哥们儿。”你听见一阵微弱的鼓掌声，充满了夸张的意味。

En: None of your business. Ooh, so spoo-ooky!
Zh: “不关你的事。哦哦哦，吓死人家了！”

En: The gate is broken as shit. Someone absolutely destroyed the access panel in the control tower above.
Zh: “大门早他妈的坏了。肯定有人砸掉了上面控制塔的控制面板。”

En: Why would anyone do that?
Zh: “为什么会有人这么做？”

En: Probably a docker, aiming to block these loud-ass scab fucks from getting in. The guys get panicky sometimes...
Zh: “也许是某个码头工人吧，想把那些吵死人的工贼挡在外面。那些人有时候就容易自乱阵脚……”

En: To keep the strikebreakers out?
Zh: “为了把工贼挡在外面？”

En: Most likely, Mr. *Amazing Investigator*. Aren't you a sharp-ass pencil! Maybe someone should write a novel about you.
Zh: “大概吧，*神奇调查员*先生，你还真敏锐呢！也许该有人给你写本小说。”

En: Are you making fun of me?
Zh: “你这是在取笑我吗？”

En: Of course! Amazing! If a little girl says so, it absolutely should happen!
Zh: “当然！太神奇了！既然那个小女孩都这么说了，那事情就该如此！”

En: Yes! Exactly what the little book girl said earlier.
Zh: “是的！那个书店的小姑娘之前也这么说过。”

En: Yes! Exactly what young Annette said earlier.
Zh: “是的！小安妮特之前也这么说过。”

En: Yeah, someone should.
Zh: “是该有人给我写本书。”

En: Step right up, folks! Read all about the Incredible Adventures of Flop the Wonder-Cop! Able to solve the *most dastardly* of plots and ploys. Ha ha ha.
Zh: “大家伙儿快来啊！快来看衰仔神奇警察不可思议的冒险故事！他是*最最歹毒*的阴谋家的克星。哈哈哈。”

En: Whatever, it's not really my thing anyway.
Zh: “无所谓，我反正对小说什么的没什么兴趣。”

En: Oh no, certainly not! Who would ever dare such a thing.
Zh: “哦，当然不是！谁敢啊。”

En: Not a learned type? Sure is a shocker! The eyes gleam brightly in the darkness of the void.
Zh: “你居然不喜欢读书？这还真是让人震惊啊！”那双眼睛在虚无的黑暗中明亮地忽闪着。

En: I'm a cop, it sure is my business!
Zh: “我是个警察，这当然关我的事！”

En: Secretive much? Hiding something?
Zh: “是什么秘密吗？你在隐瞒什么？”

En: Yes. My identity. Ha ha ha.
Zh: “没错，我在隐瞒自己的身份。哈哈哈。”

En: Indeed... oh yes. We know who you are, we do! Don't you worry about that.
Zh: “确实如此……我们知道你是谁，我们真的知道！这一点你不用担心。”

En: Looking. Peeking. Gazing. Staring. Watching. Ogling. Gawking.
Zh: “直视，窥视，凝视，审视，注视，盯视，呆视。”

En: Spying?
Zh: “还有‘监视’？”

En: Okay, who or what are you looking at?
Zh: “行吧，那你在看什么人，或者什么东西吗？”

En: Nice going, Detective Thesaurus! You do know a few words, ha ha ha! Yeah, *spying* as well.
Zh: “很好，活字典警探！你的词汇量可以啊，哈哈哈！没错，还有*监视*。”

En: Why is he suspicious?
Zh: “他有何可疑之处？”

En: You've got something against honest workers?
Zh: “你对那些老实的工人有意见吗？”

En: Just look at the guy, come on.
Zh: “拜托，光看就知道了啊。”

En: There's something twitching in the back of your mind... a connection hidden between the numbly aching folds of your brain.
Zh: 有什么东西在你的脑海深处抽搐……某条线索就隐藏在你疼得有些麻木的大脑褶皱之间。

En: Honest, my ass. Just look at the guy, geez!
Zh: “老实个屁。天哪，你瞧瞧那家伙！”

En: I dunno...
Zh: “我不知道……”

En: I'll have to... think about it.
Zh: “我需要……想一想。”

En: I mean... maybe, somehow?
Zh: “我想……也许吧，有那么点儿？”

En: Look at you, Super-Constable on his way to solve a suspicious case! Seems like that's going to take a long fucking while, ha ha.
Zh: “看看你，超级警察正准备推理一起疑案！不过他看起来他妈的要花上好一会儿了，哈哈。”

En: No, I meant... don't scare people like that.
Zh: “不，我是说……别这么吓唬人。”

En: Never mind, doesn't matter. (Calm yourself.)
Zh: “没关系，不要紧。”（让自己冷静下来。）

En: We are the eyes, we cannot be closed... if one pair shuts, another takes its place, ha ha! The eyes grow wide and ominous.
Zh: “我们是眼睛，我们永远睁开着……其中一双闭上，其他的就会顶上，哈哈哈！”眼睛突然令人不安地瞪得老大。

En: The mysterious pair of eyes attentively follow your every move.
Zh: 那双神秘的眼睛聚精会神地关注着你的一举一动。

En: The eerie pair of blue eyes is still here, gazing out from the dark void between the gates.
Zh: 那双诡异的蓝眼睛还在那里，透过大门之间的黑暗虚空注视着外面的世界。

En: Boo! The eyes of Martinaise are upon you, piggy!
Zh: “轰！马丁内斯之眼在注视着你，猪猡！”

En: You're the one sticking your nose where it doesn't belong, coppola. The eyes blink at you.
Zh: “你才是那个喜欢多管闲事的人，警官。”眼睛朝你眨了眨。

En: You know very well she did not talk about how someone should write a book about you. *You* are the one who brought the topic up.
Zh: 您心里跟明镜似的知道她并没有说别人应该怎么为您写一本书，*您*才是那个挑起这个话题的人。

En: Fine, if you won't take this seriously, I'm going to ask about something else.
Zh: “好吧，如果你不打算认真回答这个问题的话，我就问点别的好了。”

En: Try not to hurt yourself, old man, ha ha. The pair of eyes beams impishly at you.
Zh: “别闪着腰了，老人家，哈哈哈。”那双眼睛朝你顽皮地眨巴着。

En: Why should I beware?
Zh: “我为什么要当心？”

En: I'm not scared of you.
Zh: “我不怕你。”

En: Go think-man, go. Find some clues, put two and two together. That's how the magic happens, ha ha.
Zh: “去吧，思考者，去吧。找出点线索，然后两两匹配，接下来就是见证奇迹的时刻，哈哈。”

En: I don't really believe you.
Zh: “我真的没法相信你。”

En: Is it now, is it *really*?
Zh: “是吗，*真的*？”

En: You know what you've done, don't start denying it now.
Zh: “你知道自己做了什么，现在可别否认。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Hell yes!
Zh: “当然是！”

En: Well, maybe?
Zh: “呃，也许？”

En: Look at you, little authoritarian. Bet it also makes you feel good and strong to discriminate against any disenfranchised people that get in your way. Makes you feel all *manly*.
Zh: “看看你，活脱脱一个小威权主义者。我敢打赌，你肯定很喜欢歧视弱势群体，这会让你觉得自己特*男人*。”

En: I sincerely doubt it. You're supposed to help people and handle actual crime. Not walk around like an oppressive tyrant, passing punishments however you feel.
Zh: “我由衷地表示怀疑。你应该去服务人民，处理真的犯罪行为，而不是像个暴君一样到处溜达，看谁不爽就抓谁。”

En: It's easy and comfortable to side with the ignorant, the arrogant, the condescending, isn't it? Hide away in the soft, secure existence of perceived superiority, a vapid fantasy of your mind.
Zh: “站在那些或愚昧，或自大，或居高临下的人一边其实要更容易、也更舒服一些，不是吗？就这样躲藏在柔软又安全的优越感和你自己那索然无味的幻想里。”

En: Well, we know what you're up to. We observe your every move. Take care and watch your back. The eyes suddenly disappear from sight, only emptiness remains, floating in the lacuna between the gates.
Zh: “好吧，我们知道你想做什么。我们观察着你的一举一动。保重，小心你身后。”眼睛突然消失不见了，只留下一片漂浮在大门缝隙间的虚无。

En: In the shadowy depths of the gap between the gates... there is nothing. Not even a single pair of blue eyes, vigilantly observing your every move. Whoever it was, he is gone.
Zh: 在大门缝隙间的幽深之中……空无一物，就连一双警惕地盯着你一举一动的蓝眼睛也没有。不管那到底是谁，他已经不见了。

En: Oh. I don't do that... I think. As far as I remember.
Zh: “哦，我不会这么做……我觉得。至少在我记得是这样的。”

En: But what if they *really* deserved it?
Zh: “但是如果他们*真的*活该呢？”

En: Isn't that what the judicial system is all about? The rules of law are developed and set for a reason. Well, this turned boring quickly... it's time to change the subject.
Zh: “司法制度不就是为此而存在的吗？法制之所以得以确立是有原因的。唉，话题怎么又无聊起来了……是时候换个话题了。”

En: Aren't we the lucky ones then? But I'm getting *real* bored with this... maybe it's time to change the subject.
Zh: “咱们看来真的还挺走运的。不过这事我*真的*说烦了……也许是时候该换个话题了。”

En: Rotten luck. I guess I'll find another way.
Zh: “真倒霉。我还是找别的路进去吧。”

En: Yeah? I got a suggestion, you should go try knock the guard above us out. Ha ha, that would be fuckin' priceless.
Zh: “哦？我有个建议，你可以去把我们上方的那个守卫打晕。哈哈，那样就真他妈的有意思了。”

En: I don't actually remember much of what I've done.
Zh: “其实我以前到底做过什么我自己都不怎么记得了。”

En: I haven't done anything!
Zh: “我没有！”

En: A rallying cry for coppers everywhere! *We haven't done anything, we are never of any use*, ha ha.
Zh: “这是所有警察的集结口号！*我们没有，我们没用*，哈哈。”

En: A serious bout of denial? Or perhaps the result of the incessant stream of alcohol you've been pouring down your throat?
Zh: “怎么一本正经地否认起来了？还是说你又喝多了？”

En: Denial...? I don't know.
Zh: “否认……？我不知道。”

En: It's the booze, definitely. For certain. No doubt.
Zh: “绝对是酒精的作用。我敢肯定，错不了的。”

En: Both, maybe?
Zh: “两者都有吧，大概？”

En: O-ho! I wonder what *harrowing mystery* lies behind this feat of self-destruction.
Zh: “哎呀！我想知道如此壮烈的自毁行为背后到底隐藏着怎样*痛苦的秘密*。”

En: An intriguing mystery! I wonder what *terrible secrets* lie behind this dark and ominous veil, ha ha.
Zh: “一个有趣的谜题！我想知道这张黑暗和不祥的面纱下面到底隐藏着什么*可怕的秘密*，哈哈。”

En: Your life sounds like an immeasurable clusterfuck. Something's gone *really wrong*.
Zh: “你的生活听上去就是一场巨大的闹剧。有哪儿真的出了*很严重的问题*。”

En: I don't actually want to talk about my personal life with a stupid voice behind a wall.
Zh: “我并不想跟墙壁背后的某个愚蠢的声音讨论自己的个人生活。”

En: Think I don't know that?
Zh: “你以为我不知道吗？”

En: A good thing, too! I am not one of your fatuous Königstein neurosis analyst fucks, cashing in on the anxieties of nice ordinary folk. So, what now, Mr. Sensational Sleuth, sir?
Zh: “这也未尝不是件好事！反正我又不是你的混蛋柯尼希斯神经分析师，得靠善良老百姓的焦虑赚钱。所以现在怎么说，耸人听闻侦探先生？”

En: Mm, the delights of existence. A constant anguish and concern over horrible things you don't even know about, but can *imagine* so vividly. Ha ha.
Zh: “嗯，这就是存在的喜悦，一种时常因为某些你自己完全不了解、但在*想象*中却无比生动的事情而产生的苦恼和担忧。哈哈。”

En: What do you have against cops?
Zh: “你对警察是有什么意见吗？”

En: Oh whatever, I'm not going to give you the satisfaction.
Zh: “无所谓了，我才不要正中你的下怀。”

En: Aw, shucks! And here I was, getting ever so hopeful. Does the ardent cop-man want to ask *more questions* then?
Zh: “哎呀，不妙！我居然还满怀希望地在这儿等着。这位热情的警官还有*别的问题*想问吗？”

En: Nothing, sir, oh nothing at all! Please don't arrest me, I'm just a poor, honest man, trying not to make a mistake. Ha ha! The sarcastic laughter effortlessly makes it's way through the thick gates. There's no stopping it.
Zh: “没有，阿sir，没有意见！请不要逮捕我，我只是一个可怜的老实人，一直低着头做人。哈哈！”嘲讽的笑声轻易就穿过了厚厚的大门，没有遮拦。

En: Until doom comes and liberates us from all our woes.
Zh: “直到末日降临，赦免我们所有的悲痛。”

En: Okay, enough already, this is making me feel even worse.
Zh: “好吧，已经够了，这让我感觉更糟糕了。”

En: You've really fallen off the rocker, haven't you? This is getting boring, it's time to change the subject.
Zh: “你的脑子是真的坏脱了吧？这个话题越来越无聊了，换个话题吧。”

En: Good plan, I was kinda getting bored of this topic already. What's next on the menu, Inspector Repression?
Zh: “好主意，我已经有点厌倦这个话题了。清单上的下一项是什么，压迫警官？”

En: There's no way you could *fit* inside all those nooks and gaps.
Zh: “你是不可能*钻进*所有犄角旮旯的。”

En: Prove it, creep.
Zh: “证明给我看啊，怪胎。”

En: We saw you kick the mailbox and the coin-operated viewer. Are you some kind of a maniac with anger management issues?
Zh: “我们看见你踹了邮箱还有投币望远镜。你是不是那种有情绪管控问题的神经病？”

En: We saw you kick the coin-operated viewer. Bit of a streak of violence when it comes to inanimate objects, eh?
Zh: “我们看见你踹了投币望远镜。你对待没有生命的物体是不是太暴力了点？”

En: We saw you kick the mailbox. That's kind of a jerk thing to do, you know.
Zh: “我们看见你踹了邮箱。这种行为还挺混蛋的。”

En: We've had our eye on you for some time now. And I can tell you straight... goddamn, you're one *boring fuckin' cop*!
Zh: “我们已经注意你一段时间了。我直接跟你说了吧……妈的，你作为一个警察简直他娘的*没劲疯了*！”

En: Yeah, you piss off, you self-righteous, lecturing ass-goblin!
Zh: “哈哈，气到你了吧，你这个自以为是、成天说教的狗屁玩意儿！”

En: Hey, come back! Where are you going?
Zh: “喂，回来！你要去哪儿？”

En: There is no reply. Only silence lurks in the cavernous depths of the darkness beyond.
Zh: 没有回应，惟有沉默还潜伏在门内部黑暗虚空巨穴般的深处。

En: Try not to get too cocky, you might get that eye poked out.
Zh: “别太得意了，小心眼珠子被人抠出来。”

En: Umm, what? That's not... *literal* -- there are not people actually stuffed into various tiny holes. You get that, right?
Zh: “呃，你说什么？我说的不是……*字面上的意思*——没有人真的能把自己塞进各种各样的小洞里。这点你应该明白的，对吧？”

En: We've had our eye on you for quite some time. We saw you stumble out of the hostel, all messed up and stinking of booze.
Zh: “我们已经注意你一段时间了。我们看见你踉踉跄跄的从一家旅社里走出来，整个人衣衫不整，酒气冲天。”

En: That's it?
Zh: “就这样？”

En: Ooh, so *spooky*. Anyone could have seen this.
Zh: “哇哦，好*瘆人*。这个场景当时又不是没其他人看到。”

En: Okay, a bit suspicious, but still not particularly menacing.
Zh: “好吧，是有点可疑，不过还不是特别有说服力。”

En: Holy shit! A disembodied voice! Have cursed void-phantasms come to haunt us for misdeeds done in the past?
Zh: 我的妈呀！一个空洞的声音！咱们是因为过去的罪行被虚空中的幽灵给缠上了吗？

En: Oh. Of course I get it.
Zh: “哦，我当然明白了。”

En: That's way more boring than I imagined.
Zh: “这也比我想象中的无聊太多了。”

En: How can they fit into all the holes and gaps? Are they very tiny people? Or are they... eyes, separated from the body?
Zh: 他们是怎么钻进各种空洞和缝隙的？他们莫非是袖珍小人？或者就是……没有身体的眼睛？

En: Right. Good. You get it. We are watching you, ever vigilant. Nothing you do escapes our vision.
Zh: “好的，很好，你明白就行。我们随时都在警惕地盯着你呢，你的一举一动都逃不出我们的视线。”

En: Boring?! That you are always being watched and judged? Nothing you do escapes our grasp, we are ever vigilant.
Zh: “无聊？！你一直被人盯着、被人评判这件事很无聊？你做的一切都在我们的掌握之中，时时刻刻。”

En: Watch all you want, I don't mind.
Zh: “想看就看吧，我不在乎。”

En: Ha ha, you won't take us seriously? You will yet -- and you will rue that day.
Zh: “哈哈，你不把我们当回事，是吧？不过你迟早会的——而且那一天到来的时候，你会后悔的。”

En: Damn straight.
Zh: “还真他妈够直接的。”

En: Just doing my job.
Zh: “只是在履行职责罢了。”

En: Think you're some kind of a nice, clean, up-standing citizen? Don't worry, we'll get you yet... you can't keep your nose out of the gutter forever.
Zh: “你真以为自己是什么善良、体面、正直的好市民？别担心，我们会抓到你的……狗是改不了吃屎的。”

En: It's a beginning, copper! You consider yourself a clean-cut, up-standing citizen? Don't worry, we'll get you yet... no keeping your nose out of the gutter forever.
Zh: “这只是个开始，警官！你以为自己是个体面正直的好市民？别担心，我们会抓到你的……狗是改不了吃屎的。”

En: She is a demon begging for release from this mortal coil.
Zh: “她是一个恶魔，祈求着有人能帮她从尘世的烦扰中解脱出来。”

En: No such thing. I deny everything.
Zh: “没有的事，我否认所有的指控。”

En: It was just a joke, come on. I was teaching her a lesson.
Zh: “拜托，我开玩笑的，我当时只是在传授她一些人生经验。”

En: No, no. That's... like... a super fucked-up thing to do. Seriously, I can't believe you still have your job. Everything else pales compared to this shit...
Zh: “不，不是。那是……怎么说……超级操蛋的一件事。说真的，我都不敢相信你居然还没丢饭碗。跟这件破事相比，其他事都一下子相形见绌了……”

En: And we saw you got the body down. Oh, such wonderful progress! Hold the applause, ladies and gents.
Zh: “而且我们还看见你把尸体放下来了。哦，过程可太精彩了！准备好鼓掌吧，女士们，先生们。”

En: And we saw your attempt to shoot the dead guy down from the tree. That was an... exceptionally impressive failure, if I say so myself.
Zh: “我们看见你想用枪射断绳索把尸体从树上弄下来。要我说的话，那还真是一次……让人印象特别深刻的失败呢。”

En: Whoah, I retract my statement.
Zh: “哇哦，我收回我的话。”

En: Getting too hot for you? Ha ha! Yeah, skitter off, find some actual criminals.
Zh: “已经受不了了？哈哈！行吧，快走吧，找几个真正的罪犯去。”

En: Holy fuck! It's coming to get us!
Zh: 我操！它来抓咱们来了！

En: Don't be ridiculous. It's just a regular person, probably a worker from the harbour.
Zh: 别逗了，这就是个普通人，可能是某个码头工人吧。

En: Right now? You! Ha ha. We've got our eyes on you, believe you me!
Zh: “现在吗？我们在看你啊！哈哈。我们已经盯上你了，真的！”

En: Alright, you want more? We saw you punch a kid. A grown man, a *police officer* punched a wee snot-nosed punk kid. In the face! Do you understand how fucked up that is, you maniac?
Zh: “好吧，你还想听？我们还看见你出手揍了那个小孩。一个成年人，一个*警察*，居然出手揍了一个拖着鼻涕的小破孩！你知道这事有多他妈的严重吗，你这个疯子！”

En: Alright, you want more? We saw you try to punch that kid. A grown man, a *police officer* trying to punch a snot-nosed punk kid! A kid, for fuck's sake!
Zh: “好吧，你还想听？我们还看见你想出手揍那个小孩。一个成年人，一个*警察*，居然想要揍一个拖着鼻涕的小破孩！真他妈可以的，那是个孩子啊！”

En: He fuckin' deserved it.
Zh: “这他妈的是他自找的。”

En: It was a lesson in *respect*.
Zh: “我这是在教育他怎么去*尊重*他人。”

En: Sorry, things just got out of... hand.
Zh: “抱歉，当时情况有些……失控。”

En: Oh, sure, think up any justification you can for your fucked up, vicious deeds. Seriously, what a psychopath. You can't cover that up...
Zh: “哦，当然了，你可以为自己那些操蛋的恶行找到任何借口。说真的，你他妈就是个神经病，这一点你可骗不了我……”

En: Yeah? Does that happen to you often, Officer Heavyhands? Seriously, what a psychopath. You can't cover that up...
Zh: “哦？这事是不是对你来说就是家常便饭啊，下重手的警官？说真的，你他妈就是个神经病，这一点你可骗不了我……”

En: Are you the company lookout Joyce hinted at?
Zh: “你是乔暗示的那个公司派出的暗探吗？”

En: Wouldn't you like to know, fuckstick!
Zh: “你不想知道吗，猪头！”

En: Yes, I would like to. Very much.
Zh: “我很想知道。非常想。”

En: Can you please not call me names?
Zh: “你能别再给我瞎起名字了吗？”

En: Sure thing, pisspig, ha ha ha. The eyes blink rapidly. "Oh, and no, I'm not some fuckin' 'lookout' for the *leeches*."
Zh: “当然可以，猪猡，哈哈哈。”那双眼睛飞快地眨巴着。“哦，还有，不是的，我不是那些*吸血鬼*的什么鬼暗探。”

En: The eyes blink rapidly. "No, I'm not, ha ha ha."
Zh: 那双眼睛飞快地眨巴着。“不，我不是，哈哈哈。”

En: Oh-oh, you know who's got things to hide!?
Zh: 哦吼，你现在知道是谁在隐瞒了吗！？

En: Don't be an idiot and say it. In this day and age, of all times. It won't end well.
Zh: 别犯傻了，说吧。就在今天，就在这个时代，都不会有好结果的。

En: It's gotta be something *big* and *deep* and *important*. Because certainly you aren't some regular asshole avoiding the fact that he goes around fucking up everyone's lives with their self-serving, egotistical arrogance?
Zh: “那一定是某件很*大*、很*深入*、而且*很重要*的事情，因为你肯定不是个普通的混球，明明一直以一种自我为中心的傲慢态度到处搅乱所有人的生活，却还避不承认这一事实。”

En: I'm sure your story is very *unique* and not a stereotype-ridden re-hash of tired ideas.
Zh: “我肯定你的人生故事很*特别*，完全没有那些充满刻板印象、旧瓶装新酒的无聊桥段。”

En: You don't know me.
Zh: “你对我一无所知。”

En: Sure thing, you pretty flower, you. Go and discover your *exciting* backstory. I'm sure it'll be one-of-a-kind.
Zh: “当然了，你这朵娇艳的花朵。去发现你自己*刺激*的背景故事吧，我敢肯定它一定是独一无二的。”

En: Is this one of those homo things that keeps coming up?
Zh: “是那种不断冒出来的同性恋之类的东西吗？”

En: Ah, but of course! It's all a huge part of the secret homo-sexual underground.
Zh: “啊，当然是了！这可是地下同性恋组织非常重大的一部分。”

En: Really?!
Zh: “真的吗？！”

En: I'm still not sure that's an actual thing...
Zh: “我仍然无法确定那是真实存在的东西……”

En: Of course it's not, you numbnuts! A groan comes from the other side. "Unbelievable."
Zh: “当然不是了，你个榆木脑袋！”另一边传来一声抱怨。“简直难以置信。”

En: No. A groan comes from the other side. "You're a complete tool, aren't you."
Zh: “不是。”对面传来一声抱怨。“你完全就是个工具人啊，不是吗。”

En: You can fucking say that again. God, yes, the power!
Zh: “你他妈有种再说一次。没错，力量！”

En: Oh yeah, him. He's a mercenary posing as a worker, yeah.
Zh: “哦，那个人啊。他是个假扮成工人的雇佣兵。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: What, did your brain shut off or something? He grunts. "I don't understand how you get anything done. Well, whatever."
Zh: “怎么了，你脑子是宕机了还是什么？”他咕哝着。“我不明白你之前都是怎么做事的。唉，无所谓了。”

En: That so? Well I'll be damned... He snorts. "Guess you ain't all shit-for-brains after all."
Zh: “就这样？真见鬼……”他吸了吸鼻子。“我猜你也不完全是个笨蛋吧。”

En: What's a *scab* mean?
Zh: “*工贼*是什么意思？”

En: Why won't they let us in?
Zh: “他们为什么不放我们进去？”

En: Without work I am nothing!
Zh: “没了工作，我什么都不是！”

En: I haven't had a job for years!
Zh: “我都失业好几年了！”

En: Did you hear that? They're secretly working in there!
Zh: “你听见了吗？他们在里面偷偷工作呢！”

En: Fuck, I'm already tired...
Zh: “他妈的，我已经累了……”

En: We had a deal we can work here!
Zh: “我们说好了可以在这里工作的！”

En: Look, look here! I signed a six month contract!
Zh: “看，看这里！我签过一份六个月工期的合同！”

En: You're not débardeurs -- you're criminals!
Zh: “你们不是码头工人——你们是罪犯！”

Actor: Very Hard to Exploit Handicapped Person
En: We're workers, just like you! Let us in!
Zh: “我们是工人，跟你们一样！让我们进去！”

Actor: Very Hard to Exploit Handicapped Person
En: We're all working stiffs here! You're fuckin' 'yer own!
Zh: “我们都是埋头苦干的人！你们他妈的只代表*你们自己*！

Actor: Very Hard to Exploit Handicapped Person
En: You're just afraid of work yourselves!
Zh: “你们只是害怕工作！”

En: Let us through!
Zh: “让我们过去！”

En: Right to work! Right to work!
Zh: “工作权！工作权！”

En: C'mon guys, I *need* this!
Zh: “拜托，伙计们，我*需要*上班！”

En: You're just afraid, cause we're better than you!
Zh: “你们只是害怕，因为我们比你们强！”

En: Fuck politics! Let's just all work! Together!
Zh: “去他妈的政治！我们工作吧！一起工作！”

En: I will fight any one of you to work. One on one, let's go!
Zh: “只要能工作，我可以跟你们任何一个人干一架。一对一，来啊！”

En: You don't even know what work means!
Zh: “你们甚至不知道什么叫工作！”

En: Stop fuckin' ruining it for everybody!
Zh: “别他妈坑了所有人！”

En: We *just* wanna work!
Zh: “我们*只是*想要上班！”

En: Shit or get off the crapper, assholes!
Zh: “别占着茅坑不拉屎啊，混账们！”

En: I came all the way from Olduwai to work here!
Zh: “我可是大老远从奥德瓦赶过来的！”

En: I'll work for two!
Zh: “我愿意做两份工！”

En: LET US IN!
Zh: “让我们进去！”

En: We wanna work!
Zh: “我们要上工！”

En: Work is a basic human right!
Zh: “工作是基本的人权！”

En: Honest work, honest pay!
Zh: “老实工作，老实给钱！”

En: We can all work together!
Zh: “我们可以一起工作！”

En: WOORK WOORK WOORK!
Zh: “工作，工作，工作！”

En: Unions are a scam!
Zh: “工会就是个骗局！”

En: C'mon, guys! I gotta feed my kids!
Zh: “拜托，伙计们！我还有孩子要养活呢！”

En: You're fucking with the wrong worker here!
Zh: “你们他妈的惹错人了！”

En: Just let me work a little, I'm really good!
Zh: “让我稍微工作一下就行，我技术很好的！”

En: I'd leave, but I don't wanna get beaten up...
Zh: “我想离开，但我不想挨揍……”

En: I need money!
Zh: “我需要钱！”

En: I'm in debt!
Zh: “我还欠着债呢！”

En: I wouldn't do this if I didn't have to!
Zh: “如果不是走投无路的话，我干嘛要来这里！”

En: I spent all my money getting here!
Zh: “为了来这里，我把所有的积蓄都花光了！”

En: C'mon, there's plenty of work for everyone!
Zh: “拜托，工作很多的，足够分给我们所有人！”

En: I was promised a place.
Zh: “你们答应给我一个位置的。”

En: Right to work! It's our right!
Zh: “工作权！这是我们的权利！”

En: You have no idea how bad I need this!
Zh: “你们不知道我们到底有多需要工作！”

En: Let me work! I'm gonna start drinking if I don't!
Zh: “让我工作！不然我就去喝酒了！”

En: I thought it'd be over by now.
Zh: “我还以为早就结束了。”

En: Let's just sit down and talk this out!
Zh: “我们都坐下来，好好聊一聊！”

En: Seems like it ain't getting any better...
Zh: “好像情况也没有好转……”

En: I even brought my own overalls!
Zh: “我连自己的工作服都带来了！”

En: I have a family!
Zh: “我要养家！”

En: We're all so tired out here, have mercy!
Zh: “我们都很累了，可怜一下我们吧！”

En: I'm not going home!
Zh: “我不会回家的！”

En: Why don't you fucking come down here!
Zh: “你他妈的有种就下来！”

En: Just be reasonable!
Zh: “讲点道理好不好！”

En: You think you're better than us?! You're not!!!
Zh: “你觉得自己比我们强？！并没有！！！”

En: You can't win this, just let us work!
Zh: “你们赢不了的，让我们上班吧！”

En: We were promised work!
Zh: “兑现承诺，我们要工作！”

En: I'll work harder than *anyone*!
Zh: “我会比*任何人*都更努力工作！”

En: You're taking food out of our mouths!
Zh: “你们这是想让我们饿死！”

En: Please, I'll come on half-time, work night-shifts!
Zh: “拜托，我可以半工半薪，还能上夜班！”

En: Let us through!
Zh: “让我们过去！”

En: Unions are a scam!
Zh: “工会就是个骗局！”

En: How 'bout some WORK?
Zh: “不如上班吧？”

En: Should've stayed in the damn sawmill...
Zh: “早知道就留在那个该死的锯木厂不走了……”

En: Right to work! Right to work!
Zh: “工作权！工作权！”

En: Wait, you can't get by Measurehead? But *I* got by Measurehead. I kicked him in the face.
Zh: “等等，你过不去测颅先生这关？但是*我*就过去了，我直接一脚踹他脸上了。”

En: Not bad advice, actually. This might do the trick..
Zh: 这个建议其实还不错，也许能奏效……

En: You don't have any tips on how to punch a guy out, do you? Say, a really big race-theorist guarding a button?
Zh: “你能在打人的方面给点小建议吗？比如某个看守按钮的又特别魁梧的种族主义理论家，怎样才能把这种人打趴下？”

En: He smirks. "Not before you get in there and get your ass whooped. Learn by failure, I always say."
Zh: 他幸灾乐祸地笑了。“在你过去然后被揍个屁滚尿流之前，我是不会说的。我一直都说，人要从失败中学习。”

En: He might have some advice, but you gotta at least *try* to fight Measurehead first. Return if you fail.
Zh: 他也许能为你提些建议，但你至少要先去跟测颅先生打一架*试试*，赢不了的话再回来。

En: What about it? You're 'right to work'?
Zh: “怎么样？你‘准备好工作’了吗？”

En: Wrong. You did the right thing with Measurehead. The ballerina antics won you the fight.
Zh: 才不是这样。你和测颅先生战斗时作出的选择是正确的，杂耍般的舞蹈动作让你赢得了战斗。

En: Yeah, I saw your ballerina moves up there. The man spits out a yellow clot. "In a real fight these acrobatics will get you killed faster than a bullet."
Zh: “我看见你在上面的舞蹈动作了。”男人吐出一口黄色的浓痰。“在真正的战斗中，这些花拳绣腿会让你死得比挨枪子还快。”

En: You caught him off guard -- that only happens once, even if you got him twice or whatever. Now he's alert, standing on a narrow bridge and *my* men are tired and hungry...
Zh: “你打了他个措手不及——不过这种情形只会发生一次，就算你再去找他也不会再有了。现在他十分警觉，还守在一条狭窄的桥上，而*我的*人全都又累又饿……”

En: You caught him off guard -- that only happens once. Now he's alert, standing on a narrow bridge and *my* men are tired and hungry...
Zh: “你打了他个措手不及——不过这种情形只会发生一次。现在他十分警觉，还守在一条狭窄的桥上，而*我的*人全都又累又饿……”

En: He seems to be following his orders well enough for now -- but beneath it all there is a boiling rage. And a dangerous carelessness.
Zh: 目前为止，他表面上还算是很好地执行了自己的命令——但是在平静的外表之下却是正在升腾的怒火以及致命的粗心大意。

En: Do not be fooled by the programming. There is a precarious balance inside him, keeping him in check. But it's shaky as hell. And he's tired of it all...
Zh: 别被他的设计愚弄了。他的内心有着一架微妙的天平在控制着他的情绪。但是现在这架天平却在剧烈的摇晃着，更何况他已经厌倦这一切了……

En: Basteeerds! We have a RIGHT TO WORK! the man yells towards the harbour gates. His voice is the loudest of the lot -- and oddly screechy for a man his size.
Zh: “混蛋！我们有权工作！”男人朝着港口大门呼喊着。他的声音是最大的一个——对于他这种体型的男人来说，尖锐的有点诡异了。

En: What's going on here?
Zh: “这里出什么事了？”

En: Beats me. They mumble nonsense about *board rooms* and *workers' rights*. While we -- he raises his fist and starts shouting again, "-- HAVE THE RIGHT TO WORK!"
Zh: “我不知道。他们叨叨着一些什么*拓宽厂房*还有*工人权益*之类的鬼话。我们——”他扬起拳头，又开始大叫起来，“——有权工作！”

En: Have fun. He snorts. "Union shits are on full strike. Don't think they're going to let you through the gates. Why you want to meet that fat asshole?"
Zh: “玩得开心。”他哼了一声。“工会正在大罢工。依我看那些人是不会放你进那扇门的。你是想见那个死鬼胖子吗？”

En: Have fun. He snorts. "Union shits are on full strike. Don't think they're going to let you through the gates. You trying to meet their fat boss?"
Zh: “祝你玩得开心。”他哼了一声。“工会正在大罢工。依我看那些人是不会放你进那扇门的。你是想见那个胖子老大吗？”

En: I'm interviewing people about a murder that took place here. Behind the hostel-cafeteria there.
Zh: “这里发生了一起凶杀案，就在那边的旅社餐厅后面。我在寻访知情者。”

En: It would be better -- for the neighbourhood -- if you went home. At least for now. If you can't get in anyway.
Zh: “为街坊邻居着想，你们最好回家去，至少现在如此，反正你也进不去。”

En: Wait. They might go home eventually. Or get drunk. Leave the access panel unguarded. Or the company sends someone to reason with them...
Zh: “等等，那些人最后也是要回家的，要不然就会喝个烂醉，到时候这样大门开关就没人看着了。或者公司会派人去说服他们……”

En: Unexpected situation. But we hold the pressure on, until someone makes a mistake. Then it's our time, we strike. The man rubs his jaw -- a lightly bearded square wedge.
Zh: “这是之前没有预料到的情况，但是我们一直在施压，只要他们中间有人犯了一个错误，我们就会立刻出手。”男人摩挲着下巴——

En: We're all workers. Right? Workers stick together. Came from the Eminent Domain, in Jamrock. Background's in odd-jobs -- heavy lifting, cargo hauling, bouncer work. I know the drill.
Zh: “我们都是工人，是吧？工人就要团结一心。我来自加姆洛克的艾米涅特区。就是打打零工——重物搬运，货物拖运，酒吧保镖。我明白这一套的。”

En: A bouncer? Where? I frequent a lot of bars, maybe it's one I know.
Zh: “酒吧保镖？哪里的保镖？我是很多酒吧的常客，说不定我知道是哪里。”

En: Worked at... *Territorial*. Ring a bell?
Zh: “是……*领域酒吧*的保镖。有印象吗？”

En: Yeah, I think I've been there.
Zh: “有点印象，我记得我好像去过。”

En: Not right now, but I might have been there. I don't really remember things well.
Zh: “现在想不太起来，但我有可能去过。我记性不太好。”

En: Never heard of it. Are you lying?
Zh: “从没听说过。是你瞎编出来的吗？”

En: Now that *is* interesting.
Zh: 现在事情*有趣*起来了。

En: I'm just going to leave now. [Leave.]
Zh: “我还是走吧。”[离开。]

En: A monstrous shadow, high above the firetraps of the Domain -- the 8/81 motorway running over this district of Jamrock. Concrete pillars rise up from the midst of the dilapidated wooden houses. On the horizon, barely visible, the hazy machinery of the harbour...
Zh: 一个巨兽般的阴影高悬于孤岛般的艾米涅特区之上——那是横贯加姆洛克的8/81高速公路高架桥。混凝土立柱从破败的木屋间拔地而起，地平线上港口机械的模糊轮廓依稀可见……

En: Every once in a while, it is like you can see glimpses of another guy under the guise of this *fighter for jobs*. He seems a more brutal, cunning and suspicious person. Just a hunch. Or... you just might be paranoid!
Zh: 你时不时的能感觉到人群中另一个伪装成*为工作而抗争者*的目光，这个人感觉上要更为粗暴、更为狡黠、更为可疑。这出于一种直觉，要不然……这纯属你的妄想！

En: Something stirs in your stomach.
Zh: 有什么东西在你的肚中翻搅。

En: Good. We're fighting for a cause here. He chants at the gates: "RIGHT TO WORK! RIGHT TO WORK!"
Zh: “很好。我们为了一个使命才在这里抗争。”他朝着大门喊着口号，“工作权！工作权！”

En: Hold up and stay frosty, everyone! Cops are here. The broad-shouldered alpha male turns to you. He's a full head taller than everybody else here.
Zh: “大伙注意啦，保持冷静！警察来了。”宽肩男领袖转向你。他比这里所有人都要高一头。

En: Boots -- black in colour, crafted of hardened leather, if you had to guess. They look sturdy and stable, useful in rugged environments.
Zh: 一双靴子——黑色，如果要猜的话，应该是硬皮材质的。这双靴子看起来结实又耐用，适于恶劣环境。

En: Why should I?
Zh: “我为什么要这么做？”

En: I am the law.
Zh: “我就是法律。”

En: What do the strikers want?
Zh: “罢工者想要什么？”

En: What is a *strike*?
Zh: “*罢工*是什么？”

En: What exactly is your goal here?
Zh: “那你的目标到底是什么？”

En: RIGHT TO WORK! He again shakes his large fist, then turns back to you. "It's shameful, cops doing nothing. You should bring backup, open the gates for us. Blockading gainful employment for workers is a *crime*."
Zh: “工作权！”他再次挥舞起巨大的拳头，然后转向你。“真丢脸，你们这些警察毫无作为。你应该呼叫增援帮我们把门打开。阻止工人返工挣钱就是*犯罪*。”

En: We have families to feed, you piece of shit! He points his finger at the man sitting on the railing.
Zh: “我们还有家要养啊，你这个混帐！”他指向坐在栏杆上的男人。”

En: So do we, scab! Mañana hollers in return.
Zh: “我们也有家要养啊，工贼！”马列拉大声回应到。

En: So do we, scab! the loitering man hollers in return.
Zh: “我们也有家要养啊，工贼！”吊儿郎当的男人大声回应道。

En: His manner of speaking is wooden, the tone of voice bland and uninspired. Almost as if compiling replies from a set of learned phrases.
Zh: 他说话的方式很呆板，语调乏味又平淡，说出来的句子简直就像是从一组背过的短语列表中挑出词组排列组合出来的。

En: Okay, I gotta ask. Where exactly are you from?
Zh: “好吧，那我要问了。你到底是打哪里来的？”

En: Honest men and women. With rights -- to work. To be useful. Not toys for corporate interests. The man runs a hand through his steadily graying military haircut.
Zh: “老实的男人和女人们，他们有工作的权利，想作出自己的贡献，而不是成为企业利益的玩具。”男人用手理了理自己头上已经斑白的短发。

En: Who are all these strikebreakers? (Look around.)
Zh: “这些反罢工者都是些什么人？”（看看周围。）

En: The mention of a killing sends a barely noticeable shiver of tenseness through him. Interesting...
Zh: 一提到凶杀案，他以常人难以觉察到的幅度紧张地打了一个激灵。有意思……

En: We came here to help the harbour run smoothly in time of crisis. If Union fucks don't want work, they ought let in those *WHO DO WANT WORK*.
Zh: “我们来这里是为了帮助港口度过危机。就算工会那帮混账不想工作，他们也应该让那些*想工作的人*进去。”

En: Everyone understands you're *the law*. That's why you have that insignia on your arm. So you don't have to keep saying it.
Zh: “是个人都看得出来你就是*法律*，你胳膊上戴着警徽不就是为了省去重复这句话的麻烦嘛。”

En: When a bunch of ungrateful, lazy cockroaches can't get their act together. Decide to block honest work for other people. He shifts uncomfortably in his worker's overalls.
Zh: “一群吃里扒外、懒懒散散的蟑螂自己过不好就算了，还想拉其他老实人垫背不让别人工作，这就叫罢工。”他的身体在工作服里不太自在地扭动着。

En: There's something odd in the way he carries himself. His set of clothing looks vaguely mismatched. The different pieces of the attire seem ill-fitting.
Zh: 他身上有什么地方怪怪的。他的衣着看起来隐约有种不协调感，衣服裤子好像都不太合身。

En: Yes, people get that you are *the law*. You really don't have to keep saying it.
Zh: “是啊，大家都知道你就是*法律*。你真的不用一直说个不停。”

En: Looks like too much trouble for my taste. [Leave.]
Zh: “看起来太麻烦了，不合我的胃口。”[离开。]

En: We're here to fight for a cause. Stripes usually have problems with people who have *causes*.
Zh: “我们是为了一个使命才来这里抗争的。当差的一般都看不惯那些怀抱*使命*的人。”

En: What kind of a cause are we talking about?
Zh: “我们在这里说的使命是什么呢？”

En: Rights of people. Rights of workers. To have gainful employment. To make a salary and feed their families.
Zh: “人民的权利。工人的权利。保住有收入的工作。继续赚钱养家。”

En: RIGHT TO WORK! RIGHT TO WORK! the ostensible leader of the strikebreakers chants furiously. "SHAME ON YOU!"
Zh: “工作权！工作权！”这个伪装出来的反罢工者领袖怒吼着，“不要脸！”

En: I don't *think* I've chosen any sides yet.
Zh: “我*觉得*我还没选好要站哪边。”

En: Oh, then yes.
Zh: “哦，那就是吧。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Okay, then I'm thinking no.
Zh: “好吧，那我就说不是好了。”

En: No.
Zh: “不是。”

En: Hah! Couldn't handle us. A cause gives the workers strength. Gives them power. He bellows at the gates: "We have -- A RIGHT TO WORK!"
Zh: “哈！对付不了我们了吧。使命给了工人力量。给了他们能量。”他对着大门大喊：“我们拥有——工作的权利！”

En: Might be time. Don't let the fat bastards tread on you. Cops tend to side with the higher-ups, but you're essentially still *workers*.
Zh: “慢慢考虑。别被那个死胖子骗了。警察一般都倾向于站在权贵那边，但你们终归还是*劳动者*。”

En: This is an official matter, not to be discussed with outsiders.
Zh: “这是公事，不能向外人透露。”

En: I don't know...
Zh: “我不知道……”

En: This really isn't my area of expertise.
Zh: “这个真的不属于我的专业范围。”

En: He nods. "You go, take the bastards down a peg or two. Squeeze them a little. Show them the rule of law." He bellows at the gates: "LET US WORK!"
Zh: 他点点头。“你去吧，放倒一两个混账，给他们点颜色看看，让他们瞧瞧什么叫法治。”他朝着大门大喊：“让我们工作！”

En: I know nothing about a murder. His reply is snappy and terse.
Zh: “我对凶杀案一无所知。”他的回复简明扼要。

En: Wouldn't put it past these harbour bugs. They'd do *anything* to stay alive.
Zh: “我相信港口那些臭虫绝对做得出这种事。为了活下去，他们*什么*都愿意做。”

En: What's it to you? Deep-set suspicion drips from every syllable.
Zh: “关你什么事？”他吐出的每一个音节都表露出深深的怀疑。

En: Curiosity. Going to figure out this strike mess.
Zh: “出于好奇，我想搞清楚罢工这档子破事到底是怎么搞的。”

En: Routine. Part of the investigation.
Zh: “这属于刑侦调查的例行程序。”

En: Am I a suspect? His eyes narrow. "Done no crimes. I only fight for the rights of people."
Zh: “我有嫌疑吗？”他眯起眼睛。“我可从没犯过法。我只是在为民众的权利抗争。”

En: Big mess. Caused by Union greed. He shoots you a wary, distrustful look. "But I only fight for the rights of people."
Zh: “别提了，简直一团乱，都怪工会贪心不足。”他谨慎而猜疑地瞥了你一眼，“但我只是在为民众的权利抗争。”

En: Already got that. I'm interested in *your* background.
Zh: “这我已经知道了。我对*你的*背景更感兴趣。”

En: Uh huh. Was a long time ago. The man is quickly losing interest in talking with you.
Zh: “嗯哼。很久之前的事了。”男人很快就失去了跟你说话的兴趣。”

En: Hey *tough guy*, I had more questions.
Zh: “喂，*硬汉*，我还有些问题。”

En: I thought you looked real familiar for a second there.
Zh: “有那么一会儿，我觉得你看起来特别眼熟。”

En: No, don't think we've met before. His eyes narrow with mistrust. "I came to help out *the people*."
Zh: “你看错了，我不觉得我们之前见过。”他眯起眼睛，充满了不信任，“我来是为了帮助*民众*的。”

En: Sweet, I know bars. Been thrown out from several. Maybe that's why you seem familiar.
Zh: “太好了，酒吧我可太了解了，还被好几家撵出来过。难怪你才看起来那么眼熟。”

En: I wasn't done asking questions.
Zh: “我的问题还没问完呢。”

En: He looks annoyed. "Things are busy enough. You going to waste less of my time?"
Zh: 他看起来有些不耐烦。“事情已经够多的了。你是来浪费我本来就不充裕的时间吗？”

En: Maybe.
Zh: “也许。”

En: We'll see.
Zh: “看情况吧。”

En: The hulking man oozes stark scepticism.
Zh: 这个魁梧的男人毫不掩饰自己的猜疑。

En: As he does, he stomps his foot on the pavement and... stop everything! Look at that sweet pair of boots on his feet! They would absolutely complement your *soul*, would let your style fly even higher!
Zh: 他的脚踩在人行道是的时候……时间停止了！看看他脚上那双漂亮的靴子！这双靴子和你的*灵魂*有着极高的匹配度，能让你的时尚感飞达全新的高度！

En: Imagine what you could do with boots like this. Not be a little fuckin' weakling baby, for starters. There are boots for mewling and there are boots for smashing shit.
Zh: 想象一下有了这双靴子之后你可以做些什么。首先，千万不要跟个菜鸟似的喜极而泣。这个世界上有些靴子是给爱哭包穿的，还有些是给超级硬汉穿的。

En: If only I had boots like this, I could stomp many things. Got a problem, punk? Eat my boot.
Zh: 如果有这双靴子，我想踹什么都行。有意见吗，废物？吃本大爷一脚。

En: Get your daily infusion of vitamin PB -- for Police Brutality. Makes you feel like a boss. The man in charge.
Zh: 每天补充一些维他命PB——警察暴力的缩写。服用后你会觉得自己就是老大，是那个掌控全局的人。

En: It's just a pair of boots. Who cares?
Zh: 不就是双靴子嘛，谁在乎啊？

En: Never thought you so spineless. Where's your wrecking ball spirit, your brutal mojo?
Zh: 从没想过你居然这么没骨气。你的暴烈的血性呢，你的凶残的咒语呢？

En: Those are some sweet boots. Can I have them?
Zh: “这双靴子不错啊。能给我吗？”

En: I don't really care what either of you think. I'm a cool-ass bitch, baby. You can't *fake* stardom.
Zh: 我真的不在乎你俩到底在想什么。大爷我可是个狂拽酷炫叼炸天的主啊，宝贝。明星气质可是*假装*不出来的。

En: Fuckin' a, that's a solid slice of self-respect.
Zh: 去他妈的吧，那可是我牢不可破的自尊。

En: I stand corrected.
Zh: 我认错。

En: I don't think the boots really fit *my style*. They look militant and uncomfortable.
Zh: 我不觉得这双靴子真的适合*我的风格*。看上去戾气太重，而且穿起来未必舒服。

En: Geez, live a little. At least give things a try. It's just boots. A set of sweet, stylish boots.
Zh: 我的天，活出点自我行不行，遇事至少试一下啊。这只是双靴子而已，一双又美又潮的靴子。

En: Damn, you're right. Super sweet. Where can I get in on this bad-ass boot fashion?
Zh: 见鬼，你是对的。太美妙了。这么叼炸天的时尚潮靴我上哪儿找去啊？

En: Ask the man. Ask around. The doors of good taste are open to you. Be in vogue.
Zh: 跟那个男的打听打听，跟周围的人打听打听。好品味的大门向你敞开。时髦起来吧。

En: Establishment hasn't had its fill, is that right? First they obstruct our work, then they come for our shoes.
Zh: “体制内的人永远都不懂得满足，是吧？一开始他们阻止我们上工，然后又冲着我们的鞋来了。”

En: No.
Zh: “不行。”

En: Come on, why not?
Zh: “拜托，为什么不行？”

En: I mean, where did you get them? I would like a pair of my own.
Zh: “我是说，你从哪儿搞来的这么一双鞋？我自己也想搞一双。”

En: Oh, okay, whatever. Too much of a hassle.
Zh: “哦，好吧，无所谓了。太麻烦了。”

En: Around. He shifts his gaze away. "Eminent Domain. Previous... workplace."
Zh: “就这附近啊。”他移开视线。“艾米涅特区。我之前……工作的地方。”

En: I'm hardly the establishment.
Zh: “我都不能算是体制内的人。”

En: Okay okay, I got it. None of this *rob the poor* stuff.
Zh: “好吧，好吧，我明白了。不搞*抢劫穷人*的戏码了。”

En: That's what they always say, before stabbing you in the back. You want boots? Go find your own.
Zh: “他们在背后捅人刀子之前都会这么说。你想要靴子？自己找去。”

En: Well shit. I'll have to check around then. Maybe there's a boot store around.
Zh: “好吧妈的，那我自己去找。说不定这附近有鞋店呢。”

En: You do that.
Zh: “你请吧。”

En: Absolutely nothing?
Zh: “完全不知道？”

En: Why so tense?
Zh: “干嘛这么紧张？”

En: Excuse me, I had further inquiries.
Zh: “不好意思，我还有问题要问。”

En: The man has turned his attention elsewhere, he shakes his fist slowly -- while observing you from the corner of his eye.
Zh: 男人心不在焉地缓慢挥舞着自己的拳头——同时用眼角的余光观察着你。

En: I don't really drink. But work *has* taken me to some. Which one was it?
Zh: “我其实不喝酒，不过工作有时候*避不开*。是哪一个？”

En: And you are unable to breach the entrance?
Zh: “你们就不能强行闯进去吗？”

En: Wait -- *Head-Measurer*?
Zh: “等等——*颅骨测量师*？”

En: Have you considered storming in? Like, all of you?
Zh: “你就没考虑过硬闯？我的意思是，你们所有人一起，一窝蜂冲进去？”

En: Yeah, the giant on the bridge that kicked your ass -- Head-Measurer. Won't let the workers by. The access panel is right behind him.
Zh: “是啊，就是刚才在桥上把你揍出屎来的那个大块头——人称颅骨测量师。他不让工人过去。大门控制面板就在他身后。”

En: Yeah, that giant up there on the bridge you talked to -- Head-Measurer. Won't let the workers by. The access panel is right behind him.
Zh: “是啊，就是刚才在桥上跟你说话的那个大块头——人称颅骨测量师。他不放工人过去，大门控制面板就在他身后。”

En: Huge Semenese guy standing up there on the overhead passage. Won't let anyone by. The access panel is right behind him.
Zh: “上面的通道站着一个萨马拉大块头。他不会放任何人过去的，大门控制面板就在他身后。”

En: Why don't *you* go arrest them instead? I'm sure they've done plenty of criminal shit, they have *that look*.
Zh: “那*你*为什么不上去逮捕他们呢？我敢肯定他们都犯过不少事儿，他们看起来就是犯过法的*样子*。”

En: I need you to be my champion against Jean-Luc up there.
Zh: “我需要你做我的守护者，对付上面那个让·卢克。”

En: No. He slowly shakes his head. "I'm not a fighter -- I'm a WORKER!"
Zh: “不行，”他慢慢地摇了摇头，“我不会打架——我就是个工人！”

En: You're not from The Domain.
Zh: “你不是艾米涅特区来的。”

En: Yeah? The man grunts. "What makes you say that?"
Zh: “哦？”男人咕哝着。“你这么说有什么依据吗？”

En: I don't know what makes me say that.
Zh: “我也不知道自己为什么要这么说。”

En: It's a... gut feeling.
Zh: “这是一种……内在的直觉。”

En: The beefy man snorts and spits on the ground. "Yeah. I don't know either." He turns his attention back to the harbour gates.
Zh: 壮实大汉吸了吸鼻子，然后朝地上吐了口痰。“是啊，我也不知道。”他把注意力又转回到港口大门上。

En: Your gut's got too much alcohol in it. Getting delirious. The bulky man guffaws and turns away from you, focusing again on the harbour gates.
Zh: “你的内在掺了太多酒精在里头，都开始说胡话了。”大块头大笑着转过头，再次把注意力集中到港口大门上。

En: Shudder and breathe on your hands.
Zh: 颤抖着对着双手哈气。

En: Don't. No need. Focus on something else.
Zh: 不用。没必要。把注意力集中到其他事情上吧。

En: Ignore the discrepancy.
Zh: 忽略这里的不协调感。

En: Ill-fitting... what does that mean?
Zh: 不合身……这意味着什么？

En: His shirt is far too small and an unpleasantly tight fit. While the overalls, held up by a belt, seem to fit a man with much *more* corpulence.
Zh: 他的衬衫太小，紧绷得让人觉得很不舒服。工装裤虽然系了条皮带，看起来却适合一个比他*还要*肥胖的人。

En: Fine. It was probably nothing.
Zh: 好吧。可能没什么。

En: Life in the Domain is even worse off than in Martinaise. The cold air is stiff from the fumes of motor carriages and lorries roaring overhead.
Zh: 艾米涅特的生活比马丁内斯更惨。机车和货车从头顶呼啸而过，排出的废气让寒冷的空气变得更加粘稠。

En: Sweet boots? I require details.
Zh: 美妙的靴子？我想要探寻更多细节。

En: Don't care. I already have boots of my own, thank you.
Zh: 不在乎。我已经有自己的靴子了，谢谢。

En: Prissy milksop. Get back to me when you've grown a backbone!
Zh: 瞻前顾后的胆小鬼。等你有骨气的时候再回来找我吧！

En: The burly man has a set of not-very-sweet-nor-impressive boots protruding out from under the baggy, hanging folds. They are of little interest to you.
Zh: 那个壮实的男人脚上那双*不那么好看也不吸引人*的靴子，从松垮垂坠的裤子褶皱下伸了出来。你对这双靴子一点兴趣也没有。

En: Don't mean the scrawny mesque punk. He points at Mañana, who smiles and waves at you. "I mean Head-Measurer -- or whatever he is."
Zh: “我说的可不是个骨瘦如柴的小阿飞梅斯克。”他指向马列拉，对方微笑着朝你招手。“我说的是颅骨测量师——好像是这个名字。”

En: And I don't mean the scrawny Mesque punk either. He points at the dockworker idling on the staircase. "I mean Head-Measurer -- or whatever he is."
Zh: “我说的可不是个骨瘦如柴的小阿飞梅斯克。”他指向那个靠在楼梯上无所事事的码头工人。“我说的是颅骨测量师——好像是这个名字。”

En: Main gate's locked -- would take *heavy ordnance* to bust it open. Could try to get in through the secretary's office. He points up the stairs. "Door's locked. The guard's blocking the way to the access panel."
Zh: “大门已经被关闭了——这种门怕是只有*重型火力*才砸得开。”他指着楼上。“门禁系统已经被封锁了，守卫挡住了前往大门控制面板的路。”

En: How bad could one guy be? You seem capable.
Zh: “区区一个人而已，有什么可怕的？以你的个头还过不去？”

En: Bad. The man glares at you. "Standing on a narrow bridge, he's got a strategically advantageous position. And he's trained."
Zh: “很可怕。”男人瞪着你。“就这么往窄桥上一站，他就占据了战略上的有利位置，更何况他还受过专业训练。”

En: You here to fuck with us? Beat the honest worker down?
Zh: “你他妈也是来这里跟我们作对的吗？来欺负我们这些老实的工人？”

En: I don't trust cops, but... I can see you understand the -- He raises his voice and chants: "RIGHT TO WORK! RIGHT TO WORK!"
Zh: “我一般不相信警察，但是……我知道你明白——”他抬高声音，开始喊口号：“工作权！工作权！”

En: Sure, why not?
Zh: “好呀，为什么不掺一脚呢？”

En: Why don't you just *talk* to them?
Zh: “你为什么不跟他们*谈谈*呢？”

En: Like civilized folk, you mean? The man rubs his chin. "These native fucks don't understand *civilized*."
Zh: “你是说，像文明人一样？”男人摩挲着下巴。“这些土著杂碎根本不懂*文明*。”

En: Okay, I wanted to discuss something else.
Zh: “好吧，我想讨论点别的。”

En: The physically impressive man-tower has turned his attention back to the gates. He ignores your presence.
Zh: 这个身材惊人的人形高塔把注意力转回到了大门上。他无视了你。

En: Uh. I'll be going now. [Leave.]
Zh: “呃，我走了。”[离开。]

En: Besides, we're not that different. He leans closer. "It helps if people see us talking -- cops and strikebreakers together. Shows authorities are on our side. Builds confidence."
Zh: “另外，咱俩也没那么不同。”他靠近了一些。“让大家看到我们——警察和反罢工者——在对话是有好处的，这说明当局是站在我们这边的，有助于帮他们建立起信心。”

En: RIGHT TO WORK!! the man chants, then turns to you. "Back again, eh? Here to show the militia's support for our cause?"
Zh: “工作权！！！”男人喊着口号着，然后转向你。“又回来了？代表你们当局支持我们的诉求来了？”

En: RIGHT TO WORK! He seems to have returned to his previous routine, as if he'd never seen the photo of the tattoo.
Zh: “工作权！！！”他又恢复到了之前的状态，就好似他从未见过那张纹身的相片。

En: Went native on the chief, huh? He nods in approval. "Those ballerina antics were reckless. Should have just punched him in the throat again."
Zh: “以其人之道还治其人之身？”他点头表示赞许。“那些杂耍似的舞蹈动作冒险了点，你应该直接朝他的喉咙来一拳的。”

En: Nice aggression with the native. He nods in approval. "Don't let the chief fool you next time. You don't need to be that up in his face -- leave yourself space to move."
Zh: “跟土著过的那两招不错啊。”他点头赞同。“下次别被那个带头的给耍了，你不用贴他那么近——要给自己留足活动的空间。”

En: Regardless, I have some questions for you. (Proceed.)
Zh: “这些都无所谓，我有些问题要问你。”（继续。）

En: Maybe you should ask *them* the questions, like why we're not allowed to make a living here? He bellows to the gates: "SHAME ON YOU!"
Zh: “你应该去问*他们*问题，比如说凭什么不让我们上班赚钱？”他朝着大门大喊：“无耻！”

En: He pauses, then abruptly changes the subject: "Let's talk about our right to work."
Zh: 他停了一下，然后很突然地转变了话题：“我们聊聊工作权吧。”

En: He abruptly changes the subject: "Let's talk about our right to work."
Zh: 他突然转变了话题：“我们聊聊工作权吧。”

En: I actually need to speak with the Union boss.
Zh: “我真的需要跟工会老大谈谈。”

En: I want to get into the harbour too.
Zh: “我也想进港口。”

En: What are you talking about? I'm not tense.
Zh: “你在说什么呢？我才没有紧张。”

En: Yes, he's tense.
Zh: 不，他很紧张。

En: And dangerous. Somewhere below it all...
Zh: 还很危险。在那下面的某个地方……

En: He nods. "You go take the bastards down a peg or two. Squeeze them a little. Show them the rule of law." He bellows at the gates: "LET US WORK!"
Zh: 他点点头。“你去吧，放倒一两个混账，稍微给他们点颜色看看，让他们瞧瞧什么叫法治。”他朝着大门大喊：“让我们工作！”

En: We're not picking a side in this just yet, sir.
Zh: “在这件事情上我们并不打算站队，先生。”

En: Why not? I see *numerous* reasons not to pick a side in this local matter.
Zh: “为什么不掺一脚？我能说出*好些*不要掺和本地事务的理由。”

En: Pity. He turns around and bellows at the gates: "LET US WORK!"
Zh: “可惜。”他转过身，朝着大门大喊：“让我们工作！”

En: When the man moves around, you perceive some *serious abs* underneath his tight-fitting shirt. This man is in *shape*.
Zh: 男人四下走动时，你感觉到他的紧绷着的衬衫下面藏着一些*货真价实的腹肌*。这个男人的*身材*保持得很好。

En: You wearing new clothes?
Zh: “你穿的新衣服吗？”

En: He ignores your question, choosing instead to turn to the emaciated workers -- raising both fists in the air. The clothes are obviously not his.
Zh: 他无视了你的问题，转而朝向那些瘦弱的工人——高举着双拳。这衣服显然不是他的。

En: Silence is the answer. There's something off here but he won't say what.
Zh: 沉默就是他的答复。这里有哪儿不太对劲，但他什么也不会说。

En: We were promised work. He points to the gates. "We'd be in there -- WORKING -- if the bastards hadn't shut the gates."
Zh: “我们都是有工作的人。”他指向大门。“我们现在本该在里面工作的，如果那些混账没有把门关上的话。”

En: I don't know how the Union has a trained killer up there, but that one's no joke. And my men are tired. And hungry. They're WORKERS, not fighters.
Zh: “我不知道工会从哪儿找来了这么个训练有素的杀手，但那个人的手段可真不是在开玩笑。况且我的人现在又累又饿，他们是工人，不是战士。”

En: So what are you going to do now?
Zh: “所以你现在打算怎么办？”

En: No. They will give up eventually. Or get drunk. Leave the button unguarded. Then we charge. The man rubs his jaw -- a perfect, lightly bearded square wedge.
Zh: “不。他们最终一定会放弃罢工的，要不然就喝个烂醉如泥，到时候大门开关就会无人看守，我们就趁机冲进去。”男人摩挲着下巴上那一丛完美方楔形的小胡子。

En: Odd out of place colonial reference there, Mr. Scab Leader...
Zh: 古怪又不合时宜的殖民言论引用，工贼领袖先生……

En: I work hard. His voice booms ever louder: "And I would work *even harder* -- if the fuckers would LET US WORK!"
Zh: “我卖力工作。”他的声音越变越大：“而且我会*更加卖力地工作*——只要那些混账放我们进去工作！”

En: How come you're built like a brick shit-house, but the other scabs are so scrawny?
Zh: “为什么就你的身板活像堵砖房，但其他工贼都瘦的跟小鸡仔似的呢？

En: I don't want to ask that.
Zh: 我不想问这个。

En: Yes. Of course.
Zh: 是的。当然。

En: I have a question. The lieutenant looks him in the eye. "Why do all these men follow your leadership?"
Zh: “我有个问题。”警督看着他的眼睛。“为什么这些人都听你的指挥？”

En: You think they follow because I'm big and loud? No, they follow the rules of the market. The rules of the economy. Because they were -- he starts bellowing, "-- GIVEN A JOB TO DO."
Zh: “你觉得因为我个头大，声音响，所以他们才听我的？不，他们听从的是市场规律，经济规律。因为他们——”他开始大喊：“——都是要上班的人。”

En: You've been talking to him for quite a while now -- something is off with this guy. Ask him where he's from.
Zh: 现在你已经跟他聊了好一阵子——这人有点不大对劲。问问他是从哪里来的。

En: Makes sense, I guess. Never mind, not gonna pry.
Zh: “有道理。算了，我不深究了。”

En: Below -- broken-down, battered people mill on the dusty streets with no purpose. Yet amongst them there is no sight of this man. *Nowhere*.
Zh: 在下方——饱受苦难的人们漫无目的地在尘土飞扬的街道上游荡，然而他们之中却没有这个男人的身影。*任何地方*都寻不见他的身影。

En: So anyway, cop. That's where I'm from. It would be *stupid* to doubt that.
Zh: “就是这样，我就是从哪里来的。你还怀疑下去的话就太*愚蠢*了。”

En: Pretty okay, as far as lies go.
Zh: 就谎言来说的话，还挺像那么回事的。

En: He squints at you --  a little spark of violence gutters in his eyes. Then he blinks and turns to his men. "We're done here. I have a strike to break."
Zh: 他斜视着你——眼睛里闪过一丝暴虐的火花。然后他眨眨眼，转向自己的手下。“我们谈完了。我还要继续反对罢工呢。”

En: He's stopping this to control himself. You were on to something.
Zh: 他停下话头是为了自控。你戳中要害了。

En: No one there knows you.
Zh: “那里没人认识你。”

En: The beefy man snorts and spits on the ground. "They know me -- that's bullshit." He turns his attention back to the harbour gates.
Zh: 壮实大汉吸了吸鼻子，然后朝地上吐了口痰。“他们怎么不认得我——你在放屁。”他把注意力又转回到港口大门上。

En: Are you a mercenary hired by Wild Pines?
Zh: “你是野松公司雇来的佣兵吗？”

En: FREE FLOW OF COMMERCE! The man yells and wipes his face. Sweat is dripping down his brow.
Zh: “商业需要自由流通！”男人一边喊，一边擦拭着脸庞。汗水从他的眉头滴落。

En: Hell no. I'm just an honest scab. I won't have talk like that around here, you understand?
Zh: “怎么可能，我只不过是个老实的工贼。我不想在这里说这些话，你明白吗？”

En: 'Hell no, I'm just an honest scab?' That didn't sound too convincing.
Zh: “‘怎么可能，我只不过是个老实的工贼？’听起来不太有说服力呢。”

En: His neck muscles tense up, the veins on his neck bulge. Be careful -- he's holding it *deep* in but there are things in there you don't want to meet.
Zh: 他的颈部肌肉顿时绷紧了，上面的血管都凸了出来。当心——他隐藏的*很深*，里面有些东西你还是别见识得好。

En: It just has to be said -- that was not a convincing line.
Zh: 不得不说——那可不是能让人信服的说法。

En: Don't talk to him any longer. Just leave, please.
Zh: 别再跟他说话了。快走吧，求你了。

En: Okay. (Back off.)
Zh: “好吧。”（退后。）

En: Say very quietly: "Is there a *tribunal* being convened by any chance?"
Zh: 非常小声地说：“你们是不是准备抓了人然后立刻审判？”

En: Fucking loincloth... He stares you down mutely for a second.
Zh: “该死的野人……”他无声地盯了你一会儿。

En: The man's breathing steadies but his eyes are still narrow. Slowly he's trying to get his 'Right to Work' dance back on...
Zh: 男人的呼吸很沉稳，但眼睛仍然眯着。他正慢慢试着找回‘工作权’的表演状态……

En: Loincloth? Now, you see, that's not really what a scab would say is it? Better not to press the issue further, sire. He seems... tense.
Zh: 野人？您看呐，一个真正的工贼哪里会说这样的词？最好还是莫要再追下去了，陛下。他好像……很紧张。

En: Fucking bug... He breathes out slowly -- his giant chest deflating and his mouth slightly open.
Zh: “该死的蛆……”他缓缓呼出一口气——壮硕的胸口随之缩紧，嘴巴微微张开。

En: You hear a tiny rattle, a small radio, cracking somewhere -- an earpiece?
Zh: 你听到一阵微弱的噪音，一台小无线电在某个地方发出啪嗒的声响——是耳麦吗？

En: How about you fuck off now, huh?
Zh: “不如你现在赶紧给我滚，怎么样？”

En: A punch is not imminent, but it's being cocked. Hard.
Zh: 一记重拳不会立刻砸过来，但是正在蓄力。蓄力条快满了。

En: Okay. Of course. The lieutenant says, his voice a soothing calm. He looks at you.
Zh: “好的，没问题。”警督说到，他的声音平静而让人宽慰。他看向你。

En: There could be weapons aiming at us right now -- somewhere above, in the buildings. The other merc. Don't push this -- he's thinking -- this is not the time.
Zh: 现在可能就有武器在暗处瞄准了我们——就在这栋建筑里，上方的某个地方。另一名佣兵。别逼太紧——他还在思考——还不是时候。

En: It's hard to do that, when you want to beat a man into a pulp instead.
Zh: 现在可能就有武器瞄准了我们——就在这栋建筑里，上面的某个地方。其他的雇佣兵。别逼太紧——他想到——时间还没到。

En: I think you may be able to help me decipher some tattoos. (Take out the picture.)
Zh: “我觉得也许你可以帮我破译某个纹身。”（取出相片。）

En: Banaital is one of the inhabited islands of the Semenese archipelago, near the pale. Covered in  jungle. It was the anchor point for the Semenese nationalists -- in the proxy war held on the islanders' territory.
Zh: 巴奈塔属于塞美尼群岛中一个有人居住的岛屿，就在灰域附近，岛上遍布着茂密的丛林。对于塞美尼民族主义者来说，巴奈塔是一个圣地——代理人战争就是在这里爆发的。

En: Our colonel is *deep* in the bush here. Deep in the fucking bush, in Banaital, '41... Monsoon season. He's on a reconnaissance mission.
Zh: “我们的上校就潜伏在这里的密林*深处*。该死的丛林，就在巴奈塔，41年……雨季。他当时正在执行一次侦察任务。”

En: Suddenly the laughter stops. "Drowned in a creek a week later. Spirits my ass."
Zh: 笑声戛然而止。“一个星期后，他就淹死在一条小溪里。狗屁鬼神。”

En: He's spent a month behind enemy lines scouting kipt villages. Nothing but fucking bugs and snakes for fun. Men are getting restless... there's talk of switching *employers*... He licks his lips, as if drunk suddenly.
Zh: “他深入敌后，花了一个月的时间搜寻吉皮特的村庄，然而除了杀杀臭虫和蛇之外一无所获。所有人都开始变得焦躁不安……还有更换*雇主*的说法……”他舔舔嘴唇，像是突然喝醉了。

En: Don't interrupt him. (Hear it.)
Zh: 不要打断他。（用心听。）

En: With some strange emotion. This is about to get really graphic. Last moment to back off.
Zh: 某种奇怪的情绪起来了，马上就要有好戏看了，想退出的话就现在了。

En: Our boy -- he's only a captain then -- but he knows how these men think. If they don't see action soon... His voice gets strangely quiet. A long, long way from *right to work*. His gaze pierces the paper.
Zh: “我们的主人公——那时候还只是个上尉——但他知道这些人在想什么。如果他们继续这么无所事事下去……”他的声音变得异常安静，这种状态完全迥异于*工作权*。他凌厉的目光能穿透纸背。

En: There's a word on the tip of your tongue. Colourless, odourless. It's...
Zh: 一个词语停在你的舌尖，无色无味。那就是……

En: What's the matter, militiaman... no stomach for it? He looks at the photo in your hand and smiles. "This man was worth a thousand loincloths. We're done talking here."
Zh: “怎么了，民兵……倒胃了？”他看着你手中的相片，笑了。“这个男人能抵得上一千个野人。话已经谈完了。”

En: Actually, no. (Take the photo back.) "You knew him right?"
Zh: “其实呢，不想。”（将相片收起）。“你认识他，对吗？”

En: The story must have stirred up some conflicting emotions. The man looks tired for a second...
Zh: 这个故事肯定激起了一些矛盾的情绪。有那么一瞬间，这个男人看起来很疲惫……

En: She was quite the entertainment. For the week she lasted. Expired in the hands of Sarge Mason -- the kinda guy who'd make Chief there, he nods towards the man on the gates, "shit his pants and cry like a bitch."
Zh: “她是个挺不错的消遣。她坚持了一个星期，最后死在了梅森军士的手里——他那种人要是现在在这里的话，”他朝着大门顶上的男人点了点头，“那位酋长只有被吓得屁滚尿流的份。”

En: God... He suddenly bursts out laughing. "Mason couldn't let go. Cut the tits off her cold body and fuckin' ate them. Said primitive spirits were watching over him now..."
Zh: “天哪……”他突然大笑出声。“梅森还不肯罢休，他从她冰冷的尸体上切下了乳头，然后他妈的吃进了肚子里，还说什么从现在起有远古神明保佑他了……”

En: Don't think so, he grunts, barely glancing at it.
Zh: “我帮不上忙。”他咕哝着，几乎没瞟一眼。

En: Suddenly this feels like a really, *really* bad idea.
Zh: 突然间感觉这像是个非常、*非常*糟糕的主意。

En: Of course. Yes. Forget about it. Let's discuss something else. (Cancel task.)
Zh: “好吧，算了，当我没说。咱们聊点别的。”（取消任务。）

En: Another grunt. He's turned his attention to the gates and seems tense.
Zh: 又是咕哝一声。他把注意力转移到大门那边，好像有点紧张。

En: Actually... The lieutenant turns to you. "I think we should maybe even *get going* now?"
Zh: “其实……”警督转向你。“我想也许我们现在应该*出发*了？”

En: He clearly does not think poking the hornet's nest is a wise plan anymore.
Zh: 他显然觉得捅马蜂窝并不明智。

En: Silence. He takes the photo. Grey eyes dart  back and forth on the glossy surface -- his face is unmoving.
Zh: 沉默。他拿起相片，灰色的眼睛在相片光滑的表面来回扫过——脸上的肌肉却纹丝不动。

En: After a while, he cranes his head side-to-side and sighs: "Fucking loincloths really did him in."
Zh: 过了一会儿，他左右伸了伸脖子，叹了口气：“该死的野人，真的把他弄死了。”

En: Wordless, he takes the photo and looks at it. Grey eyes dart back and forth on the glossy surface -- his face is unmoving.
Zh: 他沉默不语地拿起相片端详着，灰色的眼睛在相片光滑的表面来回扫过——脸上的肌肉却纹丝不动。

En: Hard as a stone. But beneath it...
Zh: 硬如磐石。但是其下……

En: You see dead flesh -- in colourful rivers of Polychrome -- melting skin and hair.
Zh: 你看见死去的肉体——浸在五彩缤纷的河水里——模糊的皮肤和头发。

En: Can you tell me what the tattoo means?
Zh: “你能告诉我这个纹身是什么意思吗？”

En: What it *means*.... He points to one tiny star in the web of lines, away from the man's heart, almost on his stomach.
Zh: “这个纹身的*意思*……”他指着网格中的一颗小星星，位置远离男人的心脏，几乎是在腹部。

En: Yeah.
Zh: “是啊。”

En: Uhm... loin-cloths?
He's clenching his teeth. There is silent pressure behind them.
Zh: 他咬紧牙关，背后是沉默的压力。

En: In what must have been Semenine.
Zh: 这一定是塞美尼。

En: I can tell you what this one means. Only one. He squints at it. It's so small. "You wanna hear what happened here?"
Zh: “我可以告诉你这个图案的意思。就一个。”他眯眼看着。那个图案实在是太小了。“你想知道发生了什么吗？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Actually, no. (Take the photo back.)
Zh: “并不想。”（把相片收回来）。

En: At dawn he comes upon two kipts, breeding in the bushes by the river. Or maybe they weren't breeding -- maybe they were just making eyes at each other. I like to think they were breeding...
Zh: “天亮时分他偶遇了两个吉皮特，那两个吉皮特在河边的草丛里打野战，他们也有可能不是在打野战——只是在眉目传情。我宁可相信他们是在打野战……”

En: Okay. This isn't useful. (Take the photo back.) "You knew him right?"
Zh: “好吧。这个没什么用。”（把相片收回来。）“你认识他，对吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Good choice. He nods.
Zh: “明智的选择。”他点点头。

En: He clearly does not think poking the hornet's nest is a wise plan anymore.
Zh: 他显然觉得捅马蜂窝并不明智。

En: The lieutenant turns to you. "I think we should maybe even *get going* now?"
Zh: 警督转向你。“我想也许我们现在应该*出发*了？”

En: There is absolutely no need to push this any further.
Zh: 绝对没必要再继续深入这个话题了。

En: Evil.
Zh: “邪恶。”

En: What *is* evil?
Zh: “邪恶*是*什么？”

En: You can have that. (Conclude.)
Zh: “你可以留着它。”（总结。）

En: Nah, you've earned it. He turns toward the gate, slowly, and yells: "All right now! FREE COMMERCE! KEEP THE GOODS FLOWING!"
Zh: “不，这是你应得的。”他慢慢转向大门，然后大喊：“好了！自由贸易！保持货物流通！”

En: You bet it was.
Zh: “没错。”

En: It's just... nature. This guy, he points at the picture, "he used to say evil is when nature and spirit meet in the wrong place."
Zh: “邪恶不过是……天性。这个家伙，”他指着相片，“他曾经说过，邪恶就是天性和灵魂在错误地方相遇的结果。”

En: You're not really a *scab leader*, are you?
Zh: “你不是真正的*工贼领袖*，对吧？”

En: Fucking mask is getting sweaty. I want take my mask off, but... He shrugs.
Zh: “总戴着该死的面具太累人了。我也想摘下自己的面具，但是……”他耸耸肩。

En: Can I have it back? (Conclude.)
Zh: “能还给我吗？”（总结。）

En: Go ahead. He turns toward the gate, slowly, and yells: "All right now. FREE COMMERCE! KEEP THE GOODS FLOWING!"
Zh: “请便。”他慢慢转向大门，然后大喊：“好了！自由贸易！保持货物流通！”

En: On the photo in your hands -- the dead man's skin is studded with stars. Tens, hundreds of them, littering his dead skin.
Zh: 你手中的相片上——那个死去男人的皮肤上点缀着好多星星。几十颗，几百颗，布满他死气沉沉的皮肤。

En: You were there?
Zh: “你当时也在那里？”

En: No. I was somewhere else. That's just a *guess*. As to what this one little star on his belly might mean. I'm just a bouncer.
Zh: “我当时在别的地方。至于他肚子上的那颗小星星的具体含义，那只是我的*猜测*。我就是个保镖。”

En: We shot the boy, he was useless, but the girl... she was nice. A little fat you know. But not too old.
Zh: “我们直接射杀了那个男孩，他毫无用处，但是那个女孩……她挺不错的，你懂的，就是有点胖，不过还算年轻。”

En: No -- I was in the Domain. In Jamrock. Being a bouncer.
Zh: “不——我当时在艾米涅特，加姆洛克，当保镖。”

En: He's under orders. And orders -- are orders.
Zh: 他不过是在奉命行事。命令——就是命令。

En: I'm going to interpret that as a *yes*. There's a tribunal. And it won't be long until it's ready.
Zh: 看来答案是*是*，会有一场审判。而且用不了多久就会准备完毕。

En: They were on the body of the man who was hanged behind the cafeteria there. (Give him the photo.)
Zh: “这些是餐厅后院吊着的那个人身上的纹身。”（把相片递给他。）

En: It was on your colonel. (Give him the photo.)
Zh: “这是你们上校身上的纹身。”（把相片递给他。）

En: We're investigating a murder. These tattoos were on the victim's body. (Give him the photo.)
Zh: “我们正在调查一起凶杀案，这些是被害人的身体上的纹身。”（把相片递给他。）

En: Don't think so, he grunts.
Zh: “我不这么觉得。”他咕哝道。

En: Workers rights! WORKERS RIGHTS!
Zh: “工人权利！工人权利！”

En: LET US WORK!
Zh: “让我们工作！”

En: Look alive back there! LET US WORK!!!
Zh: “后面的人大声点！我们要工作！！！”

En: RIGHT TO WORK! RIGHT TO WORK!
Zh: “工作权！工作权！”

En: WE'RE NOT GOING ANYWHERE!
Zh: “我们哪里也不去！”

En: LET US THROUGH, LOINCLOTHS!
Zh: “让我们过去，野人！”

ConvTitle: GATES / WOMENS RIGHTS first phase barks
En: Rights for women!
Zh: “妇女权利！”

ConvTitle: GATES / WOMENS RIGHTS first phase barks
En: Don't fucking tell us to smile!
Zh: “我们什么时候该笑轮不到你他妈的来告诉我们！”

ConvTitle: GATES / WOMENS RIGHTS first phase barks
En: Equal wages for equal labour!
Zh: “同工同酬！”

ConvTitle: GATES / WOMENS RIGHTS first phase barks
En: Is this the correct protest?
Zh: “是这个抗议没错吧？”

ConvTitle: GATES / WOMENS RIGHTS first phase barks
En: Stop domestic abuse! Prosecute the abusers!
Zh: “终止家庭暴力！起诉家暴男！”

ConvTitle: GATES / WOMENS RIGHTS first phase barks
En: Uh...

ConvTitle: GATES / WOMENS RIGHTS first phase barks
En: Hey...
